Name: CATEGORICAL GRANT:  NONPOINT SOURCE (SEC. 319)
Title: 01 - CATEGORICAL GRANT:  NONPOINT SOURCE (SEC. 319)
Laws: CLEAN WATER ACT SECTIONS 314 AND 319
Narrative: GRANTS UNDER SECTION 319 ARE PROVIDED TO STATES, TERRITORIES, AND TRIBES TO HELP THEM IMPLEMENT THEIR EPA APPROVED NONPOINT SOURCE MANAGEMENT PROGRAMS BY REMEDIATING PAST NONPOINT SOURCE POLLUTION AND PREVENTING OR MINIMIZING NEW NONPOINT SOURCE POLLUTION. APPROVED ELEMENTS OF STATE NPS PROGRAMS ELIGIBLE FOR SUCH FUNDING INCLUDE:  STATE AND LOCAL NON-REGULATORY AND/OR REGULATORY ABATEMENT PROGRAMS; STATE AND LOCAL ENFORCEMENT; TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE; FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE; EDUCATION; TRAINING; TECHNOLOGY TRANSFER; WATERSHED PROTECTION; AND, DEMONSTRATION PROJECTS.
Definition: GRANTS PROVIDED TO ASSIST STATES AND INDIAN TRIBES IN IMPLEMENTING APPROVED ELEMENTS OF NPS MANAGEMENT PROGRAMS.

Name: CATEGORICAL GRANT:  PUBLIC WATER SYSTEM SUPERVISION (PWSS)
Title: 03 - CATEGORICAL GRANT:  PUBLIC WATER SYSTEM SUPERVISION (PWSS)
Laws: A
Narrative: GRANTS ARE PROVIDED TO STATES WITH PRIMARY ENFORCEMENT AUTHORITY TO IMPLEMENT THE NATIONAL PRIMARY DRINKING WATER REGULATIONS (NPDWR).  THESE REGULATIONS SET FORTH MONITORING, REPORTING, COMPLIANCE TRACKING, TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE, AND ENFORCEMENT ELEMENTS TO ENSURE THAT THE NATION'S WATER SUPPLIES ARE FREE FROM CONTAMINATION THAT MAY POSE ADVERSE HEALTH EFFECTS.  THESE GRANTS ARE A KEY IMPLEMENTATION TOOL, UNDER THE SAFE DRINKING WATER ACT, TO ENSURE STATES JOIN IN A FEDERAL/STATE PARTNERSHIP TO PROVIDE SAFE DRINKING WATER SUPPLIES TO THE PUBLIC.  GRANT FUNDS ARE USED BY STATES TO:PROVIDE TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE TO OWNERS AND OPERATORS OF WATER SYSTEMS;MAINTAIN COMPLIANCE DATA SYSTEMS PLUS COMPILE AND ANALYZE COMPLIANCE INFORMATION;RESPOND TO AND ENFORCE AGAINST VIOLATIONS AND RESPOND TO EMERGENCIES;CERTIFY LABORATORIES;CONDUCT LABORATORY ANALYSES;DRAFT NEW REGULATIONS AND LEGISLATIVE PROVISIONS WHERE NECESSARY; AND BUILD STATE CAPACITY.  FOR STATES AND INDIAN TRIBES WITHOUT ?PRIMACY,? THESE FUNDS ARE USED TO SUPPORT DIRECT IMPLEMENTATION ACTIVITIES BY EPA IN WYOMING AND THE DISTRICT OF COLUMBIA AND FOR DEVELOPMENTAL PWSS GRANTS TO TRIBES WITH ?TREATMENT IN A SIMILAR MANNER AS A STATE? (TAS).  THESE FUNDS HELP TRIBES DEVELOP THE DRINKING WATER PROGRAM ON INDIAN LANDS WITH THE GOAL OF TRIBAL AUTHORITIES ACHIEVING ?PRIMACY.?  A PORTION OF THE FUNDS ALLOCATED TO ?PRIMACY? STATES AND TRIBES THAT HAVE NOT YET ACQUIRED THE NECESSARY STATUTORY/REGULATORY AUTHORITIES TO IMPLEMENT NEW FEDERAL REQUIREMENTS OR WHERE A JURISDICTION IS UNABLE OR UNWILLING TO ASSUME ?PRIMACY? ARE USED BY EPA FOR DIRECT IMPLEMENTATION TO ENSURE COMPLIANCE WITH THE NEW PWSS PROGRAM REQUIREMENTS.FOR DIRECT IMPLEMENTATION PURPOSES UNDER PWSS, USE OF THESE FUNDS ARE AUTHORIZED TO FUND SEE ENROLLEES, THROUGH NONPROFIT ORGANIZATIONS.  EPA CAN ALSO USE THESE FUNDS TO AWARD A DIRECT IMPLEMENTATION TRIBAL COOPERATIVE AGREEMENT (DITCA) TO AN INDIAN TRIBE FOR INTERTRIBAL CONSORTIUM TO ASSIST EPA IN CARRYING OUT ITS DIRECT IMPLEMENTATION AUTHORITY UNDER THE PWSS PROGRAM.PWSS FUNDS CAN ALSO BE UTILIZED, AT THE REQUEST OF THE ELIGIBLE STATE, LOCAL, AND/OR TRIBAL GOVERNMENTS, TO FUND ASSOCIATED PROGRAM SUPPORT COST ACTIVITIES.  THE ASSOCIATED PROGRAM SUPPORT COSTS' AUTHORITY IS GENERALLY USED TO FUND MULTI-JURISDICTIONAL NONPROFIT ORGANIZATIONS THAT ARE CONTROLLED BY STATE, LOCAL, AND/OR TRIBAL GOVERNMENTS AND SUPPORT THE ENVIRONMENTAL PROGRAMS OF THE GOVERNMENTS.   AT THE REQUEST OF THE ELIGIBLE RECIPIENTS, EPA TAKES A PORTION OF EACH RECIPIENTS' ALLOTMENT FOR THE SPECIFIC PROGRAM FOR WHICH THE REQUEST IS MADE AND AWARDS A GRANT UNDER SECTION 1442(C)(3)(C) OF THE SDWA TO THE NONPROFIT TO CONDUCT ACTIVITIES THAT BENEFIT THE PROGRAM OF THE PARTICIPATING RECIPIENTS.
Definition: ASSISTANCE TO IMPLEMENT AND ENFORCE NATIONAL PRIMARY DRINKING WATER REGULATIONS TO ENSURE THE SAFETY OF THE NATION'S DRINKING WATER RESOURCES AND TO PROTECT PUBLIC HEALTH.

Name: CATEGORICAL GRANT: STATE AND LOCAL AIR QUALITY MANAGEMENT
Title: 04 - CATEGORICAL GRANT: STATE AND LOCAL AIR QUALITY MANAGEMENT
Laws: CLEAN AIR ACT, SECTIONS 103, 105, AND 106
Narrative: STATE AND LOCAL AIR POLLUTION CONTROL AGENCIES CAN REQUEST THAT THEIR STAG FUNDS BE USED TO OBTAIN SEE PROGRAM RESOURCES TO ASSIST THEM IN CARRYING OUT THEIR CLEAN AIR ACT RESPONSIBILITIES UNDER THIS PROGRAM/PROJECT. REGIONS ALSO MAY USE THIS PROGRAM/PROJECT TO OBTAIN SEE RESOURCES, BUT ONLY WHEN CARRYING OUT DIRECT IMPLEMENTATION UNDER THE CLEAN AIR ACT (I.E., WHEN EPA IS IMPLEMENTING A REQUIRED STATE OR LOCAL RESPONSIBILITY IN THE ABSENCE OF AN ACCEPTABLE STATE, TRIBAL OR LOCAL PROGRAM).  NAAQSOPERATE, MAINTAIN, AND REPORT DATA FROM MONITORING NETWORKS FOR OZONE, SULFUR DIOXIDE, LEAD, CARBON MONOXIDE, PARTICULATE MATTER, AND NITROGEN DIOXIDE.  OPERATE, MAINTAIN, AND REPORT DATA FROM THE INTERAGENCY MONITORING OF PROTECTED VISUAL ENVIRONMENTS (IMPROVE) NETWORK.DEVELOP AND IMPLEMENT PRE-CONSTRUCTION/NEW SOURCE PERMITTING PROGRAMS.COLLECT AND REPORT EMISSIONS INVENTORY DATA.ANALYZE AIR QUALITY DATA FOR PURPOSES OF DESIGNATING ALL AREAS AS ATTAINMENT/UNCLASSIFIABLE OR NON-ATTAINMENT.ISSUE SYNTHETIC MINOR TITLE V PERMITS.SUBMIT ATTAINMENT DESIGNATIONS UNDER REVISED NATIONAL AIR STANDARDS.DEVELOP AND SUBMIT REQUIRED SIPS.PREPARE AND SUBMIT TO EPA MAINTENANCE PLANS AND REQUESTS FOR REDESIGNATION TO ATTAINMENT. AIR TOXICSASSUME DELEGATION/IMPLEMENTATION OF APPLICABLE PROMULGATED STANDARDS ISSUED UNDER SECTIONS 111(D), 112, AND 129. DEVELOP AND IMPLEMENT STANDARDS, PLANS AND STRATEGIES AND ACTIONS TO PRESERVE AIR QUALITY IMPROVEMENTS ALREADY MADE. COLLECT, ANALYZE, AND REPORT HAP EMISSION INVENTORIES. ESTABLISH AND MAINTAIN A NATIONAL AIR TOXICS TRENDS MONITORING NETWORK.COLLECT AND ANALYZE AMBIENT AIR QUALITY DATA FOR AIR TOXICS FROM MONITORING SITES.CONDUCT COMMUNITY-SCALE RISK ASSESSMENT PROGRAMS.SUPPORT EFFORTS IN REDUCING AIR TOXICS DEPOSITION IN GREAT WATER BODIES.CLEAN AIR ALLOWANCE TRADING PROGRAMSSUPPORT MULTI-STATE EMISSIONS TRADING PROGRAMS THROUGH DATA SYSTEMS FUNDED AND MANAGED BY THE AGENCY.
Definition: THIS PROGRAM INCLUDES FUNDING FOR STATE AND LOCAL AIR POLLUTION CONTROL AGENCIES DESIGNATED BY GOVERNORS.  UNDER CAA SECTION 105 GRANTS, STATE AND LOCAL AIR POLLUTION CONTROL AGENCIES MAY DEVELOP AND IMPLEMENT CONTINUING PROGRAMS FOR THE PREVENTION AND CONTROL OF AIR POLLUTION OR IMPLEMENTATION OF NATIONAL PRIMARY AND SECONDARY AMBIENT AIR STANDARDS.  UNDER CAA SECTION 103, STATE AND LOCAL AIR POLLUTION CONTROL AGENCIES MAY RECEIVE GRANTS TO CONDUCT AND PROMOTE CERTAIN TYPES OF RESEARCH, INVESTIGATIONS, EXPERIMENTS, DEMONSTRATIONS, SURVEYS, STUDIES AND TRAINING RELATED TO AIR POLLUTION, BUT NOT INCLUDED IN CONTINUING PROGRAMS. ALLOWABLE ACTIVITIES ARE DESCRIBED IN ?GUIDANCE FOR FUNDING AIR AND RADIATION ACTIVITIES USING THE STAG APPROPRIATION,? ISSUED BY THE OFFICE OF AIR AND RADIATION ON SEPTEMBER 2008.  UNDER CAA SECTION 106 (INTERSTATE POLLUTION), INTERSTATE COMMISSIONS OR AGENCIES DESIGNATED BY THE GOVERNORS OF THE AFFECTED STATES MAY BE FUNDED TO DEVELOP OR RECOMMEND AIR QUALITY IMPLEMENTATION PLANS FOR DESIGNATED AIR QUALITY CONTROL REGIONS.

Name: CATEGORICAL GRANT:  RADON
Title: 05 - CATEGORICAL GRANT:  RADON
Laws: RADON GAS AND INDOOR AIR QUALITY RESEARCH ACT OF TITLE IV OF THESUPERFUND AMENDMENTS AND RE-AUTHORIZATION ACT (SARA) OF 1986TOXIC SUBSTANCES CONTROL ACT (TSCA), SECTION 6, TITLES II, AND TITLE   III (15 U.S.C. 2605 AND 2641-2671)INDOOR RADON ABATEMENT ACT (IRAA), SECTION 306TOXIC SUBSTANCES CONTROL ACT (TSCA), SECTION 10CLEAN AIR ACT AMENDMENTS
Narrative: PROVIDE FUNDING UNDER STATE INDOOR RADON GRANTS (SIRG) TO CONDUCT: RADON SURVEYS, PUBLIC INFORMATION AND EDUCATIONAL MATERIALS; RADON CONTROL PROGRAMS; PURCHASE OF RADON MEASUREMENT EQUIPMENT OR DEVICES; PURCHASE AND MAINTENANCE OF ANALYTIC EQUIPMENT; TRAINING; PROGRAM OVERHEAD AND ADMINISTRATION; DATA STORAGE AND MANAGEMENT; MITIGATION DEMONSTRATIONS; TOLL-FREE HOTLINES; AND DEVELOPMENT OF STATE RADON PROGRAMS.PROVIDE SUPPORT FOR POTENTIAL IMPLEMENTATION OF 1996 AMENDMENTS TO THE SAFE DRINKING WATER ACT (SDWA) THAT INCLUDES PROVISIONS FOR MULTI-MEDIA MITIGATION OF RADON IN DRINKING WATER.STATE AND LOCAL AIR POLLUTION CONTROL AGENCIES CAN REQUEST THAT THEIR STAG FUNDS BE USED TO OBTAIN SEE PROGRAM RESOURCES TO ASSIST THEM IN CARRYING OUT THEIR CLEAN AIR ACT RESPONSIBILITIES UNDER THIS PROGRAM/PROJECT.
Definition: THE PROGRAM INCLUDES FUNDING FOR STATE RADON PROGRAMS TO SUPPORT RADON OUTREACH, EDUCATION,  TESTING, MITIGATION AND RADON RESISTANT NEW CONSTRUCTION, AND OTHER ACTIVITIES TO REDUCE PUBLIC HEALTH  RISKS FROM RADON-INDUCED LUNG CANCER.

Name: CATEGORICAL GRANT:  POLLUTION CONTROL (SEC. 106)
Title: 06 - CATEGORICAL GRANT:  POLLUTION CONTROL (SEC. 106)
Laws: CLEAN WATER ACT, SECTION 106
Narrative: SECTION 106 GRANTS PROVIDE BROAD SUPPORT TO ASSIST STATES (INCLUDING TERRITORIES, THE DISTRICT OF COLUMBIA), INDIAN TRIBES QUALIFIED UNDER SECTION 518(E), AND INTERSTATE AGENCIES IN ESTABLISHING AND MAINTAINING ADEQUATE PROGRAMS FOR THE PREVENTION AND CONTROL OF SURFACE AND GROUND WATER POLLUTION FROM POINT AND NON-POINT SOURCES. ACTIVITIES SUPPORTED THROUGH THESE GRANTS INCLUDE NATIONAL POLLUTANT DISCHARGE ELIMINATION SYSTEM (NPDES) PERMITTING, WATER QUALITY PLANNING AND MONITORING, WATER QUALITY STANDARDS DEVELOPMENT, SURVEILLANCE AND ENFORCEMENT, ADVICE AND ASSISTANCE TO LOCAL AGENCIES, TRAINING, AND PUBLIC INFORMATION.SECTION 106 GRANTS MAY ALSO BE USED AS AN ?IN-KIND? GRANT IF A STATE OR TRIBE REQUESTS APPLICATION OF PART OF THEIR SECTION 106 FUNDING ALLOCATION TO PURCHASE EQUIPMENT OR SERVICES UNDER AN EPA CONTRACT.IF FUNDS REMAIN IN A STATE OR TRIBE'S ALLOTMENT AFTER CWA 106 GRANTS HAVE BEEN MADE OR BECAUSE NO GRANT WAS MADE, EPA MAY, SUBJECT TO ANY LIMITATIONS CONTAINED IN APPROPRIATIONS ACTS, USE ALL OR PART OF THE FUNDS APPROPRIATED UNDER SECTION 106 OF THE CWA FOR DIRECT IMPLEMENTATION BY THE FEDERAL GOVERNMENT OF A PROGRAM REQUIRED BY LAW IN THE ABSENCE OF AN ACCEPTABLE STATE OR TRIBAL PROGRAM. STAG CWA 106 FUNDS EARMARKED FOR A SPECIFIC PURPOSE (SUCH AS WATER QUALITY MONITORING), MAY NOT BE USED TO SUPPORT A FEDERAL PROGRAM REQUIRED BY LAW IN THE STATE OR TRIBE IN THE ABSENCE OF AN ACCEPTABLE STATE OR TRIBAL PROGRAM..
Definition: GRANTS TO STATES, TRIBES, AND INTERSTATE AGENCIES TO SUPPORT KEY PROGRAMS FOR THE PREVENTION, REDUCTION AND ELIMINATION OF SURFACE AND GROUND WATER POLLUTION FROM POINT AND NON-POINT SOURCES FOR ENHANCING THE ECOLOGICAL HEALTH OF THE NATION'S WATERS.

Name: CATEGORICAL GRANT:  WETLANDS PROGRAM DEVELOPMENT
Title: 07 - CATEGORICAL GRANT:  WETLANDS PROGRAM DEVELOPMENT
Laws: CLEAN WATER ACT SECTION 1041990 GREAT LAKES CRITICAL PROGRAMS ACT; 2002 GREAT LAKES AND LAKE CHAMPLAIN ACT; COASTAL WETLANDS PLANNING, PROTECTION, AND RESTORATION ACT OF 1990; ESTUARIES AND CLEAN WATERS ACT OF 2000; NORTH AMERICAN WETLANDS CONSERVATION ACT; WATER RESOURCES DEVELOPMENT ACT; 1909 THE BOUNDARY WATERS TREATY; 1978 GREAT LAKES WATER QUALITY AGREEMENT; 1987 GLWQA; 1996 HABITAT AGENDA; 1997 CANADA-U.S. GREAT LAKES BI-NATIONAL TOXICS STRATEGY; U.S.-CANADA AGREEMENTS.
Narrative: GRANTS ARE USED TO DEVELOP NEW OR REFINE EXISTING STATE AND TRIBAL WETLAND PROGRAMS IN ONE OR MORE OF THE FOLLOWING AREAS: MONITORING AND ASSESSMENT, VOLUNTARY RESTORATION AND PROTECTION, REGULATORY PROGRAMS INCLUDING 401 CERTIFICATION AND 404 ASSUMPTION, AND WETLAND WATER QUALITY STANDARDS. STATES AND TRIBES DEVELOP PROGRAM ELEMENTS BASED ON THEIR GOALS AND RESOURCES.  GRANTS SUPPORT DEVELOPMENT OF STATE AND TRIBAL WETLAND PROGRAMS THAT FURTHER THE GOALS OF THE CLEAN WATER ACT (CWA) AND IMPROVE WATER QUALITY IN WATERSHEDS THROUGHOUT THE COUNTRY.
Definition: REGULATORY AND NON-REGULATORY EFFORTS TO PROTECT AND RESTORE WETLANDS AND RIPARIAN HABITAT

Name: CATEGORICAL GRANT:  UNDERGROUND INJECTION CONTROL  (UIC)
Title: 08 - CATEGORICAL GRANT:  UNDERGROUND INJECTION CONTROL  (UIC)
Laws: SAFE DRINKING WATER ACT (SDWA)
Narrative: THE SAFE DRINKING WATER ACT ESTABLISHED THE UIC PROGRAM TO PROVIDE SAFEGUARDS SO THAT INJECTION WELLS DO NOT ENDANGER CURRENT AND FUTURE UNDERGROUND SOURCES OF DRINKING WATER.  THE MOST ACCESSIBLE UNDERGROUND FRESH WATER IS STORED IN SHALLOW GEOLOGICAL FORMATIONS (I.E., SHALLOW AQUIFERS) AND IS THE MOST VULNERABLE TO CONTAMINATION. FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE, IN THE FORM OF GRANTS, IS PROVIDED TO STATES THAT HAVE PRIMARY ENFORCEMENT AUTHORITY (PRIMACY) TO IMPLEMENT AND MAINTAIN UIC PROGRAMS. ELIGIBLE TRIBES WHO DEMONSTRATE INTENT TO ACHIEVE PRIMACY MAY ALSO RECEIVE A GRANT FOR THE INITIAL DEVELOPMENT OF UIC PROGRAMS AND BE DESIGNATED FOR TREATMENT AS A "STATE" IF THEIR PROGRAMS ARE APPROVED.  WHERE A JURISDICTION IS UNABLE OR UNWILLING TO ASSUME PRIMACY, EPA USES GRANT FUNDS FOR DIRECT IMPLEMENTATION OF FEDERAL UIC REQUIREMENTS.
Definition: FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE, IN THE FORM OF GRANTS, IS PROVIDED TO STATES THAT HAVE PRIMARY ENFORCEMENT AUTHORITY (PRIMACY) TO IMPLEMENT AND MAINTAIN UIC PROGRAMS.

Name: CATEGORICAL GRANT:  PESTICIDES PROGRAM IMPLEMENTATION
Title: 09 - CATEGORICAL GRANT:  PESTICIDES PROGRAM IMPLEMENTATION
Laws: FEDERAL INSECTICIDE, FUNGICIDE AND RODENTICIDE ACT (FIFRA)FEDERAL FOOD, DRUG AND COSMETIC ACT (FFDCA) FOOD QUALITY PROTECTION ACT (FQPA) ENDANGERED SPECIES ACT (ESA)
Narrative: ACTIVITIES INCLUDE ALL THOSE RELATED AND NECESSARY TO IMPLEMENT WORKER PROTECTION, ENDANGERED SPECIES, COORDINATION WITH STATES/TRIBES OTHER FEDERAL AGENCIES, CERTIFICATION AND TRAINING OF PESTICIDE APPLICATORS, COORDINATION WITH OTHER AGENCIES, PESTICIDES ENVIRONMENTAL STEWARDSHIP, AND IPM.
Definition: GRANTS TO STATES AND TRIBES FOR SAFER USE OF PESTICIDES, INCLUDING WORKER PROTECTION, CERTIFICATION AND TRAINING OF PESTICIDE APPLICATORS, PROTECTION OF ENDANGERED SPECIES, TRIBAL PESTICIDE PROGRAMS, INTEGRATED PEST MANAGEMENT AND ENVIRONMENTAL STEWARDSHIP.

Name: CATEGORICAL GRANT:  LEAD
Title: 10 - CATEGORICAL GRANT:  LEAD
Laws: TOXIC SUBSTANCE CONTROL ACT (TSCA);RESIDENTIAL LEAD-BASED PAINT HAZARD REDUCTION ACT OF 1992 (WHICH IS DESIGNATED AS TITLE IV OF TSCA).
Narrative: CERTIFICATION AND TRAINING:  EPA HAS AUTHORIZED 39 STATES & TERRITORIES, 3 TRIBES,THE DISTRICT OF COLUMBIA AND PUERTO RICO TO ADMINISTER AND ENFORCE PROGRAMS FOR LEAD ABATEMENT ACCREDITATION CERTIFICATION, AND WORKPLACE STANDARDS IN TARGET HOUSING.  EPA WILL ADMINISTER AND ENFORCE THE FEDERAL LEAD PROGRAM IN ALL NON-AUTHORIZED STATES, TERRITORIES AND TRIBES.  FUNDS SUPPORT THE TRAINING OF INDIVIDUALS ENGAGED IN LEAD-BASED PAINT ACTIVITIES, THE ACCREDITATION OF TRAINING PROGRAMS FOR THOSE INDIVIDUALS, AND THE CERTIFICATION OF CONTRACTORS ENGAGED IN LEAD-BASED PAINT ACTIVITIES.  DIRECT IMPLEMENTATION FUNDS MAY INCLUDE WORKING CAPITAL FUND SUPPORT. NINE STATES HAVE RECEIVED AUTHORIZATION FROM EPA TO ADMINISTER THE RRP PROGRAM.  (HQ AND RT)PERFORMANCE MEASUREMENT:  FUNDS ALSO SUPPORT MEASURING PROGRESS IN REDUCING LEAD-BASED PAINT EXPOSURES THROUGH THE COLLECTION OF DATA ASSOCIATED WITH THE LEAD ABATEMENT PROGRAM, INCLUDING HOW MANY PROFESSIONALS BECOME CERTIFIED AS RISK ASSESSORS, INSPECTORS, WORKERS OR SUPERVISORS; HOW MANY HOUSING UNIT ABATEMENTS ARE COMPLETED, ETC.  (HQ AND RT)INFRASTRUCTURE DEVELOPMENT:  FUNDS SUPPORT DEVELOPMENT OF DATABASES AND OTHER TOOLS USED BY STATE AND EPA REGIONAL OFFICE IMPLEMENTERS IN SUPPORT OF LEAD-BASED PAINT ABATEMENT CERTIFICATION AND TRAINING ACTIVITIES.  EPA WILL CONTINUE TO IMPLEMENT THE LEAD BASED PAINT TRAINING & CERTIFICATION PROGRAM IN THOSE AREAS THAT DO NOT HAVE AN AUTHORIZED PROGRAM.  (HQ)
Definition: GRANTS TO STATES AND TRIBES FOR LEAD-BASED PAINT TRAINING AND CERTIFICATION PROGRAMS. DIRECT IMPLEMENTATION BY EPA IN LOCATIONS WITHOUT AUTHORIZED PROGRAMS.

Name: CATEGORICAL GRANT:  HAZARDOUS WASTE FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE
Title: 11 - CATEGORICAL GRANT:  HAZARDOUS WASTE FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE
Laws: SOLID WASTE DISPOSAL ACT (SWDA) SECTION 3011 (A) AND (C) RESOURCE CONSERVATION AND RECOVERY ACT (RCRA) OF 1976   PUBLIC LAW 94-580, 42 U.S.C. 6901 ET SEQ.  DEPARTMENT OF VETERANS AFFAIRS AND HOUSING AND URBAN DEVELOPMENT AND INDEPENDENT AGENCIES APPROPRIATION ACT, PUBLIC LAW 105-275   112 STAT. 2461, 2499 (1998)
Narrative: THIS PROGRAM PROVIDES GRANTS TO STATES AND TRIBES TO FOSTER THEIR CAPABILITY TO IMPLEMENT THE WASTE MINIMIZATION RCRA HAZARDOUS WASTE PROGRAM.  THE STATES PROPOSE LEGISLATION AND UPGRADE REGULATIONS TO ACHIEVE EQUIVALENCE WITH THE FEDERAL HAZARDOUS WASTE MANAGEMENT PROGRAM AND APPLY TO EPA FOR AUTHORIZATION TO ADMINISTER THE PROGRAM.  THIS PROGRAM PROVIDES PERFORMANCE PARTNERSHIP GRANTS TO STATES. THIS ACCOUNT ALSO PROVIDES FUNDING FOR THE DIRECT IMPLEMENTATION OF THE RCRA PROGRAM BY REGION 7 AND 10 FOR THE STATES OF IOWA AND ALASKA, RESPECTIVELY.  BEGINNING IN FY 1999 (AS A RESULT OF THE FY 1998 APPROPRIATIONS ACT), THIS PROGRAM PROJECT UNDER THE STAG APPROPRIATION WAS INCREASED TO PROVIDE SUPPORT WHERE APPROPRIATE TO TRIBAL WASTE PROGRAMS. THIS PROGRAM ALSO ACCEPTS FUNDS REPROGRAMMED AS A PART OF THE ANNUAL DISTRIBUTION OF THE INDIAN ENVIRONMENTAL GENERAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAM (GAP) FUNDS. THE GAP ACT OF 1992 AUTHORIZES EPA TO PROVIDE GRANTS TO ELIGIBLE INDIAN TRIBAL GOVERNMENTS OR INTERTRIBAL CONSORTIA FOR PLANNING, DEVELOPING, ESTABLISHING, AND IMPLEMENTING ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION PROGRAMS ON INDIAN LANDS.THE FOLLOWING ARE ACTIVITIES WHICH WILL BE ACCOMPLISHED THROUGH THE GRANTS PROGRAM:IDENTIFY WASTES AND PRIORITY CHEMICALS FOR REDUCTION.IMPLEMENT A PROGRAM TO REDUCE WASTES AND PRIORITY CHEMICALS.  ISSUE PERMITS, PERMIT MODIFICATIONS OR PERMIT RENEWALS FOR HAZARDOUS WASTE RECYCLING FACILITIES. IMPLEMENT SPECIAL PARTNERSHIPS WITH INDUSTRY AND INTERGOVERNMENTAL PARTNERSHIP AGREEMENTS (IPAS) TO FACILITATE ENVIRONMENTALLY SOUND HAZARDOUS WASTE RECYCLING.
Definition: THIS PROGRAM PROVIDES GRANTS TO STATES AND TRIBES TO FOSTER THEIR CAPABILITY TO IMPLEMENT THE RCRA PROGRAM. EPA WILL REDUCE MATERIALS AND ENERGY USE THROUGH PRODUCT AND PROCESS REDESIGN AND INCREASE MATERIALS AND ENERGY RECOVERY FROM WASTES OTHERWISE REQUIRING DISPOSAL.

Name: CATEGORICAL GRANT:  PESTICIDES ENFORCEMENT
Title: 12 - CATEGORICAL GRANT:  PESTICIDES ENFORCEMENT
Laws: FEDERAL INSECTICIDE, FUNGICIDE, AND RODENTICIDE ACT (FIFRA) OF 1972, AS AMENDED AND FOOD QUALITY PROTECTION ACT (FQPA) OF 1996.
Narrative: EPA WILL AWARD STATE AND TRIBAL ENFORCEMENT GRANTS TO ASSIST IN THE IMPLEMENTATION OF THE COMPLIANCE AND ENFORCEMENT PROVISIONS OF FIFRA.  THESE GRANTS SUPPORT STATE AND TRIBAL COMPLIANCE AND ENFORCEMENT ACTIVITIES DESIGNED TO PROTECT THE ENVIRONMENT FROM HARMFUL CHEMICALS AND PESTICIDES.  EPA?S SUPPORT TO STATE AND TRIBAL PESTICIDE PROGRAMS WILL EMPHASIZE PESTICIDE WORKER PROTECTION STANDARDS, HIGH RISK PESTICIDE ACTIVITIES INCLUDING ANTIMICROBIALS, PESTICIDE MISUSE IN URBAN AREAS, AND THE MISAPPLICATION OF STRUCTURAL PESTICIDES.  STATES ALSO WILL CONTINUE TO CONDUCT COMPLIANCE MONITORING INSPECTIONS ON CORE PESTICIDE REQUIREMENTS.OPERATE COMPREHENSIVE COMPLIANCE MONITORING AND ENFORCEMENT ACTIONS RELATED TO PESTICIDES PROGRAMS.PROVIDE DIRECT SUPPORT TO IMPLEMENT COMPLIANCE MONITORING AND ENFORCEMENT PROGRAMS IN NON AND/OR PARTIALLY-DELEGATED STATES UNDER FIFRA.
Definition: PESTICIDE ENFORCEMENT GRANTS TO STATES AND TRIBES ENSURE PESTICIDE PRODUCT AND USER COMPLIANCE WITH PROVISIONS OF THE FEDERAL INSECTICIDE, FUNGICIDE AND RODENTICIDE ACT (FIFRA).  AREAS OF FOCUS INCLUDE PROBLEMS RELATING TO PESTICIDE WORKER SAFETY PROTECTION, INEFFECTIVE ANTIMICROBIAL PRODUCTS, FOOD SAFETY, ADVERSE EFFECTS, AND E-COMMERCE.  THE PROGRAM PROVIDES COMPLIANCE ASSISTANCE TO THE REGULATED COMMUNITY THROUGH SUCH RESOURCES AS EPA?S NATIONAL AGRICULTURE COMPLIANCE ASSISTANCE CENTER, SEMINARS, GUIDANCE DOCUMENTS, BROCHURES, AND OUTREACH TO FOSTER KNOWLEDGE OF AND COMPLIANCE WITH ENVIRONMENTAL LAWS PERTAINING TO PESTICIDES.  THE PROGRAM ALSO SPONSORS TRAINING FOR STATE/TRIBAL INSPECTORS THROUGH THE PESTICIDE INSPECTOR RESIDENTIAL PROGRAM (PIRT) AND FOR STATE/TRIBAL MANAGERS THROUGH THE PESTICIDE REGULATORY EDUCATION PROGRAM (PREP).

Name: CATEGORICAL GRANT:  POLLUTION PREVENTION
Title: 13 - CATEGORICAL GRANT:  POLLUTION PREVENTION
Laws: POLLUTION PREVENTION ACT (PPA)TOXIC SUBSTANCES CONTROL ACT (TSCA)
Narrative: TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE:  FUNDS SUPPORT PROVISION OF TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE SERVICES TO COMPANIES, PRIMARILY THOUGH NOT EXCLUSIVELY SMALL BUSINESSES, AND OTHER INSTITUTIONS SEEKING TO ACHIEVE REDUCED POLLUTION THROUGH SOURCE REDUCTION.  PROVIDERS INCLUDE STATE AGENCIES, STATE UNIVERSITIES, OTHER ELEMENTS OF STATE GOVERNMENTS, AND TRIBAL GOVERNMENTS.   (HQ AND RT)CAPACITY BUILDING:  FUNDS SUPPORT STATES AND TRIBES TO DEVELOP CAPACITY TO PROMOTE POLLUTION PREVENTION IN THEIR IMPLEMENTATION OF ENVIRONMENTAL PROGRAMS AND TO PROVIDE P2 TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE TO BUSINESSES.  (HQ AND RT)INFORMATION EXCHANGE:   FUNDS SUPPORT THE POLLUTION PREVENTION RESOURCE EXCHANGE (P2RX) WHICH COORDINATES THE DEVELOPMENT AND DISSEMINATION OF UP-TO-DATE INFORMATION ON P2 APPROACHES.  (HQ AND RT)
Definition: GRANT PROGRAM FOR STATES AND TRIBES TO TEST INNOVATIVE POLLUTION PREVENTION (P2) APPROACHES AND TO PROVIDE P2 TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE TO COMPANIES.

Name: CATEGORICAL GRANT:  TOXICS SUBSTANCES COMPLIANCE
Title: 14 - CATEGORICAL GRANT:  TOXICS SUBSTANCES COMPLIANCE
Laws: TOXIC SUBSTANCES CONTROL ACT (TSCA) OF 1976FOOD QUALITY PROTECTION ACT (FQPA) OF 1996LEAD RENOVATION, REPAIR, AND PAINTING RULE OF 2010
Narrative: THE STATE GRANT PROGRAMS ARE DESIGNED TO BUILD ENVIRONMENTAL PARTNERSHIPS WITH STATES BY STRENGTHENING THE ABILITY OF STATES AND LOCALITIES TO ADDRESS THE NATION'S ENVIRONMENTAL AND HUMAN HEALTH THREATS. OECA AWARDS STATE AND TRIBAL ENFORCEMENT COOPERATIVE AGREEMENTS TO ASSIST IN THE IMPLEMENTATION OF THE ENFORCEMENT PROVISIONS OF THE TOXIC SUBSTANCES CONTROL ACT (TSCA). THESE GRANTS SUPPORT STATECOMPLIANCE ACTIVITIES TO PROTECT THE ENVIRONMENT FROM HARMFUL CHEMICALS.OPERATE COMPREHENSIVE COMPLIANCE MONITORING AND ENFORCEMENT PROGRAMS RELATED TO LEAD-BASED PAINT AND COMPLIANCE MONITORING ONLY RELATED TO ASBESTOS AND PCBS PROGRAMS. (HQ, RT)
Definition: THE EPA/STATE AND TRIBAL ASSISTANCE GRANTS (STAG) PROGRAM BUILDS ENVIRONMENTAL PARTNERSHIPS WITH STATES AND TRIBES TO HELP BUILD STATE CAPACITY AND STRENGTHEN THEIR ABILITY TO MONITOR RULES PROMULGATED UNDER TSCA SUCH AS POLYCHLORINATED BIPHENYLS (PCBS), ASBESTOS, AND TO MONITOR AND ENFORCE STATE LEAD-BASED PAINT RULES.  THE STAG GRANTS ARE USED TO ENSURE COMPLIANCE WITH STANDARDS FOR THE PROPER USE, STORAGE, AND DISPOSAL OF PCBS.  THE ASBESTOS PROGRAM ENSURES COMPLIANCE WITH STANDARDS TO PREVENT EXPOSURE OF SCHOOL CHILDREN, TEACHERS, AND STAFF TO ASBESTOS FIBERS IN SCHOOL BUILDINGS.  THE FUNDS ALSO SUPPORT COMPLIANCE WITH OTHER ASBESTOS REGULATIONS SUCH AS THE ASBESTOS BAN AND PHASE-OUT RULE.  IN LEAD-BASED PAINT AUTHORIZED STATES, THE PROGRAMS ENSURES THAT LEAD ABATEMENT AND RENOVATION FIRMS AND WORKERS ARE PROPERLY TRAINED AND CERTIFIED, GIVE NOTICE, AND USE WORK PRACTICES STANDARDS. THE STAG FUNDS ALSO ENSURE THAT ASBESTOS AND LEAD-BASED PAINT ABATEMENT WORKERS HAVE RECEIVED PROPER TRAINING AND CERTIFICATION TO ENSURE PROPER WORK PRACTICE STANDARDS ARE USED DURING THE ABATEMENT PROCESS. THE LEAD-BASED PAINT PROGRAM ALSO ASSURES THAT STATE TRAINING PROVIDERS AND TRAINING PROGRAMS HAVE BEEN APPROVED BY EPA.

Name: CATEGORICAL GRANT:  TRIBAL GENERAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAM
Title: 15 - CATEGORICAL GRANT:  TRIBAL GENERAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAM
Laws: INDIAN ENVIRONMENTAL GENERAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAM (GAP) ACT OF 1992
Narrative: THIS GRANT PROGRAM ENABLES THE AGENCY TO ESTABLISH  EFFECTIVE PARTNERSHIPS WITH TRIBES AS THEY DEVELOP ENVIRONMENTAL PROGRAMS IN INDIAN COUNTRY. THE PROGRAM PROVIDES FUNDING ASSISTANCE TO FEDERALLY RECOGNIZED TRIBAL GOVERNMENTS AND TRIBAL CONSORTIA FOR THE PURPOSE OF CONDUCTING PUBLIC EDUCATION, OUTREACH EFFORTS, PLANNING, DEVELOPING AND ESTABLISHING THE CAPABILITY TO IMPLEMENT ENVIRONMENTAL MANAGEMENT PROGRAMS. TRIBES RECEIVING GENERAL ASSISTANCE AGREEMENTS MAY TAILOR CAPACITY-BUILDING THROUGH AN INTEGRATED PLAN THAT MAY INCLUDE GENERAL ASSISTANCE COMPLIMENTED AND/OR SUPPLEMENTED WITH ADDITIONAL ASSISTANCE THROUGH PROJECT AND PROGRAM-SPECIFIC GRANTS.
Definition: PROVIDES GENERAL ASSISTANCE GRANTS TO ELIGIBLE TRIBAL GOVERNMENTS AND INTERTRIBAL CONSORTIA FOR PLANNING, DEVELOPING, AND ESTABLISHING ENVIRONMENTAL PROGRAMS IN INDIAN COUNTRY.

Name: CATEGORICAL GRANT:  UNDERGROUND STORAGE TANKS
Title: 16 - CATEGORICAL GRANT:  UNDERGROUND STORAGE TANKS
Laws: SOLID WASTE DISPOSAL ACT (SWDA) OF 1976, AS AMENDED (SUBTITLE I); SECTION 2007(F)
Narrative: THIS PROGRAM PROJECT PROVIDES GRANTS TO STATES EITHER DIRECTLY OR THROUGH NON-GOVERNMENTAL ORGANIZATIONS TO BUILD CORE UNDERGROUND STORAGE TANK (UST) PROGRAMS.  THE OBJECTIVE OF THIS GRANT FUNDING IS TO ASSIST STATE GOVERNMENTS IN DEVELOPING AND MAINTAINING STATE PROGRAMS WITH SUFFICIENT AUTHORITY AND ENFORCEMENT CAPABILITIES TO OPERATE IN LIEU OF THE FEDERAL PROGRAM, AND ENSURING THAT OWNERS AND OPERATORS ROUTINELY AND CORRECTLY MONITOR ALL REGULATED TANK SYSTEMS IN ACCORDANCE WITH UST REGULATIONS.  EPA ALSO WILL ASSIST THE STATES IN IMPLEMENTING EPACT PROVISIONS SUCH AS CONDUCTING ON-SITE INSPECTIONS ON THE THREE-YEAR CYCLE, AND PROHIBITING DELIVERY TO NONCOMPLIANT TANK SYSTEMS.  SPECIFICALLY WITH THESE UST CATEGORICAL GRANTS, STATES WILL FUND SUCH ACTIVITIES AS:  APPROVING SPECIFIC TECHNOLOGIES TO DETECT LEAKS FROM TANK SYSTEMS; ENSURING THAT TANK OWNERS AND OPERATORS ARE COMPLYING WITH NOTIFICATION AND OTHER REQUIREMENTS; ENSURING EQUIPMENT COMPATIBILITY; CONDUCTING INSPECTIONS; IMPLEMENTING OPERATOR TRAINING; PROHIBITING DELIVERY FOR NON-COMPLYING FACILITIES; SEEKING STATE PROGRAM APPROVAL TO OPERATE THE UST PROGRAM IN LIEU OF THE FEDERAL PROGRAM; AND REQUIRING SECONDARY CONTAINMENT OR FINANCIAL RESPONSIBILITY FOR TANK MANUFACTURERS AND INSTALLERS.  THIS GRANT FUNDING MAY BE USED IN PERFORMANCE PARTNERSHIP AGREEMENTS WITH STATES. A STATE COULD ELECT TO CONSOLIDATE THIS AND OTHER CATEGORICAL MEDIA GRANTS INTO ONE OR MORE MULTIMEDIA OR SINGLE MEDIA GRANT. THE STATE COULD THEN TARGET ITS MOST PRESSING ENVIRONMENTAL PROBLEMS AND USE THE PERFORMANCE PARTNERSHIP GRANT FOR A NUMBER OF ACTIVITIES INCLUDING POLLUTION CONTROL, ABATEMENT, AND ENFORCEMENT.  THIS PROGRAM WILL NOT COMPROMISE BASIC NATIONAL OBJECTIVES AND LEGISLATIVE REQUIREMENTS.
Definition: THE ENERGY POLICY ACT OF 2005 (EPACT) EXPANDED THE ELIGIBLE USE OF LUST FUNDS TO INCLUDE CERTAIN RELEASE PREVENTION/DETECTION ACTIVITIES, BUT IT DID NOT AUTHORIZE LUST FUNDS FOR ALL PREVENTION/DETECTION ACTIVITIES. SOME NON-LUST ELIGIBLE EXAMPLES INCLUDE: COMPLIANCE ASSISTANCE, STATE PROGRAM APPROVALS, AND TECHNICAL EQUIPMENT REVIEWS AND APPROVALS. THUS, SOME STATES STILL NEED STAG MONEY TO FUND SOME BASIC PROGRAMMATIC FUNCTIONS FOR UNDERGROUND STORAGE TANK (UST) RELEASE PREVENTION AND DETECTION PROGRAMS.

Name: CATEGORICAL GRANT:TRIBAL AIR QUALITY MANAGEMENT
Title: 17 - CATEGORICAL GRANT:TRIBAL AIR QUALITY MANAGEMENT
Laws: CLEAN AIR ACT, SECTION 103 AND 105
Narrative: DEVELOP AND IMPLEMENT TRIBAL AIR POLLUTION CONTROL PROGRAMS.FUND ORGANIZATIONS FOR THE PURPOSE OF PROVIDING TECHNICAL SUPPORT, TOOLS AND TRAINING FOR TRIBES TO BUILD CAPACITY AS APPROPRIATE.ASSESS ENVIRONMENTAL AND PUBLIC HEALTH CONDITIONS ON TRIBAL LANDS AND, WHERE APPROPRIATE, SITE MONITORS.MAINTAIN AND OPERATE TRIBAL AIR QUALITY MONITORS.TRIBAL AIR POLLUTION CONTROL AGENCIES CAN REQUEST THAT THEIR STAG FUNDS BE USED TO OBTAIN SEE PROGRAM RESOURCES TO ASSIST THEM IN CARRYING OUT THEIR CLEAN AIR ACT RESPONSIBILITIES UNDER THIS PROGRAM/PROJECT. REGIONS ALSO MAY USE THIS PROGRAM/PROJECT TO OBTAIN SEE RESOURCES, BUT ONLY WHEN CARRYING OUT DIRECT IMPLEMENTATION UNDER THE CLEAN AIR ACT (I.E., WHEN EPA IS IMPLEMENTING A REQUIRED STATE OR LOCAL RESPONSIBILITY IN THE ABSENCE OF AN ACCEPTABLE STATE, TRIBAL OR LOCAL PROGRAM).
Definition: THIS PROGRAM INCLUDES FUNDING FOR TRIBAL AIR POLLUTION CONTROL AGENCIES AND/OR TRIBES.  THROUGH CAA SECTION 105 GRANTS, TRIBES MAY DEVELOP AND IMPLEMENT PROGRAMS FOR THE PREVENTION AND CONTROL OF AIR POLLUTION OR IMPLEMENTATION OF CONTINUING NATIONAL PRIMARY AND SECONDARY AMBIENT AIR STANDARDS.  THROUGH CAA SECTION 103 GRANTS, TRIBAL AIR POLLUTION CONTROL AGENCIES OR TRIBES, COLLEGES, UNIVERSITIES, OR MULTI-TRIBE AIR POLLUTION CONTROL AGENCIES AND/OR NON-PROFIT ORGANIZATIONS MAY CONDUCT AND PROMOTE RESEARCH, INVESTIGATIONS, EXPERIMENTS, DEMONSTRATIONS, SURVEYS, STUDIES AND TRAINING RELATED TO AIR POLLUTION.  ALLOWABLE ACTIVITIES ARE DESCRIBED IN ?GUIDANCE FOR FUNDING AIR AND RADIATION ACTIVITIES USING THE STAG APPROPRIATION,? ISSUED BY THE OFFICE OF AIR AND RADIATION ON SEPTEMBER 2008.

Name: CATEGORICAL GRANT: MULTIPURPOSE GRANTS
Title: 20 - CATEGORICAL GRANT: MULTIPURPOSE GRANTS
Laws: CONSOLIDATED APPROPRIATIONS ACT, 2016-PUBLIC LAW 114-113
Narrative: [52] FUNDING PRIORITY - FISCAL YEAR 2016  FOR STATES AND TERRITORIES, FUNDING IS AVAILABLE TO SUPPORT IMPLEMENTATION OF AIR AND STATE-LED CLIMATE ACTIVITIES AND OTHER STATE-DEFINED HIGH PRIORITY ACTIVITIES.  FOR TRIBES, FUNDING IS AVAILABLE FOR TRIBES QUALIFIED UNDER CLEAN WATER ACT SECTION 518(E) THAT HAVE RECEIVED AUTHORIZATION (TREATED IN A SIMILAR MANNER TO A STATE (TAS)) FOR WATER QUALITY STANDARDS (WQS) TO SUPPORT WQS IMPLEMENTATION RELATED ACTIVITIES.    [53] FUNDING PRIORITY - FISCAL YEAR 2017  N/A  [54] FUNDING PRIORITY - FISCAL YEAR 2018 N/A  [60] TYPES OF ASSISTANCE   FORMULA GRANTS  [70] USES AND USE RESTRICTIONS   GRANTS AND COOPERATIVE AGREEMENTS ARE AVAILABLE TO SUPPORT PROGRAMS UNDER ESTABLISHED ENVIRONMENTAL STATUTES. ALLOWABLE DIRECT AND INDIRECT COSTS ARE INCLUDED, IN ACCORDANCE WITH ESTABLISHED EPA POLICIES AND REQUIREMENTS FOR ASSISTANCE AGREEMENTS. ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT AWARDS UNDER THIS PROGRAM MAY INVOLVE OR RELATE TO GEOSPATIAL INFORMATION. FURTHER INFORMATION REGARDING GEOSPATIAL INFORMATION MAY BE OBTAINED BY VIEWING THE FOLLOWING WEBSITE: HTTP://GEODATA.EPA.GOV.GRANT RECIPIENTS AND SUB-RECIPIENTS ARE ENCOURAGED TO ADOPT AND ENFORCE POLICIES THAT BAN TEXT MESSAGING WHILE DRIVING COMPANY-OWNED OR -RENTED VEHICLES OR GOVERNMENT-OWNED VEHICLES, OR WHILE DRIVING PRIVATELY-OWNED VEHICLES WHEN ON OFFICIAL GOVERNMENT BUSINESS OR WHEN PERFORMING ANY WORK FOR OR ON BEHALF OF THE GOVERNMENT. GRANT RECIPIENTS AND SUB-RECIPIENTS ARE ENCOURAGED TO CONDUCT INITIATIVES OF THE TYPE DESCRIBED IN SECTION 3(A) OF THE FEDERAL LEADERSHIP ON REDUCING TEXT MESSAGING WHILE DRIVING EXECUTIVE ORDER THAT WAS SIGNED ON OCTOBER 1, 2009.   [80] ELIGIBILITY REQUIREMENTS       [81] APPLICANT ELIGIBILITY   ASSISTANCE UNDER THIS PROGRAM IS GENERALLY AVAILABLE TO STATES, THE DISTRICT OF COLUMBIA, THE COMMONWEALTH OF PUERTO RICO, THE VIRGIN ISLANDS, GUAM, AMERICAN SAMOA, THE COMMONWEALTH OF THE NORTHERN MARIANAS, AND TRIBES QUALIFIED UNDER CLEAN WATER ACT SECTION 518(E) THAT HAVE RECEIVED AUTHORIZATION (TAS) FOR WQS. APPLICABLE GRANT GUIDANCE MAY FURTHER LIMIT APPLICANT ELIGIBILITY.  [82] BENEFICIARY ELIGIBILITY   STATES, THE DISTRICT OF COLUMBIA, THE COMMONWEALTH OF PUERTO RICO, THE VIRGIN ISLANDS, GUAM, AMERICAN SAMOA, THE COMMONWEALTH OF THE NORTHERN MARIANAS, AND TRIBES QUALIFIED UNDER CLEAN WATER ACT SECTION 518(E) WITH TAS FOR WQS.   [83] CREDENTIALS/DOCUMENTATION   COSTS WILL BE DETERMINED IN ACCORDANCE WITH 2 CFR 200, SUBPART E - COST PRINCIPLES.  [90] APPLICATION AND AWARD PROCESS       [91] PREAPPLICATION COORDINATION   DISCUSSION WITH THE APPLICABLE EPA REGIONAL OFFICE IS ADVISED FOR MULTIPURPOSE GRANT APPLICATIONS. THE STANDARD APPLICATION FORMS AS FURNISHED BY THE ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION AGENCY AND REQUIRED BY UNIFORM GRANTS GUIDANCE 2 CFR 200 MUST BE USED FOR THIS PROGRAM. FOR ADDITIONAL INFORMATION, CONTACT THE INDIVIDUAL(S) LISTED AS "INFORMATION CONTACTS" OR SEE APPENDIX IV OF THE CATALOG. THIS PROGRAM IS ELIGIBLE FOR COVERAGE UNDER E.O. 12372, "INTERGOVERNMENTAL REVIEW OF FEDERAL PROGRAMS." AN APPLICANT SHOULD CONSULT THE OFFICE OR OFFICIAL DESIGNATED AS THE SINGLE POINT OF CONTACT IN HIS OR HER STATE FOR MORE INFORMATION ON THE PROCESS THE STATE REQUIRES TO BE FOLLOWED IN APPLYING FOR ASSISTANCE, IF THE STATE HAS SELECTED THE PROGRAM FOR REVIEW. ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACT INFORMATION IS NOT REQUIRED FOR THIS PROGRAM.   [92] APPLICATION PROCEDURE   2 CFR 200, UNIFORM ADMINISTRATIVE REQUIREMENTS, COST PRINCIPLES, AND AUDIT REQUIREMENTS FOR FEDERAL AWARDS APPLIES TO THIS PROGRAM.  THE STANDARD APPLICATION FORMS AS FURNISHED BY THE FEDERAL AGENCY AND REQUIRED BY 2 CFR 200 AND 1500, AS APPLICABLE, MUST BE USED FOR THIS PROGRAM. EPA REQUIRES FINAL APPLICATIONS TO BE MADE ON STANDARD FORM 424.    COMPLETED APPLICATIONS MUST BE SUBMITTED TO THE APPROPRIATE EPA REGIONAL GRANTS ADMINISTRATION BRANCH. ADDITIONAL INFORMATION ON THE EPA GRANT PACKAGE CAN BE FOUND AT: HTTPS://WWW.EPA.GOV/GRANTS/HOW-APPLY-GRANTS.  APPLICANTS, EXCEPT IN LIMITED CIRCUMSTANCES APPROVED BY THE AGENCY, MUST SUBMIT ALL INITIAL APPLICATIONS FOR FUNDING THROUGH HTTP://WWW.GRANTS.GOV. [93] AWARD PROCEDURE   EPA WILL EVALUATE EACH APPLICATION TO DETERMINE THE ADEQUACY OF THE PROPOSED PROJECT TO MEET THE SPECIFIED PURPOSE OF THE PROJECT AND COMPLIANCE WITH OTHER APPLICABLE LAWS. EPA WILL ALSO REVIEW THE APPLICATION IN RELATION TO EPA'S GENERAL GRANT REGULATIONS AND OMB GUIDANCE.  [94] DEADLINES   CONTACT THE HEADQUARTERS OR REGIONAL OFFICE, AS APPROPRIATE, FOR APPLICATION DEADLINES.  [95] RANGE OF APPROVAL/DISAPPROVAL TIME   APPROXIMATELY 90-180 DAYS.  [96] APPEALS   DISPUTES WILL BE RESOLVED UNDER 2 CFR 1500 SUBPART E, AS APPLICABLE.  [97] RENEWALS   RENEWALS ARE SUBJECT TO APPROVAL OF EPA.[100] ASSISTANCE CONSIDERATIONS       [101] FORMULA AND MATCHING REQUIREMENTS   THIS PROGRAM UTILIZES A FORMULA ALLOCATION. MATCHING REQUIREMENTS: THIS PROGRAM DOES NOT HAVE MAINTENANCE OF EFFORT REQUIREMENTS OR MATCH REQUIREMENTS.  [102] LENGTH AND TIME PHASING OF ASSISTANCE  THE TERMS OF THE ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT SHALL BE DETERMINED AT TIME OF AWARD. THE METHOD OF FUND DISBURSEMENT WILL BE DETERMINED AT THE TIME OF AWARD.  [110] POST ASSISTANCE REQUIREMENTS       [111] REPORTS   EPA INCLUDES REPORTING REQUIREMENTS FOR GRANTS AND COOPERATIVE AGREEMENTS IN THE TERMS AND CONDITIONS OF THE AGREEMENTS. AGREEMENTS MAY REQUIRE QUARTERLY, INTERIM, AND FINAL PROGRESS REPORTS, AND FINANCIAL, EQUIPMENT, AND INVENTION REPORTS. REPORTING REQUIREMENTS ARE ALSO IDENTIFIED IN THE UNIFORM GRANTS GUIDANCE 2 CFR 200.GRANTEES ARE REQUIRED TO SUBMIT PROGRAM REPORTS IN ACCORDANCE WITH AGENCY POLICY AND THE MONITORING AND REPORTING PROGRAM PERFORMANCE REQUIREMENTS STATED IN UNIFORM GRANTS GUIDANCE 2 CFR 200. GRANTEES ARE REQUIRED TO SUBMIT FINANCIAL REPORTS IN ACCORDANCE WITH AGENCY POLICY AND THE FINANCIAL REPORTING AND FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT SYSTEMS REQUIREMENTS STATED IN THE UNIFORM GRANTS GUIDANCE 2 CFR 200. GRANTEES ARE REQUIRED TO SUBMIT PROGRESS REPORTS IN ACCORDANCE WITH AGENCY POLICY AND THE MONITORING AND REPORTING PROGRAM PERFORMANCE REQUIREMENTS STATED IN THE UNIFORM GRANTS GUIDANCE 2 CFR 200. NO EXPENDITURE REPORTS ARE REQUIRED. GRANTEES ARE REQUIRED TO PERFORM PERFORMANCE MONITORING IN ACCORDANCE WITH AGENCY POLICY AND REQUIREMENTS STATED IN THE UNIFORM GRANTS GUIDANCE 2 CFR 200.  [112] AUDITSIN ACCORDANCE WITH THE PROVISIONS OF 2 CFR 200, SUBPART F - AUDIT REQUIREMENTS, NON-FEDERAL ENTITIES THAT EXPEND FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE OF $750,000 OR MORE IN FEDERAL AWARDS WILL HAVE A SINGLE OR A PROGRAM-SPECIFIC AUDIT CONDUCTED FOR THAT YEAR. NON-FEDERAL ENTITIES THAT EXPEND LESS THAN $750,000 A YEAR IN FEDERAL AWARDS ARE EXEMPT FROM FEDERAL AUDIT REQUIREMENTS FOR THAT YEAR, EXCEPT AS NOTED IN 2 CFR 200.503. GRANTS AND COOPERATIVE AGREEMENTS ARE SUBJECT TO INSPECTIONS AND AUDITS BY THE COMPTROLLER GENERAL OF THE UNITED STATES, THE EPA OFFICE OF INSPECTOR GENERAL, OTHER EPA STAFF, OR ANY AUTHORIZED REPRESENTATIVE OF THE FEDERAL GOVERNMENT. REVIEWS BY THE EPA PROJECT OFFICER AND THE GRANTS SPECIALIST MAY OCCUR EACH YEAR.  [113] RECORDS   RECIPIENTS MUST KEEP FINANCIAL RECORDS, INCLUDING ALL DOCUMENTS SUPPORTING ENTRIES ON ACCOUNTING RECORDS AND TO SUBSTANTIATE CHANGES IN GRANTS AVAILABLE TO PERSONNEL AUTHORIZED TO EXAMINE EPA RECIPIENTS? GRANTS AND COOPERATIVE AGREEMENTS RECORDS. RECIPIENTS MUST MAINTAIN ALL RECORDS UNTIL 3 YEARS FROM THE DATE OF SUBMISSION OF FINAL EXPENDITURE REPORTS. IF QUESTIONS, SUCH AS THOSE RAISED AS A RESULT OF AUDITS REMAIN FOLLOWING THE 3-YEAR PERIOD, RECIPIENTS MUST RETAIN RECORDS UNTIL THE MATTER IS COMPLETELY RESOLVED.
Definition: TO IMPLEMENT HIGH PRIORITY ACTIVITIES, INCLUDING THE PROCESSING OF PERMITS, WHICH COMPLEMENT PROGRAMS UNDER ESTABLISHED ENVIRONMENTAL STATUTES.  THESE GRANTS ARE TO BE AWARDED TO STATES AND TERRITORIES TO ASSIST WITH THE IMPLEMENTATION OF ENVIRONMENTAL PROGRAMS, AND TO TRIBES ELIGIBLE UNDER CLEAN WATER ACT 518(E) TO ASSIST WITH THE IMPLEMENTATION OF ENVIRONMENTAL PROGRAMS.

Name: CATEGORICAL GRANT:  ENVIRONMENTAL INFORMATION
Title: 21 - CATEGORICAL GRANT:  ENVIRONMENTAL INFORMATION
Laws: ANNUAL APPROPRIATIONS ACT
Narrative: THE EXCHANGE NETWORK GRANT PROGRAM PROVIDES FUNDING TO ITS NETWORK PARTNERS, STATES, TERRITORIES AND FEDERALLY RECOGNIZED INDIAN TRIBES TO SUPPORT THE DEVELOPMENT OF THE ENVIRONMENTAL INFORMATION EXCHANGE NETWORK.  FUNDING IS PROVIDED FOR: INFRASTRUCTURE TO SUPPORT THE DEVELOPMENT OR UPGRADE OF INFORMATION MANAGEMENT AND TECHNOLOGY CAPABILITIES; DATA EXCHANGE ANALYSIS AND INTEGRATION; AND MENTORING, PLANNING, TRAINING AND COLLABORATION ON INNOVATIVE, MULTI-PARTNER PROJECTS.   THE NETWORK MAKES IT EASIER FOR THE EPA AND ITS PARTNERS TO OBTAIN THE TIMELY, ACCURATE INFORMATION THEY NEED TO MAKE DECISIONS ABOUT HUMAN HEALTH AND THE ENVIRONMENT WHILE LOWERING COST AND REDUCING THE BURDEN OF REPORTING.  THE EXCHANGE NETWORK GRANT PROGRAM IS PIVOTING TO THE SECOND PHASE OF THE NETWORK?S DEVELOPMENT. IN THIS PHASE, THE NETWORK COMMUNITY WILL: EXPAND NETWORK PUBLISHING (DATA OWNERS MAKING THEIR INFORMATION AVAILABLE TO OTHER PARTNERS ON THE NETWORK USING AUTOMATED SERVICES); DEVELOP APPLICATIONS (BOTH MOBILE AND DESKTOP) AND WEBSITES (INCLUDING PUBLIC SITES) THAT ANALYZE AND/OR DISPLAY DATA ACCESSED USING EXCHANGE NETWORK SERVICES; EXPAND THE USE OF THE NETWORK FOR DATA REPORTING TO ADDITIONAL EPA DATA SYSTEMS; EXPAND INTRA-PARTNER DATA SHARING FOR AGENCY PRIORITIES;      INTEGRATING GEOGRAPHIC/ GEOSPATIAL INFORMATION INTO THE NETWORK DATA EXCHANGES; AND DEVELOP NEW TECHNOLOGIES THAT MAKE USING THE NETWORK EASIER AND LESS COSTLY, MOST NOTABLY BY PROVISIONING SHARED NETWORK SERVICES HOSTED BY EPA.
Definition: ADVANCES THE EXCHANGE NETWORK BY PROVIDING FUNDING TO STATES, TRIBES, AND TERRITORIES TO DEVELOP AND DEPLOY TECHNOLOGIES BASED ON EXCHANGE NETWORK SPECIFICATIONS AND STANDARDS TO REPORT AND SHARE ENVIRONMENTAL DATA.

Name: CATEGORICAL GRANT:  BEACHES PROTECTION
Title: 23 - CATEGORICAL GRANT:  BEACHES PROTECTION
Laws: CLEAN WATER ACT SECTION 406
Narrative: AWARD GRANTS TO ELIGIBLE COASTAL AND GREAT LAKES STATES, TERRITORIES AND TRIBES TO CONDUCT WATER QUALITY MONITORING AT BEACHES AND TO NOTIFY THE PUBLIC OF RISK OF EXPOSURE TO PATHOGEN INDICATORS IN BEACH WATERS.  ENTITIES RECEIVE PROGRAM DEVELOPMENT OR IMPLEMENTATION GRANTS.  IMPLEMENTATION GRANTS TO ASSIST STATES OPERATE THEIR PROGRAMS ARE AWARDED IF THE ENTITY MEETS THE PERFORMANCE CRITERIA IN THE NATIONAL BEACH GUIDANCE AND REQUIRED PERFORMANCE CRITERIA FOR GRANTS.
Definition: GRANTS AUTHORIZED UNDER THE BEACH ACT TO SUPPORT CONTINUED DEVELOPMENT AND IMPLEMENTATION OF COASTAL AND GREAT LAKES RECREATIONAL WATER MONITORING AND PUBLIC NOTIFICATION PROGRAMS.

Name: CATEGORICAL GRANT:  BROWNFIELDS
Title: 24 - CATEGORICAL GRANT:  BROWNFIELDS
Laws: THE COMPREHENSIVE ENVIRONMENTAL RESPONSE, COMPENSATION, AND LIABILITIES ACT (CERCLA, 1980) AS AMENDED BY THE SMALL BUSINESS LIABILITY RELIEF AND BROWNFIELDS REVITALIZATION ACT (SBLRBRA)  (PUB. L. 107-118, 2002)GOVERNMENT MANAGEMENT REFORM ACT (1990)FEDERAL GRANT AND COOPERATIVE AGREEMENT ACT (1977)
Narrative: STATE RESPONSE PROGRAM: CERCLA SECTION 128(A) AUTHORIZES A $50 MILLION GRANT PROGRAM TO ESTABLISH AND ENHANCE STATE AND TRIBAL RESPONSE PROGRAMS TO CLEANUP AND REDEVELOP BROWNFIELD SITES.  STATES AND INDIAN TRIBES MAY USE SECTION 128(A) FUNDING TO, FOR EXAMPLE, DEVELOP LEGISLATION, REGULATIONS, PROCEDURES, GUIDANCE, ETC., THAT WOULD ESTABLISH OR ENHANCE THE ADMINISTRATIVE AND LEGAL STRUCTURE OF THEIR RESPONSE PROGRAMS.  IN ADDITION, STATES AND INDIAN TRIBES MAY USE GRANT FUNDING TO DEVELOP A PUBLIC RECORD, CAPITALIZE A REVOLVING LOAN FUND FOR BROWNFIELD?S CLEANUP UNDER CERCLA SECTION 104(K)(3), PURCHASE ENVIRONMENTAL INSURANCE, AND CONDUCT SITE-SPECIFIC RELATED ACTIVITIES SUCH AS ASSESSMENTS AT BROWNFIELD SITES (REGIONS).
Definition: BROWNFIELDS ARE DEFINED AS REAL PROPERTIES, THE EXPANSION, REDEVELOPMENT, OR REUSE OF WHICH MAY BE COMPLICATED BY THE PRESENCE OR POTENTIAL PRESENCE OF A HAZARDOUS SUBSTANCE, POLLUTANT, OR CONTAMINANT.  (THERE IS SOME STATUTORY EXCLUSION TO THIS DEFINITION).  THE BROWNFIELDS PROGRAM EMPOWERS STATES AND TRIBES TO WORK TO PREVENT, ASSESS, SAFELY CLEANUP, AND REUSE BROWNFIELDS.

Name: BASE REALIGNMENT AND CLOSURE (BRAC)
Title: 41 - BASE REALIGNMENT AND CLOSURE (BRAC)
Laws: COMPREHENSIVE ENVIRONMENTAL RESPONSE, COMPENSATION, AND LIABILITY ACT (CERCLA) OF 1980, AS AMENDED BY THE SUPERFUND AMENDMENTS AND REAUTHORIZATION ACT (SARA) OF 1986 RESOURCE CONSERVATION AND RECOVERY ACT (RCRA), AS AMENDED BY THE HAZARDOUS AND SOLID WASTE AMENDMENTS (HSWA) OF 1984 NATIONAL ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION ACT (NEPA) OF 1970 BASE REALIGNMENT AND CLOSURE ACTS (BRAC) OF 1988, 1991, 1993, AND 1995 COMMUNITY ENVIRONMENTAL RESPONSE FACILITATION ACT (CERFA) OF 1992
Narrative: THE BRAC PROGRAM SUPPORTS DOD IN CARRYING OUT THE ACCELERATED CLEANUP PROGRAM AT SPECIFIC BRAC I ? IV INSTALLATIONS WITH THE ULTIMATE GOAL OF PROVIDING FOR RAPID ECONOMIC CONVERSION AND REDEVELOPMENT. SPECIFICALLY, EPA WORKS WITH DOD AND THE STATES AS PART OF THE BASE CLOSURE TEAM (BCT) IN THE FOLLOWING WAYS: ACCELERATING THE IDENTIFICATION OF CLEAN PARCELS UNDER CERFA; PROMOTING COMMUNITY INVOLVEMENT IN RESTORATION AND REUSE DECISION MAKING; SUPPORTING UP-FRONT PLANNING AND SCOPING; PREPARING AND REVIEWING DOCUMENTS ON AN ACCELERATED BASIS (FOR EXAMPLE, CONCURRENT REVIEW OF DRAFT AND FINAL ENVIRONMENTAL BASELINES STUDIES DOCUMENTS, AND WORK WITH THE MILITARY COMPONENTS ON THE REMEDIAL INVESTIGATION/FEASIBILITY STUDY), REMEDIAL DESIGN, AND REMEDIAL ACTION STUDY AND SAMPLING DATA);EXPEDITING REVIEW OF ENVIRONMENTAL DOCUMENTATION RELATING TO DEEDS AND LEASES TO ACCELERATE ECONOMIC REVITALIZATION THROUGH REUSE; AND PROVIDING ACCELERATED CLEANUP AND/OR PROPERTY TRANSFER ASSISTANCE TO DOD (INCLUDING POST ADMINISTRATIVE ORDERS ON CONSENT PRIVATIZATION WORK).
Definition: BRAC IS AN EPA/DEPARTMENT OF DEFENSE (DOD) SUPPORTED REIMBURSABLE PROGRAM. DOD PROVIDES REIMBURSEMENT TO EPA ON AN ANNUAL BASIS TO FUND EPA WORK YEARS (FTES) FOR THE AGENCY'S PARTICIPATION AT SELECTED DOD BRAC I ? IV INSTALLATIONS. EPA'S ROLE IS TO ENSURE PROTECTION OF HUMAN HEALTH, EFFECTIVE PROGRAM IMPLEMENTATION, AND MEANINGFUL PUBLIC INVOLVEMENT WHILE FACILITATING RESTORATION AND REUSE OF DOD'S EXCESS PROPERTIES.

Name: BEACH / FISH PROGRAMS
Title: 42 - BEACH / FISH PROGRAMS
Laws: CLEAN WATER ACT SECTIONS: 104 (B) AND 101
Narrative: THE FISH PROGRAM SUPPORTS THE PROTECTION OF HUMAN HEALTH BY PROVIDING THE SCIENCE AND TOOLS STATES AND TRIBES NEED TO ISSUE CONSUMPTION ADVICE WHEN FISH ARE CAUGHT FOR RECREATION OR SUBSISTENCE.  HEADQUARTERS AND REGIONAL OFFICES PURSUE THE FOLLOWING ACTIVITIES TO SUPPORT THIS PROGRAM:  PUBLISH CRITERIA GUIDANCE THAT STATES AND TRIBES CAN USE TO ADOPT HEALTH-BASED WATER QUALITY STANDARDS, ASSESS THEIR WATERS, AND ESTABLISH PERMIT LIMITS.DEVELOP AND DISSEMINATE SOUND SCIENTIFIC RISK ASSESSMENT METHODOLOGIES AND GUIDANCE THAT STATES AND TRIBES CAN USE TO SAMPLE, ANALYZE, AND ASSESS FISH TISSUE IN SUPPORT OF WATER BODY-SPECIFIC OR REGIONAL CONSUMPTION ADVISORIES OR A DETERMINATION THAT NO CONSUMPTION ADVICE IS NECESSARY.DEVELOP AND DISSEMINATE GUIDANCE THAT STATES AND TRIBES CAN USE TO COMMUNICATE THE RISKS OF CONSUMING CHEMICALLY CONTAMINATED FISH.GATHER, ANALYZE, AND DISSEMINATE INFORMATION TO THE PUBLIC AND TO HEALTH PROFESSIONALS THAT ENABLE INFORMED DECISIONS ON WHEN, WHERE TO FISH, AND HOW TO PREPARE FISH CAUGHT FOR RECREATION AND SUBSISTENCE.  GATHER, ANALYZE, AND DISSEMINATE DATA ON CONTAMINATION IN FISH TISSUE TO PROVIDE AN UNDERSTANDING OF THE HUMAN HEALTH IMPLICATIONS OF FISH CONTAMINATION AND THE EFFECTIVENESS OF POLLUTANT CONTROL REGULATIONS.THE BEACH PROGRAM ALSO SUPPORTS THE PROTECTION OF HUMAN HEALTH BY REDUCING EXPOSURE TO CONTAMINATED WATER AT COASTAL AND GREAT LAKES BEACHES.  HEADQUARTERS AND REGIONAL OFFICE ACTIVITIES INCLUDE:FACILITATE STATE AND TRIBAL ADOPTION OF RECREATIONAL WATER QUALITY STANDARDS AND USE OF THOSE STANDARDS AS THE BASIS FOR MONITORING AND PUBLIC NOTIFICATION WHEN BACTERIAL CONTAMINATION POSES A RISK TO SWIMMERS.ISSUE GUIDANCE TO IMPROVE BEACH MONITORING AND PUBLIC NOTIFICATION PROGRAMS, INCLUDING EFFECTIVE STRATEGIES TO COMMUNICATE PUBLIC HEALTH RISKS TO SWIMMERS.DEVELOP AND DISSEMINATE SOUND SCIENTIFIC RISK ASSESSMENT METHODS AND CRITERIA FOR USE IN EVALUATING BEACH WATER QUALITY, PRIORITIZING BEACH WATERS FOR MONITORING, AND WARNING BEACH USERS OF HEALTH RISKS OR CLOSING THE BEACHES.PROMULGATE FEDERAL WATER QUALITY STANDARDS WHERE A STATE OR TRIBE FAILS TO ADOPT APPROPRIATE STANDARDS TO PROTECT COSTAL AND GREAT LAKES RECREATIONAL WATERS.PROVIDE PUBLICLY ACCESSIBLE INTERNET-BASED INFORMATION ABOUT LOCAL BEACH NOTIFICATION ACTIONS.
Definition: PROVIDE SOUND SCIENCE, GUIDANCE, TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE AND NATIONWIDE INFORMATION TO STATE, TRIBAL, AND FEDERAL AGENCIES ON THE HUMAN HEALTH RISKS ASSOCIATED WITH EATING LOCALLY CAUGHT FISH/SHELLFISH OR WILDLIFE WITH EXCESSIVE LEVELS OF CONTAMINANTS.  PROVIDE GUIDANCE, TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE AND NATIONWIDE INFORMATION TO SUPPORT STATE, TRIBAL, AND LOCAL GOVERNMENT BEACH MONITORING AND NOTIFICATION PROGRAMS.

Name: BROWNFIELDS
Title: 43 - BROWNFIELDS
Laws: COMPREHENSIVE ENVIRONMENTAL RESPONSE, COMPENSATION, AND LIABILITIES ACT (CERCLA) AS AMENDED BY THE SMALL BUSINESS LIABILITY RELIEF AND BROWNFIELDS REVITALIZATION ACT (PUB. L.107-118)SOLID WASTE DISPOSAL ACTCLEAN AIR ACT, SECTION 103CLEAN WATER ACT, SECTION 104 FEDERAL GRANT AND COOPERATIVE AGREEMENT ACTGOVERNMENT MANAGEMENT REFORM ACT (1990)ANNUAL APPROPRIATIONS ACT
Narrative: THE BROWNFIELDS PROGRAM FOSTERS FEDERAL, STATE, LOCAL, AND PUBLIC-PRIVATE PARTNERSHIPS TO RETURN PROPERTIES TO PRODUCTIVE ECONOMIC USE IN COMMUNITIES.  THIS APPROACH EMPHASIZES ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH AND PROTECTION THAT ALSO ACHIEVES ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT AND JOB CREATION THROUGH THE REDEVELOPMENT OF BROWNFIELDS PROPERTIES, PARTICULARLY IN UNDERSERVED AND DISADVANTAGED COMMUNITIES. AS PART OF THE AMERICA?S GREAT OUTDOOR INITIATIVE, EPA IS PARTICIPATING ON INTERAGENCY TEAMS IN THE DEVELOPMENT OF A BROAD RANGE OF POLICY OPTIONS TO BETTER ALIGN AND LEVERAGE FEDERAL PROGRAMS AND INVESTMENTS, MAKE REGULATORY AND VOLUNTARY EFFORTS MORE COMPLEMENTARY, AND INCREASE THE EFFICIENCY AND EFFECTIVENESS OF PROGRAMS TO CONNECT AMERICANS WITH THE GREAT OUTDOORS.  EPA IS LEADING TEAMS FOCUSED ON PROMOTING OUTDOOR RECREATION ON PUBLIC AND PRIVATE LANDS IN URBAN PARKS, GREENWAYS, BEACHES, TRAILS, AND WATERWAYS, AND EDUCATING AND ENGAGING AMERICANS IN OUR NATURAL, CULTURAL, AND HISTORICAL RESOURCES.IN ADDITION TO SUPPORTING THE OPERATIONS AND MANAGEMENT OF THE BROWNFIELDS PROGRAM, FUNDS WILL PROVIDE FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE FOR TRAINING ON HAZARDOUS WASTE TO ORGANIZATIONS REPRESENTING THE INTERESTS OF STATE AND TRIBAL CO-IMPLEMENTERS OF THE SMALL BUSINESS LIABILITY RELIEF AND BROWNFIELDS REVITALIZATION ACT (SBLRBRA), OTHERWISE KNOWN AS THE 2002 BROWNFIELDS AMENDMENTS.  THE PROGRAM ALSO OFFERS OUTREACH SUPPORT FOR THE ADMINISTRATOR?S PRIORITY OF PROMOTING ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE ISSUES AFFECTING TRIBAL AND NATIVE ALASKAN VILLAGES OR OTHER DISADVANTAGED COMMUNITIES THAT NEED TO ADDRESS PERCEIVED OR REAL HAZARDOUS SUBSTANCE CONTAMINATION AT SITES IN THEIR NEIGHBORHOOD OR COMMUNITY. EPA BROWNFIELDS GRANTS ARE IN THE FORM OF COOPERATIVE AGREEMENTS, AND REQUIRE CONSIDERABLE AGENCY STAFF INVOLVEMENT TO ENSURE THAT SITES ARE PROPERLY ASSESSED AND CLEANED UP CONSISTENT WITH THE APPLICABLE REQUIREMENTS (E.G., VOLUNTARY CLEANUP PROGRAM (VCP)).  CURRENT AGENCY GUIDELINES RECOMMEND AN AVERAGE OF NO MORE THAN 11 COOPERATIVE AGREEMENTS PER PROJECT OFFICER.  DESPITE WORKLOAD INCREASES IN MANY AREAS OF THE COUNTRY (INCLUDING AREAS WITH MANY DISADVANTAGED COMMUNITIES AND ?CITIES IN TRANSITION?), THE AVERAGE PROJECT OFFICER WORKS ON AS MANY AS 30 GRANTS.  THIS GREATLY COMPROMISES THE ABILITY TO EFFECTIVELY AND EFFICIENTLY MANAGE THESE GRANTS FOR THE BENEFIT OF THE AFFECTED COMMUNITIES.SINCE EPA?S BROWNFIELDS PROGRAM MANAGES A SIGNIFICANT WORKLOAD OF ASSESSMENT, CLEANUP, REVOLVING LOAN FUND (RLF), JOB TRAINING AND AREA-WIDE PLANNING COOPERATIVE AGREEMENTS, FTE HELP PROVIDE NEEDED SUPPORT IN THE PLANNING, EXPEDITIOUS AWARD, AND PERFORMANCE MANAGEMENT OF BROWNFIELDS COOPERATIVE AGREEMENTS.    THE FTE WILL ALSO BE USED FOR PROJECT OFFICERS WHO WILL MORE EFFECTIVELY AND EFFICIENTLY NEGOTIATE AND AWARD COOPERATIVE AGREEMENTS AS PART OF CURRENT WORKLOAD AS WELL AS MANAGE THE AGREEMENTS THROUGHOUT THEIR FULL LIFE-CYCLE, PROVIDING THE NECESSARY TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE THE RECIPIENT COMMUNITIES NEED THROUGHOUT THE IMPLEMENTATION OF THE PROJECT TO ENSURE SUCCESSFUL OUTCOMES.  AS PROJECT OFFICERS, THESE FTE WILL ALSO FACILITATE INITIAL COORDINATION WITHIN EPA AND WITH OTHER AGENCIES IN TWO WAYS:1) FACILITATE INITIAL COORDINATION WITH EPA ENFORCEMENT, AIR AND WATER QUALITY PROGRAMS (AS APPROPRIATE) TO TARGET ENVIRONMENTAL IMPROVEMENTS IDENTIFIED DURING THE AREA-WIDE PLANNING PROCESS.  THROUGH AREA-WIDE PLANNING, LOCAL COMMUNITIES WILL BE ABLE TO ASSESS AND ADDRESS A SINGLE LARGE OR MULTIPLE BROWNFIELD SITES WITHIN THEIR BOUNDARIES, THEREBY ADVANCING AREA-WIDE PLANNING TO ENABLE REDEVELOPMENT ON A BROADER SCALE. FOR EXAMPLE, THESE IMPROVEMENTS COULD COME FROM AIR QUALITY OR WATER INFRASTRUCTURE INVESTMENTS -- PLANNED OR UNDERWAY -- WITHIN OR NEAR THE PILOT PROJECT AREA, OR FROM A SUPPLEMENTAL ENVIRONMENTAL PROJECT IDENTIFIED BY EPA'S ENFORCEMENT OFFICE (IF INFORMATION IS PUBLICLY AVAILABLE).  THE FTE ALSO WILL CONSULT AIR AND WATER MEDIA OFFICES AS NEEDED TO ADVISE ON DEVELOPMENT TECHNIQUES THAT IMPROVE ENVIRONMENTAL OUTCOMES, SUCH AS APPROACHES WHICH REDUCE AIR EMISSIONS (CO2, NOX, HC, CO), ENERGY USE FROM VEHICULAR ENERGY CONSUMPTION (E.G., REDUCE VEHICLE MILES TRAVELED), LAND CONSUMPTION, STORMWATER RUN-OFF, AND POLLUTANT LOADINGS.  2) WORK WITH OTHER FEDERAL, STATE AND/OR TRIBAL AGENCIES (AS APPROPRIATE) IN AN EFFORT TO PROVIDE ADDITIONAL INFORMATION IN SUPPORT OF DEVELOPING THE AREA-WIDE PLAN FOR THE BROWNFIELDS-IMPACTED AREA, SUCH AS PLANNED NEIGHBORHOOD INVESTMENTS OR SERVICES NEEDED. BY IDENTIFYING OPPORTUNITIES FOR CROSS-PROGRAM COORDINATION AND POSSIBLE INTEGRATION, EPA WILL BE ABLE TO DELIVER MORE COMPREHENSIVE TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE TO THE PILOT COMMUNITIES.   THE NATIONAL BROWNFIELDS CONFERENCE IS THE LARGEST AND MOST COMPREHENSIVE CONFERENCE IN THE NATION FOCUSED ON ENVIRONMENTAL REVITALIZATION AND ECONOMIC REDEVELOPMENT ISSUES.  DUE TO INCREASED CONTRIBUTIONS AND SUPPORT FROM EXTERNAL PARTNERS, IN FY 2013, THE EPA PLANS TO DISTRIBUTE A LARGER PORTION OF THE TOTAL COST OF PLANNING AND DELIVERING THE BROWNFIELDS CONFERENCE TO CONFERENCE ATTENDEES BY CHARGING A REGISTRATION FEE FOR THIS CONFERENCE.EPA WILL PROVIDE TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE TO COMMUNITIES THAT WERE AWARDED FUNDING TO COMBINE GROWTH POLICIES WITH BROWNFIELDS REDEVELOPMENT.  EPA ALSO WILL CONDUCT FURTHER RESEARCH ON INCENTIVES FOR CLEANUP THAT ENCOURAGE BROWNFIELDS REDEVELOPMENT, PILOT ADDITIONAL TECHNIQUES TO ACCOMPLISH REDEVELOPMENT WITHIN COMMUNITIES, IDENTIFY NEW POLICY AND RESEARCH NEEDS, AND HIGHLIGHT BEST PRACTICES THAT CAN BE COPIED IN OTHER COMMUNITIES.IN FY 2013, EPA?S BROWNFIELDS PROGRAM REQUEST INCLUDES NEARLY $1.3 MILLION FOR THE GROWTH PROGRAM.  THE GROWTH PROGRAM ADDRESSES CRITICAL ISSUES FOR BROWNFIELDS REDEVELOPMENT, INCLUDING LAND ASSEMBLY, DEVELOPMENT PERMITTING ISSUES, FINANCING, PARKING AND STREET STANDARDS, ACCOUNTABILITY TO UNIFORM SYSTEMS OF INFORMATION FOR LAND USE CONTROLS, AND OTHER FACTORS THAT INFLUENCE THE ECONOMIC VIABILITY OF BROWNFIELDS REDEVELOPMENT.  THE BEST PRACTICES, TOOLS, AND LESSONS LEARNED FROM THE GROWTH PROGRAM WILL DIRECTLY INFORM AND ASSIST EPA?S EFFORTS TO INCREASE AREA-WIDE PLANNING FOR ASSESSMENT, CLEANUP, AND REDEVELOPMENT OF BROWNFIELDS SITES.IN FY 2013, EPA IS REQUESTING $371 THOUSAND FOR EPA?S ENFORCEMENT PROGRAM.  EPA?S ENFORCEMENT PROGRAM WILL WORK COLLABORATIVELY WITH OUR PARTNERS ON INNOVATIVE APPROACHES TO HELP ACHIEVE THE AGENCY?S LAND REUSE PRIORITIES.  EPA?S ENFORCEMENT PROGRAM WILL DEVELOP GUIDANCE AND TOOLS TO PROVIDE GREATER CERTAINTY AND COMFORT REGARDING POTENTIAL LIABILITY CONCERNS FOR PARTIES SEEKING TO REUSE THESE PROPERTIES.
Definition: THE BROWNFIELDS PROGRAM IS DESIGNED TO HELP STATES, TRIBES, LOCAL COMMUNITIES, AND OTHER STAKEHOLDERS INVOLVED IN ENVIRONMENTAL REVITALIZATION AND ECONOMIC REDEVELOPMENT TO WORK TOGETHER TO PLAN, INVENTORY, ASSESS, SAFELY CLEANUP, AND REUSE BROWNFIELDS.  BROWNFIELD SITES ARE REAL PROPERTY, THE EXPANSION, REDEVELOPMENT, OR REUSE OF WHICH MAY BE COMPLICATED BY THE PRESENCE OR POTENTIAL PRESENCE OF A HAZARDOUS SUBSTANCE, POLLUTANT, OR CONTAMINANT.  REVITALIZING THESE ONCE PRODUCTIVE PROPERTIES HELPS COMMUNITIES BY REMOVING BLIGHT, SATISFYING THE GROWING DEMAND FOR LAND, HELPING TO LIMIT URBAN SPRAWL, FOSTERING ECOLOGIC HABITAT ENHANCEMENTS, ENABLING ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT, AND MAINTAINING OR IMPROVING QUALITY OF LIFE.  THIS PROGRAM COMPRISES THE ADMINISTRATIVE COMPONENT OF THE BROWNFIELDS PROGRAM, SUPPORTING HUMAN RESOURCES, TRAVEL, TRAINING, TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE, AND RESEARCH ACTIVITIES. THE BROWNFIELDS PROGRAM ALSO INCLUDES GROWTH AND SUSTAINABLE DESIGN THAT ADDRESS BROWNFIELD ISSUES. THE GROWTH ACTIVITIES INCLUDE: (1) WORKING WITH STATE AND LOCAL GOVERNMENTS AND OTHER STAKEHOLDERS TO CREATE AN IMPROVED ECONOMIC AND INSTITUTIONAL CLIMATE FOR BROWNFIELDS REDEVELOPMENT; (2) REMOVING BARRIERS AND CREATING INCENTIVES FOR BROWNFIELDS REDEVELOPMENT BY CHANGING STANDARDS THAT AFFECT THE VIABILITY OF BROWNFIELDS REDEVELOPMENT; AND (3) CREATING CROSS-CUTTING SOLUTIONS THAT IMPROVE THE ECONOMIC, REGULATORY, AND INSTITUTIONAL CLIMATE FOR BROWNFIELDS REDEVELOPMENT

Name: CIVIL ENFORCEMENT
Title: 44 - CIVIL ENFORCEMENT
Laws: RESOURCE CONSERVATION AND RECOVERY ACT AS AMENDED, 1984SUPERFUND AMENDMENTS AND REAUTHORIZATION ACT, 1986 OIL POLLUTION ACT, 1990SAFE DRINKING WATER ACT AS AMENDED, 1996 CLEAN AIR ACT AND AMENDMENTS, 1990CLEAN WATER ACT AND AMENDMENTS, 1972TOXIC SUBSTANCES CONTROL ACT, 1976FEDERAL INSECTICIDE, FUNGICIDE AND RODENTICIDE ACT, 1996 FOOD QUALITY PROTECTION ACT, 2005EMERGENCY PLANNING AND RIGHT TO KNOW ACT, 1986POLLUTION PROSECUTION ACT, 1990 OCEAN DUMPING ACT, 1972OIL POLLUTION ACT, 1990LEAD RENOVATION, REPAIR, AND PAINTING RULE, 2010ENERGY INDEPENDENCE AND SECURITY ACT, 2007ENERGY POLICY ACT, 2005
Narrative: EPMPROVIDE DIRECTION TO, AND SET GOALS AND PRIORITIES FOR, THE NATIONAL ENFORCEMENT PROGRAM.  UNDER THIS PROGRAM/PROJECT, HEADQUARTERS DEVELOPS NATIONAL COMPLIANCE AND ENFORCEMENT MANDATES AND POLICIES TO SUSTAIN FAIR AND CONSISTENT ENFORCEMENT. (HQ)DEVELOP GUIDANCE AND POLICY FOR TECHNICAL EVALUATIONS, INVESTIGATIONS, AND CASE DEVELOPMENT STRATEGIES WHICH MAY INCLUDE DEVELOPMENT OF AGENCY POSITION IN TERMS OF INJUNCTIVE RELIEF, SUPPLEMENTAL ENVIRONMENTAL PROJECTS (SEPS) AND APPROPRIATE CIVIL PENALTIES. (HQ, RT)COORDINATE WITH MEDIA PROGRAM OFFICES TO REVIEW MEDIA PROGRAM POLICIES, GUIDANCE, REGULATIONS, AND PERMITS FOR ENFORCEABILITY. (HQ, RT)DEVELOP, ANALYZE, COORDINATE, AND COMMENT ON, AS APPROPRIATE, LEGISLATIVE PROPOSALS, CONGRESSIONAL TESTIMONY, COMMENTS ON STATEMENTS OF AGENCY OR ADMINISTRATION POSITION, AND OTHER LEGISLATIVE MATTERS TO ENSURE THAT ENFORCEMENT CONCERNS ARE ADDRESSED. (HQ)ESTABLISH GOALS AND PRIORITIES FOR THE REGIONAL ENFORCEMENT PROGRAM CONSISTENT WITH NATIONAL COMPLIANCE AND ENFORCEMENT GUIDANCE.  (RT)ENHANCE ENFORCEMENT, COMPLIANCE, AND CAPACITY BUILDING EFFORTS WITH MEXICO AND CANADA RELATING TO TRANS-BOUNDARY COMPLIANCE MONITORING ON THE U.S. BORDERS FOR HAZARDOUS WASTE, CFCS, SELECTED CHEMICALS (E.G, PCBS, MERCURY), AND OTHER REGULATED SUBSTANCES (HQ, RT)PROMOTE INTERNATIONAL ENVIRONMENTAL ENFORCEMENT THROUGH PARTICIPATION IN RELEVANT ORGANIZATIONS AND NETWORKS, SUCH AS THE ENFORCEMENT WORKING GROUP OF THE NORTH AMERICAN COMMISSION FOR ENVIRONMENTAL COOPERATION (CEC) AND THE INTERNATIONAL NETWORKS FOR ENVIRONMENTAL COMPLIANCE AND ENFORCEMENT (INECE), INCLUDING ITS SEAPORT ENVIRONMENTAL SECURITY NETWORK. (HQ, RT)USE APPROPRIATE ENFORCEMENT TOOLS TO IDENTIFY AND REDUCE SIGNIFICANT NONCOMPLIANCE IN HIGH PRIORITY AREAS, AND TO MAINTAIN A STRONG BASE PROGRAM. (HQ, RT)CONDUCT EVALUATIONS OR REVIEWS OF STATES TO ENSURE ADEQUATE ENFORCEMENT PROGRAMS WHERE SUCH PROGRAMS HAVE BEEN DELEGATED OR AUTHORIZED. (HQ, RT)PREPARE WRITTEN INFORMATION REQUEST TO OBTAIN INFORMATION REGARDING COMPLIANCE STATUS OF REGULATED ENTITIES.  DEVELOP EVIDENCE DOCUMENTING NON-COMPLIANCE AND/OR A PLAN FOR OBTAINING SUCH EVIDENCE.  EVIDENCE IS REVIEWED AND ANALYZED, CIVIL JUDICIAL CASES ARE PREPARED FOR REFERRAL TO DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE FOR JUDICIAL LITIGATION OR FOR ADMINISTRATIVE LITIGATION BY HQ AND REGIONAL OFFICES. (HQ, RT)PROVIDE EXPERT ADVICE AND LEGAL COUNSEL ON NATIONALLY SIGNIFICANT ENFORCEMENT LITIGATION AND INITIATE INVESTIGATIONS AGAINST VIOLATORS OPERATING NATIONALLY. (HQ, RT)PROVIDE EXPERT, TECHNICAL, AND LEGAL SUPPORT TO THE AGENCY'S CIVIL JUDICIAL, ADMINISTRATIVE, CRIMINAL, AND FEDERAL FACILITIES ENFORCEMENT PROGRAMS. (HQ, RT)TRACK COMPLIANCE WITH JUDICIAL CONSENT DECREES AND ADMINISTRATIVE ORDERS, AND BRING APPROPRIATE ACTION IN RESPONSE TO VIOLATIONS.  (HQ, RT)PROVIDE TRAINING AND ASSISTANCE TO REGIONS AND STATES IN THE CALCULATION OF ECONOMIC BENEFIT GAINED BY VIOLATORS.  ECONOMIC BENEFIT INFORMATION IS USED TO HELP DETERMINE SETTLEMENTS AND/OR PENALTIES. (HQ, RT)REVIEW ROUTINE AND REQUESTED DOCUMENTS RECEIVED FROM REGULATED ENTITIES, ENSURING COMPLIANCE WITH ENFORCEMENT DOCUMENTS, AND TAKING APPROPRIATE ENFORCEMENT WHEN DELEGATED/AUTHORIZED STATES ARE UNABLE OR UNWILLING TO DO SO.  (HQ, RT)SUPPORT STATES IN TRAINING AND PROVIDE ASSISTANCE TO SMALL AND MEDIUM-SIZED BUSINESSES. (HQ, RT)COLLECT, MAINTAIN, AND ANALYZE ENFORCEMENT INFORMATION TO SUPPORT EFFECTIVE TARGETING OF EPA, STATE, AND TRIBAL ENFORCEMENT RESOURCES ON IDENTIFYING AND ADDRESSING AREAS OF MOST ENVIRONMENTAL CONCERN., (HQ)SUPPORT THE DESIGN AND IMPLEMENTATION OF ELECTRONIC FILING AND RECORD KEEPING SYSTEMS, THEREBY INCREASING INCENTIVES FOR REGULATED ENTITIES TO COMPLY. (HQ)DEVELOP VIRTUAL AND ON-LINE COMPLIANCE ASSISTANCE TOOLS.SUPPORT ENVIRONMENTAL COMPLIANCE ASSISTANCE CENTERS.  THESE CENTERS OPERATE IN PARTNERSHIP WITH INDUSTRY ASSOCIATIONS, ENVIRONMENTAL GROUPS, UNIVERSITIES, AND OTHER GOVERNMENT AGENCIES PROVIDING ONE-STOP SHOPPING FOR REGULATORY AND TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE, POLLUTION PREVENTION ACTIVITIES, AND OTHER INFORMATION PARTICULARLY SUITED TO SPECIFIC INDUSTRIAL OR GOVERNMENT SECTORS. (HQ)WORK WITH FEDERALLY-RECOGNIZED INDIAN TRIBES ON A "GOVERNMENT TO GOVERNMENT" BASIS TO IDENTIFY CAPACITY BUILDING ISSUES AFFECTING TRIBAL LANDS.  THE GOAL IS TO HELP TRIBES DEVELOP THEIR OWN PROGRAMS SO THAT THEY CAN ASSUME GREATER MANAGEMENT OF ENVIRONMENTAL PROGRAMS IN INDIAN COUNTRY. (HQ, RT)ASSIST FEDERAL FACILITIES IN COMPLYING WITH LEGAL, REGULATORY AND EXECUTIVE ORDER ENVIRONMENTAL REQUIREMENTS, AND ENFORCE WHERE VIOLATIONS AT FEDERAL FACILITIES RE NOT ADDRESSED.THE AGENCY IS WORKING TO REDUCE ITS CARBON FOOTPRINT BY PROMOTING GREEN TRAVEL PRACTICES AND MOVING ROUTINE MEETINGS TO A WEB OR VIDEO CONFERENCE FORMAT. IN ORDER TO BE SUCCESSFUL, STRATEGIC INVESTMENTS IN VIDEO/WEB CONFERENCING CAPABILITIES ARE NECESSARY TO CREATE MULTI-USE CONFERENCE ROOMS IN SELECTED LOCATIONS, AS WELL AS THE NEEDED INTERNET CAPACITY.LUSTDEVELOP REGULATIONS AND POLICIES.OIL SPILLS RESPONSESUPPORT CLEAN WATER ACT SECTION 311 (OIL SPILL AND HAZARDOUS SUBSTANCES) POLICY DEVELOPMENT AND CASE DEVELOPMENT AND REVIEW. (HQ, RT)SUPPORT FIELD INVESTIGATIONS AND INSPECTIONS FOR SPILLS AND SPILL CONTROL COUNTERMEASURE (SPCC) COMPLIANCE. (HQ, RT)
Definition: EPA?S CIVIL ENFORCEMENT PROGRAM'S OVERARCHING GOAL IS TO PROTECT HUMAN HEALTH AND THE ENVIRONMENT BY TARGETING ENFORCEMENT ACTIONS ACCORDING TO THE DEGREE OF HEALTH AND ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACTS DUE TO NONCOMPLIANCE WITH FEDERAL ENVIRONMENTAL STATUTES AND REGULATIONS.  THESE ACTIVITIES INCLUDE DATA GATHERING AND ANALYSIS OF INFORMATION TO DETERMINE HIGH PRIORITY NONCOMPLIANCE OR ENVIRONMENTAL PROBLEMS ON WHICH TO FOCUS FEDERAL, STATE, TRIBAL OR LOCAL ATTENTION; INSPECTIONS; ENFORCEMENT OR COMPLIANCE ASSISTANCE.  THE PROGRAM COLLABORATES WITH THE DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE AND STATES, LOCAL AGENCIES AND TRIBAL GOVERNMENTS TO ENSURE CONSISTENT AND FAIR ENFORCEMENT OF ALL ENVIRONMENTAL LAWS AND REGULATIONS.  THE PROGRAM SEEKS TO AGGRESSIVELY PURSUE VIOLATIONS THAT THREATEN COMMUNITIES, ENSURE A LEVEL ECONOMIC PLAYING FIELD BY ENSURING THAT VIOLATORS DO NOT REALIZE ECONOMIC BENEFITS FROM NONCOMPLIANCE AND DETER FUTURE VIOLATIONS AND OVERSEES AUTHORIZED OR DELEGATED PROGRAMS.  THE CIVIL ENFORCEMENT PROGRAM DEVELOPS, LITIGATES AND SETTLES ADMINISTRATIVE AND CIVIL JUDICIAL CASES AGAINST SERIOUS VIOLATORS OF ENVIRONMENTAL LAWS.

Title: 45 - CLEAN AIR ALLOWANCE TRADING PROGRAMS

Narrative: FUNDS UNDER THIS PROGRAM PROJECT MAY BE USED FOR SEE SUPPORT.EPM - HQPROVIDE ADVICE AND COUNSEL TO THE ADMINISTRATOR ON EFFICIENT AND EFFECTIVE WAYS TO MEET AIR HEALTH STANDARDS, REDUCE PUBLIC EXPOSURE TO AIR TOXICS AND PROTECT THE ENVIRONMENT.PROVIDE ADVICE AND COUNSEL TO THE ADMINISTRATOR ON THE ACID RAIN PROGRAM, CLEAN AIR ALLOWANCE TRADING PROGRAMS, AND IMPACTS OF AIR POLLUTION TRANSPORT.PROVIDE POLICY, PROGRAM, AND MANAGEMENT GUIDANCE TO THE OFFICE OF AIR AND RADIATION PROGRAM, STAFF, AND REGIONAL OFFICES ON THE ACID RAIN PROGRAM, CLEAN AIR ALLOWANCE TRADING PROGRAMS, AND IMPACTS OF AIR POLLUTION TRANSPORT.PROVIDE ANALYSIS, PLANNING, BUDGETING, AND MANAGEMENT CAPABILITY TO ENSURE ANALYTIC SUPPORT TO REGIONAL, STATE, AND TRIBAL PROGRAMS FOR THE ACID RAIN PROGRAM, CLEAN AIR ALLOWANCE TRADING PROGRAMS, AND IMPACTS OF AIR POLLUTION TRANSPORT.ASSESS THE HEALTH AND ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACTS OF EMISSION REDUCTIONS.ESTABLISH ALLOWANCE TRADING PROGRAMS IN THE FUTURE.OPERATE ALLOWANCE TRACKING SYSTEMS TO RECORD TRANSFERS OF EMISSION ALLOWANCES BETWEEN DIFFERENT PARTIES.RECONCILE EMISSIONS AND ALLOWANCES AT EACH ELECTRIC UTILITY UNIT TO ENSURE COMPLIANCE.PROCESSES PERMIT ACTIONS.EPM  (RT)WORK WITH STATES AND TRIBES AND AFFECTED SOURCES TO MEET NAAQS, REDUCE ACID RAIN AND TOXICS DEPOSITION, AND REDUCE REGIONAL HAZE. WORK WITH STATES AND TRIBES TO ASSESS THE HEALTH AND ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACTS OF EMISSION REDUCTIONS.PROVIDE POLICY AND PROGRAM INPUT TO THE OFFICE OF AIR AND RADIATION.S&T  (HQ)TRACK TRENDS IN DRY ACID DEPOSITION FROM THE CLEAN AIR STATUS AND TRENDS NETWORK (CASTNET).SUPPORT THE TEMPORALLY INTEGRATED MONITORING OF ECOSYSTEMS (TIME) PROGRAM AND THE LONG-TERM MONITORING (LTM) PROGRAM.  THESE PROGRAMS WERE SPECIFICALLY DESIGNED TO ASSESS WHETHER THE 1990 CLEAN AIR ACT AMENDMENTS HAVE BEEN EFFECTIVE IN REDUCING THE ACIDITY OF SURFACE WATERS IN SENSITIVE AREAS.PROVIDE ANALYSIS AND RECOMMENDATIONS FOR ALLOWANCE TRADING PROGRAMS TO SUPPORT AGENCY ACTIVITIES.
Definition: THIS PROGRAM INCLUDES DEVELOPMENT, IMPLEMENTATION, AND EVALUATION OF FEDERALLY ADMINISTERED PROGRAMS FOR THE TRADING OF EMISSIONS ALLOWANCES.  THE TRADING PROGRAMS HELP IMPLEMENT NATIONAL AMBIENT AIR QUALITY STANDARDS (NAAQS) AND REDUCE ACID DEPOSITION, TOXICS DEPOSITION AND REGIONAL HAZE.  POLLUTANTS INCLUDE:  SULFUR DIOXIDE (SO2) AND NITROGEN OXIDES (NOX).  PROGRAMS INCLUDE:  THE ACID RAIN PROGRAM ESTABLISHED UNDER TITLE IV OF THE CLEAN AIR ACT AMENDMENTS, THE NOX TRADING PROGRAM ESTABLISHED BY NORTHEASTERN STATES AND ADMINISTERED BY EPA, AND THE SEASONAL CAIR NOX PROGRAM FOR CONTROL OF TRANSPORTED OZONE POLLUTION AND NOX EMISSIONS.

Narrative: EPMCONTINUE TO IMPLEMENT THE GREENHOUSE GAS REPORTING PROGRAM.RECRUIT PARTNERS AND IMPLEMENT THE ENERGY STAR PROGRAM TO ENCOURAGE THE PROFITABLE USE OF ENERGY EFFICIENT HEATING, VENTILATION, AIR CONDITIONING AND TRANSFORMERS. PROMOTE ENERGY STAR COMMERCIAL AND RESIDENTIAL TECHNOLOGIES, INCLUDING FAX MACHINES, COPIERS, HEATING AND COOLING EQUIPMENT, LIGHTING FIXTURES, ETC.DEVELOP ADDITIONAL RESIDENTIAL AND COMMERCIAL ENERGY STAR PRODUCTS IN CONJUNCTION WITH DOE.IMPLEMENT INITIATIVES TO REDUCE METHANE EMISSIONS COST-EFFECTIVELY.EXPAND COOPERATIVE, NON-REGULATORY PROGRAMS TO PROFITABLY CAPTURE AND USE METHANE AND EMISSIONS OF OTHER POTENT GREENHOUSE GASES BY PROVIDING TECHNICAL SUPPORT, REMOVING INSTITUTIONAL BARRIERS SUCH AS PROPERTY RIGHTS ISSUES, FAIR PRICING FROM UTILITIES, AND RECRUITING PARTNERS FOR THESE VOLUNTARY PROGRAMS.IMPLEMENT AND EXPAND ENVIRONMENTAL STEWARDSHIP PROGRAMS TO REDUCE HFC AND PFC EMISSIONS.REPORT REGIONAL IMPACTS OF CLIMATE CHANGE ON THE U.S.ANALYZE TECHNOLOGY POLICY OPTIONS FOR DEVELOPING COUNTRIES.STRENGTHEN THE INSTITUTIONAL CAPACITY OF DEVELOPING COUNTRIESWORK WITH STATE AND REGIONAL CONSTITUENCIES TO ASSESS ECONOMIC AND ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACTS, DEVELOP STRATEGIES AND IMPLEMENT TECHNOLOGY-BASED OPTIONS THAT REDUCE GREENHOUSE GAS EMISSIONS. ASSESS GREENHOUSE GAS IMPLICATIONS OF MAJOR SECTOR-BASED POLICIES AND DEVELOP STRATEGIES.WORK WITH COMPANIES AND INDUSTRIES TO DEVELOP ACTIONS IN THE PUBLIC AND PRIVATE SECTORS THAT LEAD TO IMPROVED ENERGY USE AND REDUCTIONS IN GREENHOUSE GAS EMISSIONS.WORK WITH STATE AND LOCAL DECISION-MAKERS TO ENCOURAGE TRANSPORTATION IMPROVEMENTS AND REDUCE THE GROWTH OF VEHICLE TRAVEL THROUGH VOLUNTARY PROGRAMS SUCH AS SMARTWAY PROGRAMS.PROVIDE POLICY AND PROGRAM INPUT TO THE OFFICE OF AIR AND RADIATION.FUNDS UNDER THIS PROGRAM PROJECT MAY BE USED FOR SEE SUPPORT. IN ORDER TO BE SUCCESSFUL, STRATEGIC INVESTMENTS IN VIDEO/WEB CONFERENCING CAPABILITIES ARE NECESSARY TO CREATE MULTI-USE CONFERENCE ROOMS IN SELECTED LOCATIONS, AS WELL AS THE NEEDED INTERNET CAPACITY.OATHE WORK PERFORMED BY THE HQ OFFICE OF THE ADMINISTRATOR IS AN ENABLING AND SUPPORT FUNCTION THAT SUPPORTS THE OUTCOMES OF THE AGENCY?S STRATEGIC GOALS AND THE FOLLOWING CROSS-GOAL STRATEGIES:  STRATEGY 1:  EXPANDING THE CONVERSATION ON ENVIRONMENTALISM; STRATEGY 2: WORKING FOR ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE AND CHILDREN?S HEALTH; STRATEGY 3: ADVANCING SCIENCE, RESEARCH, AND TECHNOLOGICAL INNOVATION; AND STRATEGY 4:  STRENGTHENING STATE, TRIBAL, AND INTERNATIONAL PARTNERSHIPS.
Definition: THIS PROGRAM INCLUDES VOLUNTARY DOMESTIC AND INTERNATIONAL PROGRAMS THAT ADDRESS GREENHOUSE GASES (GHG) AND CLIMATE CHANGE ISSUES. EFFORTS ARE AIMED AT REDUCING EMISSIONS OF GREENHOUSE GASES AND MITIGATING THE EFFECTS OF GLOBAL CLIMATE CHANGE ON THE ENVIRONMENT AND HUMAN HEALTH WHILE GROWING THE ECONOMY. IN ADDITION, THIS PROGRAM PROVIDES TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE AND ONLINE REPORTING TOOLS FOR REGULATED FACILITIES TO REPORT ANNUAL GREENHOUSE GAS EMISSIONS IN SUPPORT OF THE GREENHOUSE GAS REPORTING PROGRAM.

Title: 50 - COMPLIANCE MONITORING
Laws: RESOURCE CONSERVATION AND RECOVERY ACT AS AMENDED IN 1984OIL POLLUTION ACT, 1990SAFE DRINKING WATER ACT AS AMENDED, 1986CLEAN AIR ACT AND AMENDMENTS, 1990CLEAN WATER ACT AND AMENDMENTS, 1972TOXIC SUBSTANCES CONTROL ACT, 1976FEDERAL INSECTICIDE, FUNGICIDE AND RODENTICIDE ACT, 1996FOOD QUALITY PROTECTION ACT, EMERGENCY PLANNING AND RIGHT TO KNOW ACT, 1986OCEAN DUMPING ACT, 1972OIL POLLUTION ACT, 1990LEAD RENOVATION, REPAIR AND PAINTING RULE, 2010ENERGY POLICY ACT OF 2005
Narrative: THIS PROGRAM/PROJECT ENSURES COMPLIANCE WITH THE ENVIRONMENTAL STATUTES ENACTED BY CONGRESS THROUGH COMPLIANCE MONITORING OF THE REGULATED COMMUNITY, AND CIVIL AND CRIMINAL INVESTIGATIONS. THE FOLLOWING IS A LIST OF ACTIVITIES SUPPORTING THIS PROGRAM AND INDICATING WHETHER THE ACTIVITY IS CONDUCTED IN THE HEADQUARTERS, FIELD, AND/OR REGIONAL OFFICES.IDENTIFY MEASURES TO EVALUATE THE SUCCESSES OF THE COMPLIANCE AND ENFORCEMENT PROGRAM AND TO BETTER ASSESS ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACTS OF ENFORCEMENT ACTIVITIES. (HQ, RT)DEVELOP AND IMPLEMENT MAINTENANCE AND EXIT STRATEGIES FOR SECTORS EVALUATED BASED ON THE RESULTS OF THE COMPLIANCE RATES FOUND. (HQ, RT)DEVELOP ANNUAL, MEDIA-SPECIFIC AND SECTOR PROGRAM GUIDANCE FOR ACTIVITIES RELATED TO COMPLIANCE WITH, AND CIVIL ENFORCEMENT OF, FEDERAL ENVIRONMENTAL LAWS. (HQ, RT)PERFORM STRATEGIC AND TACTICAL TARGETING ANALYSES TO IDENTIFY SECTORS, GEOGRAPHIC AREAS, OR PUBLIC HEALTH RISKS REQUIRING COMPLIANCE ATTENTION. IDENTIFY SPECIFIC FACILITIES WITHIN THOSE PRIORITY SECTORS OR GEOGRAPHIC AREAS REQUIRING CIVIL ADMINISTRATIVE ATTENTION. (HQ, RT)INSPECT AND INVESTIGATE THE REGULATED COMMUNITY, INCLUDING FEDERAL FACILITIES, TO DETERMINE COMPLIANCE WITH APPLICABLE LAWS, REGULATIONS, PERMIT CONDITIONS AND SETTLEMENT AGREEMENTS AND TO DETERMINE WHETHER CONDITIONS PRESENTING IMMINENT AND SUBSTANTIAL ENDANGERMENT MAY EXIST. ON-SITE INSPECTIONS ACTIVITIES INCLUDE MONITORING, RECORD REVIEWS, SAMPLING AND EMISSIONS TESTING. COMPLIANCE MONITORING ACTIVITIES MAY BE BOTH MEDIA AND SECTOR BASED AND MAY OCCUR ON-SITE AND OFF-SITE.  (HQ, RT)RESPOND TO CITIZEN COMPLAINTS AND COMPANY INQUIRIES. (HQ, RT)DEVELOP GUIDANCE, INSPECTION TRAINING COURSES AND COMPLIANCE MONITORING TOOLS NEEDED TO SUPPORT HIGH QUALITY COMPLIANCE MONITORING EFFORTS BY EPA INCLUDING MULTIMEDIA INSPECTIONS. (HQ)ENHANCE ELECTRONIC REPORTING (E-REPORTING) TO INCREASE EFFICIENCY AND EFFECTIVENESS IN MONITORING COMPLIANCE, NEW TECHNOLOGIES TO MONITOR ON-SITE EMISSIONS, AND TRANSPARENCY TO DATA.  (HQ)CONDUCT ANNUAL PLANNING PROCESSES WITH REGIONS AND STATES TO ENSURE ADDRESSING MOST IMPORTANT PROBLEMS. (HQ)WORK WITH STATES ON COMPLIANCE MONITORING STRATEGY'S (CMSS) TO ENSURE COVERAGE TO THE REGULATED COMMUNITY TO DETERMINE COMPLIANCE WITH THE ENVIRONMENTAL LAWS.PROVIDE TECHNICAL AND LEGAL ASSISTANCE AND TRAINING TO STATES AND SUPPORT STATES BY FACILITATING THE EXCHANGE AND DISSEMINATION OF INFORMATION AND DATA.  (HQ)PROVIDE THE PUBLIC ACCESS TO CURRENT STATE AND FEDERAL ENVIRONMENTAL COMPLIANCE AND ENFORCEMENT INFORMATION ON OVER 800,000 REGULATED FACILITIES NATIONWIDE THROUGH ENFORCEMENT AND COMPLIANCE HISTORY ONLINE (ECHO). (HQ)WORK WITH STATES TO ENHANCE THEIR ENFORCEMENT AND COMPLIANCE PROGRAMS THROUGH THE PERFORMANCE PARTNERSHIP AGREEMENT PROCESS. (HQ, RT)PROVIDE TECHNICAL AND LEGAL ASSISTANCE AND TRAINING TO OFFICES OF STATE ATTORNEYS GENERAL (AGS), AND SUPPORT AG?S OFFICES BY FACILITATING THE EXCHANGE AND DISSEMINATION OF INFORMATION AND DATA. (HQ)PROVIDE OVERSIGHT OF AND SUPPORT TO THE REGIONS AND STATES CONDUCTING COMPLIANCE MONITORING ACTIVITIES (E.G., OPERATION OF DATA SYSTEMS FOR REPORTING VIOLATION AND COMPLIANCE INFORMATION INSPECTOR TRAINING, POLICIES, GUIDANCE MANUALS, TOOLS, AND CREDENTIALS.  (HQ)REVIEW COMPLIANCE STATUS OF U.S. RECEIVING FACILITIES AND PROCESS NOTIFICATIONS FOR IMPORTING AND EXPORTING OF HAZARDOUS WASTE AND OTHER ENVIRONMENTALLY REGULATED SUBSTANCES, AND THE EXPORT OF CATHODE RAY TUBES AND SPENT LEAD ACID BATTERIES TO ENSURE COMPLIANCE WITH DOMESTIC REGULATIONS AND INTERNATIONAL AGREEMENTS. (HQ, RT)TRACK THE FLOW OF HAZARDOUS WASTE BOTH IN AND OUT OF THE UNITED STATES AND MONITOR COMPLIANCE WITH APPLICABLE LEGAL REQUIREMENTS BY REVIEWING DOCUMENTATION ASSOCIATED WITH SHIPMENTS. (HQ, RT)CONTINUE THE BILATERAL EFFORTS WITH MEXICO TO BRING CIVIL AND CRIMINAL ENFORCEMENT ACTIONS ALONG THE BORDER AND AGAINST THOSE ILLEGALLY IMPORTING HAZARDOUS WASTES, PESTICIDES, TOXIC CHEMICALS AND OZONE DEPLETING SUBSTANCES. (HQ)SUPPORT VIRTUAL ENVIRONMENTAL COMPLIANCE ASSISTANCE CENTERS.  THESE CENTERS OPERATE IN PARTNERSHIP WITH INDUSTRY ASSOCIATIONS, ENVIRONMENTAL GROUPS, UNIVERSITIES, AND OTHER GOVERNMENT AGENCIES PROVIDING ONE-STOP SHOPPING FOR REGULATORY AND TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE, POLLUTION PREVENTION ACTIVITIES, AND OTHER INFORMATION PARTICULARLY SUITED TO SPECIFIC INDUSTRIAL OR GOVERNMENT SECTORS. (HQ)
Definition: COMPLIANCE MONITORING INCLUDES ALL ACTIVITIES TO DETERMINE WHETHER REGULATED ENTITIES ARE IN COMPLIANCE WITH ENVIRONMENTAL LAWS AND REGULATIONS, AS WELL AS ENFORCEMENT AGREEMENTS (E.G. SUPPLEMENTAL ENVIRONMENTAL PROJECTS AND ADMINISTRATIVE ORDERS). THESE ACTIVITIES CAN INCLUDE, BUT ARE NOT LIMITED TO, PERMIT, FILE AND REPORT REVIEWS; SAMPLING AND GATHERING DATA; ANALYSIS AND USE OF INFORMATION TO DETERMINE HIGH PRIORITY NONCOMPLIANCE OR ENVIRONMENTAL PROBLEMS ON WHICH TO FOCUS FEDERAL, STATE, TRIBAL OR LOCAL ATTENTION; INSPECTIONS AND INVESTIGATIONS. COMPLIANCE MONITORING ACTIVITIES OCCUR BEFORE AND AT THE POINT WHEN ACTUAL COMPLIANCE STATUS IS DETERMINED, E.G., WHETHER A FACILITY IS IN COMPLIANCE OR VIOLATION WITH ENVIRONMENTAL REQUIREMENTS.  COMPLIANCE MONITORING CAN RESULT IN ASSISTANCE OR INCENTIVES BEING OFFERED, ENFORCEMENT BEING TAKEN AS WELL AS ACTIONS BY THE FACILITY TO CORRECT PROBLEMS OR REDUCE POLLUTION.THE STATES, TRIBES, AND LOCAL AUTHORITIES ARE RESPONSIBLE FOR OVERALL COMPLIANCE MONITORING WHERE DELEGATED OR AUTHORIZED UNDER FEDERAL LAWS. THE STATES CONDUCT THE MAJORITY OF COMPLIANCE INSPECTIONS, PERMIT REVIEWS, AND RECORD REVIEWS. EPA FOCUSES ITS COMPLIANCE MONITORING EFFORTS ON A SMALLER NUMBER OF HIGH PRIORITY AREAS (E.G., PETROLEUM REFINERIES, COAL-FIRED POWER PLANTS), ON OPERATING NON-DELEGATED FEDERAL PROGRAMS (E.G., MOBILE SOURCE, GLP, WOODSTOVES, STRATOSPHERIC OZONE, ETC.), RESPONDING TO TIPS AND COMPLAINTS, AND ASSISTING AND TRAINING STATE, TRIBAL, AND LOCAL PERSONNEL.
