Name: CRIMINAL ENFORCEMENT
Title: 52 - CRIMINAL ENFORCEMENT
Laws: RESOURCE CONSERVATION AND RECOVERY ACT AS AMENDED 1984COMPREHENSIVE ENVIRONMENTAL RESPONSE, COMPENSATION, AND      LIABILITY ACT, 1980SUPERFUND AMENDMENTS AND REAUTHORIZATION ACT, 1986OIL POLLUTION ACT, 1990SAFE DRINKING WATER ACT, 1996 CLEAN AIR ACT AND AMENDMENTS, 1990 CLEAN WATER ACT AND AMENDMENTS, 1972TOXIC SUBSTANCES CONTROL ACT, 1976FEDERAL INSECTICIDE, FUNGICIDE AND RODENTICIDE ACT, 1996 FOOD QUALITY PROTECTION ACT, 2005EMERGENCY PLANNING AND RIGHT TO KNOW ACT, 1986 OCEAN DUMPING ACT, 1972
Narrative: EPMCONDUCT CRIMINAL INVESTIGATIONS OF ENVIRONMENTAL CRIMES, PURSUE INVESTIGATIVE LEADS, AND COLLECT AND ANALYZE EVIDENCE TO DEVELOP CASES. (HQ, FIELD)REFER CASES TO THE U.S. ATTORNEY?S OFFICES OF THE DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE FOR PROSECUTION WITH SPECIAL AGENTS SERVING AS KEY WITNESSES IN JUDICIAL PROCEEDINGS.  (HQ, FIELD)PROVIDE LEGAL SUPPORT TO CRIMINAL INVESTIGATORS TO DEVELOP CASES AND REFER CASES TO THE U.S. ATTORNEY?S OFFICES OF THE DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE FOR PROSECUTION.  (RT)WORK WITH OTHER FEDERAL, STATE, TRIBAL, AND LOCAL LAW ENFORCEMENT AUTHORITIES, BOTH TO INVESTIGATE AND SUCCESSFULLY PROSECUTE CRIMINAL VIOLATIONS AND TO BUILD THE CRIMINAL ENFORCEMENT CAPACITY OF OTHER UNITS OF GOVERNMENT, WITH PARTICULAR EMPHASIS PLACED ON PARTICIPATION IN TASK FORCES AND COMMUNITY POLICING EFFORTS. (HQ, FIELD)WORK WITH INTERNATIONAL POLICE ORGANIZATIONS, SUCH AS THE INTERNATIONAL CRIMINAL POLICE ORGANIZATION (INTERPOL), TO IDENTIFY AND APPREHEND FUGITIVES OF ENVIRONMENTAL CRIMES AND SECURE WITNESSES FOR INVESTIGATIONS.  (HQ, FIELD)EMPLOY A PROFILE-BASED TARGETING AND INVESTIGATIONS STRATEGY FOR SCREENING OF POTENTIAL CRIMINAL ENFORCEMENT CASES, USING A VARIETY OF CRIMINAL LAW ENFORCEMENT AND OTHER ELECTRONIC DATA RESOURCES ON REGULATED FACILITIES AND PERSONS, ALONG WITH REMOTE AND SPECIALIZED MONITORING.  (HQ, FIELD)PERFORM SCIENTIFIC AND TECHNICAL FORENSIC ACTIVITIES TO DEVELOP CRIMINAL CASES.  REGIONAL TECHNICAL COORDINATORS WORK ON-SITE WITH CRIMINAL INVESTIGATORS IN THE FIELD.  (FIELD)THE AGENCY IS WORKING TO REDUCE ITS CARBON FOOTPRINT BY PROMOTING GREEN TRAVEL PRACTICES AND MOVING ROUTINE MEETINGS TO A WEB OR VIDEO CONFERENCE FORMAT. IN ORDER TO BE SUCCESSFUL, STRATEGIC INVESTMENTS IN VIDEO/WEB CONFERENCING CAPABILITIES ARE NECESSARY TO CREATE MULTI-USE CONFERENCE ROOMS IN SELECTED LOCATIONS, AS WELL AS THE NEEDED INTERNET CAPACITY.  (HQ)SUPERFUNDCOMPLETE ACTIVITIES IDENTIFIED ABOVE IN THE CONDUCT OF CRIMINAL INVESTIGATIONS RELATED TO CERCLA VIOLATIONS. (HQ, FIELD)
Definition: THE CRIMINAL ENFORCEMENT PROGRAM SEEKS TO IDENTIFY, APPREHEND, AND ASSIST PROSECUTORS IN SUCCESSFULLY CONVICTING THOSE WHO ARE RESPONSIBLE FOR THE MOST SIGNIFICANT AND EGREGIOUS INTENTIONAL VIOLATIONS OF ENVIRONMENTAL LAWS.  BY DEMONSTRATING THAT THE REGULATED COMMUNITY WILL BE HELD ACCOUNTABLE, IN TERMS OF BOTH FINES AND JAIL SENTENCES, FOR SERIOUS AND WILLFUL STATUTORY VIOLATIONS, THE PROGRAM ACTS AS A FORCEFUL DETERRENT IN A WAY THAT CIVIL JUDICIAL AND ADMINISTRATIVE ENFORCEMENT RARELY CAN.  CRIMINAL ENFORCEMENT OF FEDERAL LAWS CANNOT BE DELEGATED TO THE STATES, MAKING THE PROGRAM SOLELY RESPONSIBLE FOR ENFORCING THE CRIMINAL PROVISIONS OF THE ENVIRONMENTAL STATUTES EPA MUST ADMINISTER.  THE PROGRAM IS COMPRISED PRIMARILY OF CRIMINAL INVESTIGATORS LOCATED IN AREA OFFICES ACROSS THE COUNTRY.  STAFF AT HEADQUARTERS PROVIDES OVERALL DIRECTION AND OVERSIGHT OF THE PROGRAM AS WELL AS ADMINISTRATIVE AND LEGAL SUPPORT.

Name: DRINKING WATER PROGRAMS
Title: 53 - DRINKING WATER PROGRAMS
Laws: SAFE DRINKING WATER ACT (SDWA) CLEAN WATER ACT (CWA)
Narrative: EPMTHE DRINKING WATER PROGRAM SUPPORTS STATE AND TRIBAL AGENCIES THAT ARE MAINTAINING PRIMACY (PRIMARY ENFORCEMENT AUTHORITY) FOR EXISTING HEALTH-BASED DRINKING WATER REGULATIONS; ARE ADOPTING NEW REGULATIONS; AND ARE UPDATING PRIMACY ACCORDING TO THE REQUIREMENTS AND DEADLINES SET FORTH IN THE 1996 SAFE DRINKING WATER ACT, AS AMENDED (1996 SDWA).   TO IMPLEMENT THE MULTIPLE BARRIER APPROACH, THE AGENCY WORKS IN PARTNERSHIP WITH STATES, TRIBES, AND COMMUNITIES TO ENSURE THAT ALL DRINKING WATER SYSTEMS HAVE ADEQUATE TECHNICAL, FINANCIAL, AND MANAGERIAL CAPACITY TO PROVIDE SAFE DRINKING WATER UNDER THE REQUIREMENTS OF FEDERAL DRINKING WATER REGULATIONS.OTHER IMPORTANT DRINKING WATER PROGRAM IMPLEMENTATION ACTIVITIES INCLUDE:DIRECTLY IMPLEMENTING PUBLIC WATER SUPPLY SUPERVISION (PWSS) ACTIVITIES IN INDIAN COUNTRY, INCLUDING ALLOCATION OF TRIBAL PWSS SET-ASIDES.WORKING WITH STATE DRINKING WATER STATE REVOLVING FUND (DWSRF) PROGRAMS TO PROVIDE FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE TO PUBLIC DRINKING WATER SYSTEMS DEVELOPING AND IMPLEMENTING POLICIES, PROCEDURES, AND INFORMATION MANAGEMENT SYSTEMS, E.G., SDWIS AND FED/LAN TO TRACK THE IMPLEMENTATION OF THE PROGRAM.ASSISTING STATES IN COLLECTING AND LOADING DATA INTO SDWIS/FED, AND PROVIDING TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE AND TRAINING TO STATES THAT ARE USING SDWIS/STATE AS THEIR DATABASE OF RECORDANOTHER MAJOR RESPONSIBILITY OF THE DRINKING WATER PROGRAM UNDER THE 1996 SDWA IS TO PROMULGATE DRINKING WATER REGULATIONS BASED ON SOUND SCIENCE, COST ANALYSIS, AND RISK ASSESSMENT.  THIS RESPONSIBILITY INVOLVES USING SCIENTIFIC AND ENGINEERING DATA ON EFFECTIVE TREATMENT TECHNOLOGIES, DRINKING WATER MONITORING APPROACHES, AND ANALYTICAL (DETECTION) METHODS.  THE DRINKING WATER REGULATIONS REQUIRE THAT AMOUNTS OF CONTAMINANTS - MICROBES, ORGANIC AND INORGANIC CHEMICALS, AND RADIONUCLIDE - IN FINISHED DRINKING WATER DO NOT EXCEED SAFE LEVELS.  FOR EACH REGULATED CONTAMINANT, EPA IDENTIFIES A MAXIMUM CONTAMINANT LEVEL (MCLS) OR A TREATMENT TECHNIQUE TO ENSURE THE REQUISITE LEVEL OF CONTAMINANT REMOVAL.RELATED TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF NEW REGULATIONS ARE 1) THE ANALYSIS AND SELECTION OF UNREGULATED CONTAMINANTS FOR POTENTIAL REGULATION, AND 2) THE REVIEW OF EXISTING REGULATIONS FOR POSSIBLE REVISIONS TO IMPROVE PUBLIC HEALTH PROTECTION, THROUGH THE SDWA-MANDATED CONTAMINANT CANDIDATE LIST (CCL) AND SIX YEAR REVIEW (SIX-YEAR) PRIORITY-SETTING PROCESSES, RESPECTIVELY.  CCL AND SIX-YEAR ACTIVITIES INVOLVE THE ESTABLISHMENT OF REGULATORY PRIORITIES BY EVALUATING THE SUFFICIENCY OF DATA ON CURRENT ANALYTICAL METHODS AND TREATMENT TECHNOLOGIES; THE BEST AVAILABLE PEER-REVIEWED HEALTH AFFECTS STUDIES; AND DATA ON CONTAMINANT OCCURRENCE IN DRINKING WATER SYSTEMS.  IF THERE ARE ADEQUATE DATA IN EACH OF THESE AREAS, EPA IS ABLE TO DETERMINE WHETHER A NEW OR REVISED STANDARD IS NECESSARY TO PROTECT PUBLIC HEALTH.  OTHER ACTIVITIES IN THIS PROGRAM AREA INCLUDE;UPDATE HEALTH ADVISORIES FOR CONTAMINANT CANDIDATE LIST (CCL) CHEMICALS, WHICH ARE NOT RECOMMENDED FOR REGULATION. (HQ ONLY)UPDATE HUMAN HEALTH CRITERIA USING THE LATEST HUMAN HEALTH CRITERIA METHODOLOGY.IDENTIFY AND, IF APPROPRIATE, ISSUE OR REVISE HUMAN HEALTH CRITERIA UNDER SECTION 304(A) OF THE CWA.IDENTIFY SURFACE WATERS THAT ARE USED AS A DRINKING WATER SOURCE AND, WHERE ATTAINABLE, ADOPT PUBLIC WATER SUPPLY AS A DESIGNATED USE OR PROVIDE AN EQUIVALENT LEVEL OF HUMAN HEALTH PROTECTION USING CWA AUTHORITIES.THE DRINKING WATER PROGRAM ALSO IS RESPONSIBLE FOR PROTECTING SURFACE AND GROUNDWATER SOURCES OF DRINKING WATER FROM CONTAMINATION.  EPA WORKS WITH STATES, TRIBES, OTHER FEDERAL AGENCIES, APPROPRIATE ORGANIZATIONS, AND LOCAL COMMUNITIES TO CONDUCT SOURCE WATER ASSESSMENTS AND DEVELOP AND IMPLEMENT SOURCE WATER PROTECTION PROGRAMS.  ACTIVITIES WITHIN THIS PROGRAM INCLUDES:SUPPORTING IMPLEMENTATION OF SOURCE WATER PROTECTION IN BOTH GROUND AND SURFACE WATER-BASED SYSTEMS.WORK WITH STATES AND TRIBES TO IDENTIFY SURFACE WATERS THAT ARE USED AS A DRINKING WATER SOURCE AND, WHERE ATTAINABLE, ADOPT PUBLIC WATER SUPPLY AS A DESIGNATED USE OR PROVIDE AN EQUIVALENT LEVEL OF HUMAN HEALTH PROTECTION USING CWA AUTHORITIES.ASSISTING STATES AND TRIBES IN PREVENTING CONTAMINATION OF DRINKING WATER SOURCES FROM MORE THAN TWO DOZEN TYPES OF CLASS V (SHALLOW) UNDERGROUND INJECTION WELLS THAT PRESENT THE GREATEST RISK IN SOURCE WATER PROTECTION AREAS; AND DETERMINE, THROUGH SURVEYS, IF OTHER TYPES OF CLASS V WELLS NEED TO BE REGULATED IN SOURCE WATER PROTECTION AREAS.THE AGENCY ALSO DIRECTLY IMPLEMENTS UNDERGROUND INJECTION CONTROL (UIC) ACTIVITIES IN STATES AS WELL AS ON INDIAN LANDS, INCLUDING ALLOCATION OF TRIBAL SET-ASIDES, AND PROVIDES TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE TO UIC PROGRAMS TO ENSURE THAT WELL CLASSES I-IV ALSO DO NOT ENDANGER GROUND WATER RESOURCES. (HQ AND R)THE AGENCY IS WORKING TO REDUCE ITS CARBON FOOTPRINT BY PROMOTING GREEN TRAVEL PRACTICES AND MOVING ROUTINE MEETINGS TO A WEB OR VIDEO CONFERENCE FORMAT. IN ORDER TO BE SUCCESSFUL, STRATEGIC INVESTMENTS IN VIDEO/WEB CONFERENCING CAPABILITIES ARE NECESSARY TO CREATE MULTI-USE CONFERENCE ROOMS IN SELECTED LOCATIONS, AS WELL AS THE NEEDED INTERNET CAPACITY.S&TS&T RESOURCES IN THE DRINKING WATER PROGRAM ARE CHARGED ONLY TO THE DRINKING WATER TECHNICAL SUPPORT CENTER (TSC) IN CINCINNATI, OHIO. TSC EVALUATES ENGINEERING AND SCIENTIFIC DATA, INCLUDING TREATMENT TECHNOLOGIES, MONITORING APPROACHES AND ANALYTICAL METHODS, TO DEVELOP NATIONAL PRIMARY DRINKING WATER REGULATIONS THAT ENSURE THE SAFETY OF DRINKING WATER.OATHE WORK PERFORMED BY THE HQ OFFICE OF THE ADMINISTRATOR IS AN ENABLING AND SUPPORT FUNCTION THAT SUPPORTS THE OUTCOMES OF THE AGENCY?S STRATEGIC GOALS AND THE FOLLOWING CROSS-GOAL STRATEGIES:  STRATEGY 1:  EXPANDING THE CONVERSATION ON ENVIRONMENTALISM; STRATEGY 2: WORKING FOR ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE AND CHILDREN?S HEALTH; STRATEGY 3: ADVANCING SCIENCE, RESEARCH, AND TECHNOLOGICAL INNOVATION; AND STRATEGY 4:  STRENGTHENING STATE, TRIBAL, AND INTERNATIONAL PARTNERSHIPS.
Definition: THIS PROGRAM COMPRISES THE MULTIPLE-BARRIER APPROACH TO PROTECTING PUBLIC HEALTH FROM UNSAFE DRINKING WATER.  UNDER THIS APPROACH, THE AGENCY PROTECTS PUBLIC HEALTH THROUGH: SOURCE WATER ASSESSMENT AND PROTECTION PROGRAMS; PROMULGATION OF NEW OR REVISED, SCIENTIFICALLY SOUND AND RISK-BASED NATIONAL PRIMARY DRINKING WATER REGULATIONS (NPDWRS); TRAINING, TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE AND FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAMS TO ENHANCE SYSTEMS? CAPACITY TO COMPLY WITH EXISTING AND NEW REGULATIONS; AND THE NATIONAL IMPLEMENTATION OF NPDWRS BY STATE AND TRIBAL DRINKING WATER PROGRAMS THROUGH REGULATORY, NON-REGULATORY AND VOLUNTARY PROGRAMS AND POLICIES TO ENSURE SAFE DRINKING WATER.

Name: ENDOCRINE DISRUPTORS
Title: 54 - ENDOCRINE DISRUPTORS
Laws: FOOD QUALITY PROTECTION ACT (FQPA) OF 1996SAFE DRINKING WATER ACT (SDWA) OF 1996
Narrative: FINALIZING THE VALIDATION OF THE TIER 2 ASSAYS; PRIORITIZING AND SELECTING ADDITIONAL CHEMICALS FOR TIER 1 SCREENING; ISSUING TIER 1 TEST ORDERS FOR THE SELECTED CHEMICALS BASED ON PUBLICALLY-VETTED POLICIES AND PROCEDURES. EVALUATING RESULTS OF TIER 1 SCREENING DATA SUBMITTED FOR PESTICIDE CHEMICALS. DETERMINING WHICH PESTICIDE CHEMICALS THAT HAVE COMPLETED TIER 1 SCREENING WILL REQUIRE TIER II TESTING, BASED PUBLICALLY AVAILABLE EVALUATION CRITERIA. ESTABLISH POLICIES, PROCEDURES, AND RULES FOR IMPLEMENTING THE ENDOCRINE EFFECTS SCREENING AUTHORITIES OF THE FOOD QUALITY PROTECTION ACT AND SAFE DRINKING WATER ACT AMENDMENTS OF 1996.
Definition: THE PROGRAM DEVELOPS AND VALIDATES SCIENTIFIC TEST METHODS FROM WHICH A BATTERY OF TESTS WILL BE SELECTED AND USED FOR THE ROUTINE, ONGOING EVALUATION OF PESTICIDES AND OTHER CHEMICALS TO DETERMINE THEIR POTENTIAL FOR ADVERSE HEALTH OR ENVIRONMENTAL EFFECTS BY INTERFERING WITH NORMAL ENDOCRINE SYSTEM FUNCTION.

Name: ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE
Title: 57 - ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE
Laws: EXECUTIVE ORDER 12898 (FEDERAL ACTIONS TO ADDRESS ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE IN MINORITY POPULATIONS AND LOW INCOME POPULATIONS, 1994RESOURCE CONSERVATION AND RECOVERY ACT, 1984 COMPREHENSIVE ENVIRONMENTAL RESPONSE, COMPENSATION, AND LIABILITY ACT,1980SUPERFUND AMENDMENTS AND REAUTHORIZATION ACT, 1986OIL POLLUTION ACT, 1990SAFE DRINKING WATER ACT, 1996CLEAN WATER ACT, 1972CLEAN AIR ACT AS AMENDED, 1990TOXIC SUBSTANCES CONTROL ACT, 1976FEDERAL INSECTICIDE, FUNGICIDE AND RODENTICIDE ACT, 1996FOOD QUALITY PROTECTION ACT, 1996EMERGENCY PLANNING AND RIGHT TO KNOW ACT, 1986OCEAN DUMPING ACT, 1972
Narrative: THE OFFICE OF ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE (OEJ) RESOURCES IN THIS PROGRAM/PROJECT SUPPORT THE ADMINISTRATOR, OECA, OTHER AGENCY PROGRAM OFFICES, REGIONAL OFFICES, AND COMMUNITIES IN ADDRESSING ENVIRONMENTAL AND HUMAN HEALTH CONCERNS IN MINORITY, LOW-INCOME, TRIBAL AND OTHER HISTORICALLY UNDERREPRESENTED COMMUNITIES. A VARIETY OF ACTIVITIES HAVE BEEN ESTABLISHED TO ADDRESS THIS NEED. THE AGENCY?S OFFICE OF ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE IS LOCATED WITHIN OECA.  IN ADDITION, EACH HEADQUARTERS AND REGIONAL PROGRAM SUPPORTS THIS EFFORT THROUGH ACTIVITIES UNDER THEIR APPROPRIATE PROGRAM/PROJECT. THE FOLLOWING IS A DETAILED LIST OF ACTIVITIES INDICATING WHETHER THE ACTIVITY IS PERFORMED BY HEADQUARTERS OR REGIONAL OFFICES, OR BOTH.EPM PROVIDE GUIDANCE TO THE AGENCY ON APPROACHES WHICH ADDRESS THE ENVIRONMENTAL RISKS AND IMPACTS OF AGENCY DECISIONS AND ENVIRONMENTAL PROGRAMS ON MINORITY, LOW-INCOME, TRIBAL AND OTHER HISTORICALLY UNDERREPRESENTED POPULATIONS; (HQ)SUPPORT AGENCY EFFORTS TO ENGAGE MINORITY, LOW-INCOME, TRIBAL AND OTHER HISTORICALLY UNDERREPRESENTED COMMUNITIES IN AGENCY DECISION-MAKING PROCESSES; (HQ, RT)DEVELOP POLICY, LEGAL AND TECHNICAL TOOLS TO ENHANCE ANALYSIS OF EJ ISSUES AND THEIR INCORPORATION IN AGENCY DECISION-MAKING PROCESSES; (HQ)SUPPORT IMPLEMENTATION OF THE AGENCY'S ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE STRATEGIES AND WORKING WITH OECA AND OTHER AGENCY PROGRAM OFFICES TO ENSURE THAT AGENCY ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE PRIORITIES ARE IMPLEMENTED; (HQ, RT)PROVIDE GUIDANCE AND SUPPORT TO NATIONAL AND REGIONAL ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE PROGRAMS TO IMPLEMENT AGENCY ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE STRATEGIES AND ENSURE THAT ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE IS INTEGRATED WITHIN ALL AGENCY PRIORITIES; (HQ)MANAGE AND MONITOR UTILIZATION OF REGIONAL ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE RESOURCES, WHICH INCLUDES ADMINISTRATIVE AND PROGRAM ELEMENTS SUCH AS PAYROLL/FTE AND GRANTS, (HQ, RT) SERVING AS A FOCAL POINT FOR COORDINATION OF INTERAGENCY ACTIVITIES ON ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE, INCLUDING THE DEVELOPMENT AND IMPLEMENTATION OF ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE STRATEGIES BY INDIVIDUAL FEDERAL AGENCIES AND PARTNERSHIP EFFORTS TO ADVANCE ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE AMONG MULTIPLE FEDERAL AGENCIES; (HQ)DEVELOP AND PROVIDE ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE TRAINING SUPPORT TO AGENCY MANAGERS, STAFF, FEDERAL AGENCIES, AND COMMUNITIES; (HQ, RT)PROVIDE FINANCIAL AND TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE TO COMMUNITIES FOR COMMUNITY-BASED EFFORTS TO ADDRESS ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE CONCERNS; AND (HQ, RT)MANAGE AND SUPPORT THE ACTIVITIES OF THE NATIONAL ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE ADVISORY COMMITTEE (NEJAC) TO PROVIDE THE ADMINISTRATOR WITH ADVICE AND RECOMMENDATIONS WITH RESPECT TO INTEGRATING ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE CONSIDERATIONS INTO EPA'S PROGRAMS, POLICIES, AND DAY-TO-DAY ACTIVITIES. (HQ)SUPERFUNDSUPPORT AGENCY PERSONNEL AND OTHERS? ABILITIES TO ASSESS GEOGRAPHIC AREAS WITH POTENTIAL ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE CONCERNS BY PROVIDING GIS MAPPING AND OTHER TOOLS THAT HELP IDENTIFY POTENTIALLY IMPACTED COMMUNITIES.  EJVIEW IS ONE OF THE TOOLS AND IS PUBLICLY AVAILABLE ON THE INTERNET.  A NEWER TOOL, EJ SCREEN, WAS RELEASED FOR INTERNAL USE IN OCTOBER 2012 AND WILL REPLACE EJVIIEW.  (HQ)SUPPORT ADMINISTRATOR, OECA, OTHER AGENCY PROGRAM OFFICES, REGIONAL OFFICES WITH COMMUNITY ENGAGEMENT ACTIVITIES AND OTHER WORK DONE UNDER THE SUPERFUND PROGRAM AT,  NEAR OR ASSOCIATED WITH AFFECTED SITES, SUCH AS COMMUNITY-BASED RESEARCH EFFORTS, TRAINING, INTERNSHIP PROGRAMS, AND COMMUNITY OUTREACH EFFORTS.   (HQ)
Definition: ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE IS DEFINED AS THE FAIR TREATMENT AND MEANINGFUL INVOLVEMENT OF ALL PEOPLE REGARDLESS OF RACE, COLOR, NATIONAL ORIGIN, OR INCOME WITH RESPECT TO THE DEVELOPMENT, IMPLEMENTATION, AND ENFORCEMENT OF ENVIRONMENTAL LAWS, REGULATIONS, AND POLICIES. THE ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE PROGRAM PROVIDES SUPPORT TO THE ADMINISTRATOR, OFFICE OF ENFORCEMENT AND COMPLIANCE ASSURANCE (OECA), OTHER AGENCY PROGRAM OFFICES, AND REGIONAL OFFICES TO ADDRESS ENVIRONMENTAL AND HUMAN HEALTH CONCERNS IN MINORITY, LOW-INCOME, TRIBAL AND OTHER HISTORICALLY UNDERREPRESENTED COMMUNITIES. THIS SUPPORT IS PROVIDED BY FACILITATING THE AGENCY?S ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE INTEGRATION EFFORTS IN ALL PROGRAMS, POLICIES, AND ACTIVITIES.

CONTINUE TO IMPLEMENT STANDARDS, PLANS, STRATEGIES, AND ACTIONS TO PRESERVE AIR QUALITY IMPROVEMENTS ALREADY MADE.  PERFORM STUDIES ON SPECIFIC AIR POLLUTION ISSUES SUCH AS VOLATILE ORGANIC COMPOUND (VOC) EMISSIONS FROM THE USE OF CONSUMER PRODUCTS. REGULARLY ISSUE THE AIR QUALITY INDEX (AQI) REPORTS AND AIR QUALITY FORECASTS. CONTINUE INNOVATIVE PROGRAMS OZONE AND PM ADVANCEOAR - EPM-RT  WORK WITH STATES AND TRIBES TO MONITOR AMBIENT AIR QUALITY FOR CRITERIA POLLUTANTS IN ORDER TO ASSESS ENVIRONMENTAL QUALITY AND PROGRESS.  OBTAIN AND REVIEW STATE AND TRIBAL EMISSIONS INVENTORY DATA.  ENSURE THAT CRITERIA POLLUTANT AMBIENT MONITORING SITES COMPLY WITH SITE?S REQUIREMENTS.  REVIEW NSR AND TITLE V PERMITS PROPOSED BY STATES.  REVIEW PROGRAM REVISIONS TO ENSURE COMPLIANCE WITH THE PART 70 RULES.  DEVELOP PART 71 TITLE V PERMITS FOR STATES AND TRIBES WHERE NECESSARY. PROMOTE THE GOALS AND REQUIREMENTS OF EXECUTIVE ORDER (EO) 12898 ON ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE IN REVIEWING TITLE V PERMITS AND IMPLEMENT THE AGENCY'S CUSTOMER SERVICE STANDARDS FOR PERMITTING UNDER EO 12862.  DEVELOP AND IMPLEMENT STANDARDS, PLANS, STRATEGIES, AND ACTIONS TO PRESERVE AIR QUALITY IMPROVEMENTS ALREADY MADE.  CONDUCT FEDERAL REFERENCE METHOD PERFORMANCE AUDITS ON ESTABLISHED AMBIENT AIR MONITORING SITES AS APPROPRIATE.  CONDUCT TECHNICAL SYSTEMS AUDITS ON THE REPORTING ORGANIZATION RESPONSIBLE FOR CRITERIA POLLUTANT MONITORING.  PROVIDE POLICY GUIDANCE AND TECHNICAL SUPPORT TO STATES AND TRIBES DEVELOPING ATTAINMENT DEMONSTRATIONS FOR ACHIEVING THE NAAQS AND REGIONAL HAZE PROGRAMS.  REVIEW CAA SECTION 111 (D) PLANS AND WHERE APPROPRIATE REVISE THE FEDERAL IMPLEMENTATION PLAN (FIPS) AS STATES SUBMIT THE PLANS.  ASSIST HEADQUARTERS IN PROMULGATING OZONE TRANSPORT FIPS AND SECTION 126 PETITIONS.  OVERSEE AND PROVIDE TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE TO EXISTING AIR MONITORING NETWORKS. PROVIDE POLICY AND PROGRAM INPUT TO THE OFFICE OF AIR AND RADIATION.  REVIEW AND PROCESS STATE IMPLEMENTATION PLANS (SIP) REVISIONS. IMPLEMENT, IN COLLABORATION WITH STATES, FEDERAL MEASURES, ASSIST WITH THE DEVELOPMENT OF SIPS, AND DEVELOP AIR TOXICS TOOLS TO CONTINUE IMPROVING AIR QUALITY (AS MEASURED BY THE AIR QUALITY INDEX AND OTHER MEASURES) AND TO CONTINUE REDUCING AIR TOXICS RISK.OA THE WORK PERFORMED BY THE HQ OFFICE OF THE ADMINISTRATOR IS AN ENABLING AND SUPPORT FUNCTION THAT SUPPORTS THE OUTCOMES OF THE AGENCY?S STRATEGIC GOALS AND THE FOLLOWING CROSS-GOAL STRATEGIES:  STRATEGY 1:  EXPANDING THE CONVERSATION ON ENVIRONMENTALISM; STRATEGY 2: WORKING FOR ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE AND CHILDREN?S HEALTH; STRATEGY 3: ADVANCING SCIENCE, RESEARCH, AND TECHNOLOGICAL INNOVATION; AND STRATEGY 4:  STRENGTHENING STATE, TRIBAL, AND INTERNATIONAL PARTNERSHIPS.
Definition: THIS PROGRAM INCLUDES BOTH EPA HEADQUARTERS AND REGIONAL FEDERAL SUPPORT TO ASSIST STATE, TRIBES, AND LOCAL AIR POLLUTION CONTROL AGENCIES IN THE DEVELOPMENT, IMPLEMENTATION, AND EVALUATION OF PROGRAMS TO IMPLEMENT THE NAAQS, ESTABLISH STANDARDS FOR REDUCING AIR TOXICS, AND SUSTAIN VISIBILITY PROTECTION.

Name: FEDERAL VEHICLE AND FUELS STANDARDS AND CERTIFICATION
Title: 61 - FEDERAL VEHICLE AND FUELS STANDARDS AND CERTIFICATION
Laws: CLEAN AIR ACT AMENDMENTS, TITLE I (NAAQS); CLEAN AIR ACT AMENDMENTS, TITLE II (MOBILE SOURCES); MOTOR VEHICLE INFORMATION AND COST SAVINGS ACT; ALTERNATIVE MOTOR FUELS ACT OF 1988 (AFMA); NATIONAL HIGHWAY SYSTEM DESIGNATION ACTCLEAN AIR ACT AMENDMENTS, 42 U.S.C. 7401 ET SEQ. - SECTIONS 102, 103, 104, AND 108ENERGY POLICY ACT OF 2005ENERGY INDEPENDENCE AND SECURITY ACT OF 2007LIGHT-DUTY VEHICLE GREENHOUSE GAS EMISSION STANDARDS AND CORPORATE AVERAGE FUEL ECONOMY STANDARDS (40 CFR PARTS 85, 86, AND 600)NATIONAL ENVIRONMENTAL POLICY ACTSAFETEA-LU OF 2005GLOBAL CLIMATE PROTECTION ACT, 15 U.S.C. 2901 ? SECTION 1103.
Narrative: PROVIDE ADVICE AND COUNSEL TO THE ADMINISTRATOR ON FEDERAL VEHICLE AND FUELS STANDARDS AND CERTIFICATION.  PROVIDE POLICY, PROGRAM, AND MANAGEMENT GUIDANCE TO THE OFFICE OF AIR AND RADIATION PROGRAM, STAFF, AND REGIONAL OFFICES ON FEDERAL VEHICLE AND FUELS STANDARDS AND CERTIFICATION. PROVIDE ANALYSIS, PLANNING, BUDGETING, AND MANAGEMENT CAPABILITY TO ENSURE ANALYTIC SUPPORT TO REGIONAL, STATE, AND TRIBAL PROGRAMS FOR THE FEDERAL VEHICLE AND FUELS STANDARDS AND CERTIFICATION.  ESTABLISH APPROPRIATE FUEL AND EMISSION STANDARDS FOR VEHICLES AND ENGINES, ENSURE COMPLIANCE WITH STANDARDS, AND ASSESS PROGRESS IN MEETING EMISSION REDUCTIONS GOALS THROUGH IMPLEMENTATION OF MOBILE SOURCE REGULATORY AND VOLUNTARY PROGRAMS. ASSESS THE NEED FOR AND RECOMMEND PROGRAM CHANGES REQUIRED TO ENSURE MORE EFFECTIVE CARBON MONOXIDE (CO) EMISSION REDUCTIONS FROM MOBILE SOURCES.  PROVIDE ACCURATE FUEL ECONOMY INFORMATION TO THE CONSUMER THROUGH THE FUEL ECONOMY LABEL AND GUIDE PROGRAMS, AND PROVIDE CORPORATE AVERAGE FUEL ECONOMY (CAFE) DATA TO THE DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION.  ASSESS THE QUALITY OF FUEL ADDITIVES USED IN MOBILE SOURCES AND ENSURE THAT FUELS AND FUEL ADDITIVE REQUIREMENTS ARE IMPLEMENTED.  CERTIFY IMPORTED VEHICLES MEET FEDERAL EMISSIONS STANDARDS.  ASSESS AND CHARACTERIZE TOXIC EMISSIONS FROM MOBILE SOURCES AND IMPLEMENT PROGRAMS WHICH REDUCE THESE EMISSIONS. DEVELOP PLANS, STRATEGIES, AND ACTIONS TO PRESERVE AIR QUALITY IMPROVEMENTS ALREADY MADE FOR CONTROL OF AIR TOXICS.  OPERATE THE NATIONAL VEHICLE AND FUELS EMISSIONS LABORATORY IN ANN ARBOR, MICHIGAN.  THIS PROGRAM/PROJECT CAN USE FUNDS TO OBTAIN SEE PROGRAM RESOURCES.
Definition: THIS PROGRAM INCLUDES FEDERAL ACTIVITIES FOR THE DEVELOPMENT, IMPLEMENTATION, AND EVALUATION OF REGULATORY, MARKET-BASED, AND VOLUNTARY PROGRAMS TO REDUCE POLLUTANT EMISSIONS FROM MOBILE SOURCES AND FUELS, AS WELL AS REDUCE VEHICLE TRAVEL.  REDUCING EMISSIONS FROM MOBILE SOURCES HELP IMPLEMENT NATIONAL AMBIENT AIR QUALITY STANDARDS (NAAQS) AND REDUCE GREENHOUSE GASES, AIR  TOXICS  AND REGIONAL HAZE.  TYPES OF MOBILE SOURCES INCLUDE:  LIGHT-DUTY VEHICLES/ENGINES/EQUIPMENT (AUTOMOBILES, LIGHT TRUCKS, MOTORCYCLES, LAWN AND GARDEN EQUIPMENT) , HEAVY-DUTY ENGINES (BUSES AND LARGE TRUCKS), NON-ROAD VEHICLES/ENGINES/EQUIPMENT (CONSTRUCTION AND FARM EQUIPMENT, BOATS, RECREATIONAL VEHICLES, LOCOMOTIVES, ETC.), AND FUELS (DIESEL, GASOLINE, ALTERNATIVE RENEWABLE FUELS).

Name: FORENSICS SUPPORT
Title: 62 - FORENSICS SUPPORT
Laws: RESOURCE CONSERVATION AND RECOVERY ACT AS AMENDED IN 1984, COMPREHENSIVE ENVIRONMENTAL RESPONSE, COMPENSATION, AND LIABILITY ACT AS AMENDED IN 1986,OIL POLLUTION ACT IN 1990, SAFE DRINKING WATER ACT AS AMENDED IN 1984, CLEAN AIR ACT AS AMENDED IN 1990, CLEAN WATER ACT IN 1972, TOXIC SUBSTANCES CONTROL ACT IN 1976, FEDERAL INSECTICIDE, FUNGICIDE AND RODENTICIDE ACT IN 1996, FOOD QUALITY PROTECTION ACT IN 2005, EMERGENCY PLANNING AND RIGHT TO KNOW ACT IN 1986, ANDOCEAN DUMPING ACT IN 1972
Narrative: S&TPROVIDE TECHNICAL FORENSIC SUPPORT FOR THE NATION'S MOST COMPLEX CRIMINAL AND CIVIL ENFORCEMENT CASES INCLUDING SPECIALIZED LAB ANALYSIS, EVIDENCE SAMPLING, DATA TARGETING, COMPLIANCE MONITORING AND EVIDENCE AUDIT SUPPORT.PERFORM PROCESS-BASED FACILITY/INDUSTRY INVESTIGATIONS, POLLUTION CONTROL INVESTIGATIONS, FACILITY ENVIRONMENTAL MANAGEMENT SYSTEM INVESTIGATIONS, SINGLE AND MULTIMEDIA COMPLIANCE MONITORING INVESTIGATIONS, AND DEVELOP FIELD INVESTIGATION PROTOCOLS.PERFORM LABORATORY ANALYSIS TO PROVIDE EVIDENCE OF VIOLATIONS OF HAZARDOUS WASTE, WATER, AND AIR LAWS AND REGULATIONS AND/OR IDENTIFY THE SPECIFIC SOURCE OF OFF-SITE CONTAMINATION.PERFORM FINANCIAL AND ECONOMIC DATA ANALYSIS, INCLUDING FINANCIAL INVESTIGATIONS CASE SUPPORT, ECONOMIC BENEFIT OF NONCOMPLIANCE DETERMINATION, ABILITY TO PAY AND ECONOMIC BENEFIT EXPERT TESTIMONY.SUPPORT INFORMATION FORENSICS ACTIVITIES WITH EXPERTISE IN LIBRARY ADMINISTRATION SERVICES, BUSINESS INFORMATION SERVICES, EPA DATABASE RETRIEVAL AND DATA INTEGRATION SERVICES, AND COMMERCIAL DATABASE RETRIEVAL AND INFORMATION SERVICES.SEIZE AND RECOVER COMPUTER DATA FOR ACTIVITIES RELATED TO LABORATORY FRAUD CASES, FRAUDULENT DATA REPORTING ISSUES, INTERNAL DATA AND SECURITY ISSUES, AND REVERSE ENGINEERING.PROVIDE NATIONAL PROGRAM OVERSIGHT, LEGAL REVIEW, AND ADMINISTRATIVE SUPPORT. (HQ)PERFORM A WIDE RANGE OF CIVIL INVESTIGATIONS.SUPERFUNDPERFORM ACTIVITIES LISTED ABOVE IN SUPPORT OF CERCLA RELATED CASES.
Definition: THE FORENSICS PROGRAM PROVIDES SPECIALIZED SUPPORT FOR THE NATION?S MOST COMPLEX CIVIL AND CRIMINAL ENFORCEMENT CASES, AND TECHNICAL EXPERTISE FOR NON-ROUTINE AGENCY COMPLIANCE EFFORTS.  THE PROGRAM, AS ADMINISTERED BY THE NATIONAL ENFORCEMENT INVESTIGATIONS CENTER (NEIC) IN DENVER, COLORADO, PROVIDES FIELD, TECHNICAL, LABORATORY, AND INFORMATION SERVICES FOR ENFORCEMENT INVESTIGATIONS, CASE PREPARATIONS, AND SETTLEMENT NEGOTIATIONS AT BOTH PRIVATE AND FEDERAL FACILITIES.  RESOURCES ARE DIRECTED TO ACTIVITIES THAT: (A) INVOLVE PRECEDENT-SETTING CASES; (B) INVOLVE VIOLATIONS OF THE CRIMINAL, CIVIL, AND ADMINISTRATIVE PROVISIONS OF ENVIRONMENTAL LAWS; (C) HAVE MULTI-REGIONAL OR MULTI-MEDIA IMPACTS; (D) REQUIRE THE INNOVATIVE APPLICATION OF ENGINEERING AND SCIENTIFIC TECHNOLOGY TO RESOLVE COMPLEX POLLUTION AND ENFORCEMENT ISSUES; (E) ADDRESS A SPECIFIC REGIONAL ENFORCEMENT PRIORITY THAT EXCEEDS REGIONAL RESOURCES OR CAPABILITIES; OR (F) SUPPORT DEVELOPMENT AND/OR IMPLEMENTATION OF NATIONAL ENFORCEMENT INITIATIVES.  THE NEIC ALSO PROVIDES TECHNICAL AND ADMINISTRATIVE SUPPORT AND INSTRUCTORS TO SUPPORT TRAINING OF FEDERAL, STATE, AND LOCAL ENFORCEMENT PERSONNEL ON THE TECHNICAL ASPECTS OF ENVIRONMENTAL ENFORCEMENT.

Name: GEOGRAPHIC PROGRAM:  CHESAPEAKE BAY
Title: 63 - GEOGRAPHIC PROGRAM:  CHESAPEAKE BAY
Laws: CLEAN WATER ACT (CWA), 33 U.S.C. 26 ET SEQ. ? SECTIONS 1267 AND 1313; RESOURCE CONSERVATION AND RECOVERY ACT (RCRA), 42 U.S.C. 6901 ET SEQ; CLEAN AIR ACT (CAA), 42 U.S.C. 85 ET SEQ.
Narrative: OFFICE OF WATER AND REGION 3WORK WITH THE STATES, FEDERAL AGENCIES, LOCAL GOVERNMENTS, AND STAKEHOLDERS TO RESTORE AND PROTECT LIVING RESOURCES, THEIR HABITATS AND ECOLOGICAL RELATIONSHIPS IN THE CHESAPEAKE BAY WATERSHED.IMPLEMENT EXECUTIVE ORDER 13508 ?TO PROTECT AND RESTORE THE HEALTH, HERITAGE, NATURAL RESOURCES, AND SOCIAL AND ECONOMIC VALUE OF THE NATION?S LARGEST ESTUARINE ECOSYSTEM AND THE NATURAL SUSTAINABILITY OF ITS WATERSHED.?  THE EXECUTIVE ORDER DECLARED THE BAY A ?NATIONAL TREASURE? WHILE SIMULTANEOUSLY ACKNOWLEDGING THAT THE PAST 25 YEARS HAD NOT SEEN SUFFICIENT PROGRESS IN RESTORING THE HEALTH OF THE BAY AND ITS WATERSHED.IMPLEMENT THE CHESAPEAKE BAY TOTAL MAXIMUM DAILY LOAD (TMDL), A COMPREHENSIVE ?POLLUTION DIET? WITH APPROPRIATE ACCOUNTABILITY MEASURES TO RESTORE CLEAN WATER IN THE CHESAPEAKE BAY AND THE REGION?S STREAMS, CREEKS, AND RIVERS.  THE TMDL IS DESIGNED TO ENSURE THAT ALL NITROGEN, PHOSPHORUS, AND SEDIMENT POLLUTION CONTROL EFFORTS NEEDED TO FULLY RESTORE THE BAY AND ITS TIDAL RIVERS ARE IN PLACE BY 2025, WITH CONTROLS, PRACTICES AND ACTIONS IN PLACE BY 2017 THAT WOULD ACHIEVE 60 PERCENT OF THE NECESSARY REDUCTIONS. THE TMDL IS SUPPORTED BY APPROPRIATE ACCOUNTABILITY MEASURES TO ENSURE CLEANUP COMMITMENTS ARE MET, INCLUDING SHORT-AND LONG-TERM BENCHMARKS, A TRACKING AND ACCOUNTING SYSTEM FOR JURISDICTION ACTIVITIES, AND FEDERAL CONTINGENCY ACTIONS THAT CAN BE EMPLOYED, IF NECESSARY, TO SPUR PROGRESS.  WORK WITH STATES AND LOCAL GOVERNMENTS TO REDUCE AND CONTROL POINT AND NONPOINT SOURCES OF NUTRIENT AND SEDIMENT POLLUTION TO ATTAIN WATER QUALITY CONDITIONS NECESSARY TO SUPPORT LIVING RESOURCES IN THE CHESAPEAKE BAY IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE CHESAPEAKE BAY TMDL AND THE ASSOCIATED WATERSHED IMPLEMENTATION PLANS.WORK WITH BAY PARTNERS, CONTINUE COMMUNICATION AND OUTREACH ACTIVITIES OF BAY RESTORATION EFFORTS AND WHAT CITIZENS CAN DO TO HELP SUPPORT THIS EFFORT. OPERATE A DATA CENTER IN SUPPORT OF INFORMATION AND ANALYSIS THAT SUPPORTS DECISION-MAKING, PLANNING, AND REPORTING BY STATE PARTNERS AND FEDERAL AGENCIES.CONTINUE COMPUTER MODELING AND SUPPORT TO THE STATES FOR WATER QUALITY MONITORING TO MEASURE PROGRESS TOWARD MEETING BAY RESTORATION GOALS.MAINTAIN AND IMPROVE A WATER QUALITY MONITORING NETWORK THAT ALLOWS THE EPA AND THE CHESAPEAKE BAY JURISDICTIONS TO EVALUATE THE EFFECTIVENESS OF PLANNED AND IMPLEMENTED ACTIONS IN REDUCING POLLUTION IN THE BAY AND ITS TRIBUTARIES.WORK WITH FEDERAL PARTNERS TO EXPAND THE UNDERSTANDING OF THE TOXIC CONTAMINANT PROBLEM IN THE BAY AND ITS WATERSHED AND DEVELOPING CONTAMINANT REDUCTION OUTCOMES AND STRATEGIES.SUPPORT OPERATIONS OF THE CHESAPEAKE BAY PROGRAM.OFFICE OF ENFORCEMENT AND COMPLIANCE ASSURANCEIN FY 2013, OECA WILL CONTINUE TO TARGET MOBILE SOURCES OF AIR DEPOSITION OF NITROGEN IN THE CHESAPEAKE BAY AIRSHED AND PURSUE APPROPRIATE ENFORCEMENT ACTIONS TO ENSURE COMPLIANCE.IN ADDITION, ENFORCEMENT RESOURCES WILL SUPPORT THE AGENCY?S PRIORITY TO RESTORE THE CHESAPEAKE BAY BY PROVIDING INFORMATION ABOUT WET WEATHER SOURCES OF POLLUTION.  THIS WILL RESULT IN AN INCREASE IN KNOWLEDGE, USE, TRANSPARENCY, AND PUBLIC ACCESS TO DATA ABOUT WET WEATHER SOURCES THROUGH: A) BROADENING THE ELECTRONIC REPORTING TOOL (ENOI) TO SUPPORT GETTING MORE NON-MAJOR PERMIT DATA (E.G., MULTI SECTOR GENERAL PERMIT DATA) INTO THE INTEGRATED COMPLIANCE INFORMATION SYSTEM (ICIS)-NPDES; B) IDENTIFYING THE MOST SIGNIFICANT SOURCES OF NONCOMPLIANCE AND DISCHARGES OF POLLUTANTS MOST RESPONSIBLE FOR THE IMPAIRMENT OF THIS IMPORTANT WATER BODY; AND, C) MAKING ALL NON-ENFORCEMENT CONFIDENTIAL DATA AVAILABLE, WITH EASY-TO-USE TOOLS TO AID IN THE PUBLIC'S ABILITY TO USE AND UNDERSTAND THE DATA.OFFICE OF ENVIRONMENTAL INFORMATIONDEVELOP AND IMPLEMENT THE CHESAPEAKE DATA ENTERPRISE, AN INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY INFRASTRUCTURE FOR ENABLING THE RAPID DISCOVERY OF AND ACCESS TO REGULATORY AND SCIENCE DATA REQUIRED FOR CARRYING OUT THE MISSION OF THE CHESAPEAKE BAY PROGRAM (CBP).  OEI WILL SUPPORTS THE ADVANCEMENT OF CHESAPEAKESTAT, A WEB-BASED DECISION SUPPORT TOOL DESIGNED TO PROVIDE ACCESS TO INFORMATION THAT WILL INFORM DECISIONS AND FACILITATE ADAPTIVE MANAGEMENT EFFORTS FOR THE BAY.  OFFICE OF GENERAL COUNSELLEGAL SERVICES, PROVIDED BY THE OFFICE OF GENERAL COUNSEL (OGC) AND THE OFFICES OF REGIONAL COUNSEL (ORCS), PRIMARILY CONSIST OF LEGAL REPRESENTATIONAL SERVICES, LEGAL COUNSELING, AND LEGAL SUPPORT FOR ALL EPA ENVIRONMENTAL ACTIVITIES.LEGAL SERVICES INCLUDE REPRESENTING EPA AND PROVIDING LITIGATION SUPPORT IN CASES WHERE EPA IS A DEFENDANT AS WELL AS THOSE CASES WHERE EPA IS NOT A DEFENDANT BUT MAY HAVE AN INTEREST IN THE CASE.  IT ALSO INCLUDES PROVIDING LEGAL ADVICE, COUNSEL, AND SUPPORT TO AGENCY MANAGEMENT AND PROGRAM OFFICES ON MATTERS INVOLVING ENVIRONMENTAL ISSUES INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO PROVIDING INTERPRETATION OF RELEVANT AND APPLICABLE LAWS, REGULATIONS, DIRECTIVES, POLICY AND GUIDANCE DOCUMENTS, AND OTHER MATERIALS.
Definition: THE CHESAPEAKE BAY PROGRAM INCLUDES THE STATES OF DELAWARE, MARYLAND, NEW YORK, VIRGINIA, PENNSYLVANIA, AND WEST VIRGINIA, THE DISTRICT OF COLUMBIA, THE CHESAPEAKE BAY COMMISSION, EPA AND OTHER FEDERAL PARTNERS WORKING TOGETHER TO PROTECT AND RESTORE THE BAY'S ECOSYSTEM.

Name: GEOGRAPHIC PROGRAM:  GULF OF MEXICO
Title: 65 - GEOGRAPHIC PROGRAM:  GULF OF MEXICO
Laws: CLEAN WATER ACT SECTION 104
Narrative: EFFORTS IN THIS PROGRAM SUPPORT THE PROTECTION AND RESTORATION OF THE COASTAL MARINE WATERS OF THE GULF OF MEXICO BY (1) PROTECTING HUMAN HEALTH AND THE FOOD SUPPLY; (2) MAINTAINING AND IMPROVING GULF HABITATS THAT SUPPORT LIVING RESOURCES; AND (3) MAINTAINING AND ENHANCING THE SUSTAINABILITY OF GULF LIVING RESOURCES. TO ACCOMPLISH THIS, ACTIVITIES ARE PRIMARILY FOCUSED ON THE FOLLOWING AREAS: REDUCING/PREVENTING EXCESSIVE NUTRIENT ENRICHMENT; PROTECTING PUBLIC HEALTH BY PREVENTING DISEASE AND REDUCING POLLUTION SOURCES; PROTECTING AND RESTORING IMPORTANT HABITAT; AND REDUCING ENVIRONMENTAL RISKS ASSOCIATED WITH THE INTRODUCTION OF HARMFUL NON-INDIGENOUS SPECIES.ASSIST THE GULF STATES IN IMPLEMENTING WATERSHED RESTORATION ACTIONS IN IMPAIRED COASTAL RIVER AND ESTUARY SEGMENTS.MINIMIZE ADVERSE HEALTH EFFECTS RESULTING FROM PATHOGENS ASSOCIATED WITH CONSUMPTION OF MOLLUSCAN BIVALVE SHELLFISH HARVESTED FROM THE GULF OF MEXICO.MINIMIZE EXPOSURE (INGESTION, INHALATION, AND PHYSICAL CONTACT) TO MARINE BIO- TOXINS IN COASTAL WATERS OF THE GULF OF MEXICO. .MINIMIZE EXPOSURE TO PATHOGENS THROUGH RECREATIONAL CONTACT IN COASTAL WATERS OF THE GULF OF MEXICO.MINIMIZE EXPOSURE TO TOXIC SUBSTANCES IN SEAFOOD HARVESTED FROM THE GULF OF MEXICO.PROTECT THE WATERS OF THE GULF OF MEXICO FROM THE DELETERIOUS EFFECTS OF NUTRIENT ENRICHMENT, FROM ALL CONTRIBUTING SOURCES, AND THEREBY ENHANCE BIODIVERSITY AND AESTHETIC, RECREATIONAL AND ECONOMIC BENEFITS.CREATE, PROTECT AND RESTORE IMPORTANT GULF OF MEXICO COASTAL AND MARINE HABITATS.REDUCE THE INTRODUCTION OF NON-INDIGENOUS AQUATIC SPECIES INTO THE GULF OF MEXICO.
Definition: A PARTNERSHIP OF THE FIVE GULF STATES, GULF COASTAL COMMUNITIES, CITIZENS, NONGOVERNMENTAL ORGANIZATIONS, AND FEDERAL AGENCIES WORKING TOGETHER TO INITIATE COOPERATIVE ACTIONS BY PUBLIC AND PRIVATE ORGANIZATIONS TO ACHIEVE SPECIFIC ENVIRONMENTAL RESULTS.

Name: GEOGRAPHIC PROGRAM:  LAKE CHAMPLAIN
Title: 66 - GEOGRAPHIC PROGRAM:  LAKE CHAMPLAIN
Laws: CLEAN WATER ACT SECTION 1201909 THE BOUNDARY WATERS TREATY; 1990 GREAT LAKES CRITICAL PROGRAMS ACT; 2002 GREAT LAKES AND LAKE CHAMPLAIN ACT; CLEAN WATER ACT; NORTH AMERICAN WETLANDS CONSERVATION ACT; U.S.-CANADA AGREEMENTS; NATIONAL HERITAGE AREAS ACT OF 2006; WATER RESOURCES DEVELOPMENT ACT  OF 2000 AND 2007.
Narrative: *REDUCE PHOSPHORUS INPUTS TO LAKE CHAMPLAIN TO PROMOTE A HEALTHY AND DIVERSE ECOSYSTEM AND PROVIDE FOR SUSTAINABLE HUMAN USE AND ENJOYMENT OF LAKE CHAMPLAIN.  *REDUCE CONTAMINANTS THAT POSE A RISK TO PUBLIC HEALTH AND THE LAKE CHAMPLAIN ECOSYSTEM.*MAINTAIN RESILIENT AND DIVERSE COMMUNITIES OF FISH, WILDLIFE, AND PLANTS. *PREVENT THE INTRODUCTION, LIMIT THE SPREAD, AND CONTROL THE IMPACT OF NON-NATIVE AQUATIC INVASIVE SPECIES TO PRESERVE THE INTEGRITY OF THE LAKE CHAMPLAIN ECOSYSTEM. *IDENTIFY POTENTIAL CHANGES IN CLIMATE AND DEVELOP APPROPRIATE ADAPTATION STRATEGIES TO MINIMIZE ADVERSE IMPACTS ON LAKE CHAMPLAIN?S ECOSYSTEM AND SOCIOECONOMIC RESOURCES. *PROMOTE HEALTHY AND DIVERSE ECONOMIC ACTIVITY AND SUSTAINABLE DEVELOPMENT PRINCIPLES WHILE IMPROVING WATER QUALITY AND CONSERVING NATURAL AND CULTURAL HERITAGE RESOURCES..
Definition: CREATE A COMPREHENSIVE POLLUTION PREVENTION, CONTROL, AND RESTORATION PLAN FOR PROTECTING THE FUTURE OF THE LAKE CHAMPLAIN BASIN AND IMPLEMENT THE PLAN.

Name: GEOGRAPHIC PROGRAM:  LONG ISLAND SOUND
Title: 67 - GEOGRAPHIC PROGRAM:  LONG ISLAND SOUND
Laws: CLEAN WATER ACT SECTIONS 119 AND 320LONG ISLAND SOUND RESTORATION ACT, P.L. 106-457 AS AMENDED BY P.L. 109-137; 33 U.S.C. 1269. LONG ISLAND SOUND STEWARDSHIP ACT, P.L. 109-353; 33 U.S.C. 1269.
Narrative: OVERSEE IMPLEMENTATION OF THE LONG ISLAND SOUND STUDY COMPREHENSIVE CONSERVATION AND MANAGEMENT PLAN BY COORDINATING THE CLEANUP AND RESTORATION ACTIONS OF THE LISS MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE AS AUTHORIZED UNDER SECTIONS 119 AND 320 OF THE CLEAN WATER ACT.
Definition: IMPLEMENTATION OF THE COMPREHENSIVE CONSERVATION AND MANAGEMENT PLAN FOR THE LONG ISLAND SOUND NATIONAL ESTUARY PROGRAM, THROUGH SUPPORT FOR THE LONG ISLAND SOUND OFFICE.

Name: GEOGRAPHIC PROGRAM:  OTHER
Title: 68 - GEOGRAPHIC PROGRAM:  OTHER
Laws: THE PONTCHARTRAIN BASIN RESTORATION ACT OF 2000; CLEAN WATER ACT; WATER RESOURCES DEVELOPMENT ACT OF 1996; WATER RESOURCES DEVELOPMENT ACT OF 2000;
Narrative: NORTHWEST FOREST PROGRAM: SUPPORTS INTERAGENCY COORDINATION, WATERSHED ASSESSMENT,   CONSERVATION, AND RESTORATION EFFORTS ACROSS SEVEN STATES IN THE PACIFIC NORTHWEST.  KEY ELEMENTS OF THE PROGRAM INCLUDE WATERSHED-SCALE MONITORING PROGRAMS THAT HELP CHARACTERIZE WATERSHED CONDITIONS ACROSS 72 MILLION ACRES OF FOREST SERVICE AND BUREAU OF LAND MANAGEMENT (BLM) ADMINISTERED LANDS IN THE NORTHWEST.  IN ADDITION TO PROVIDING STATUS AND TREND INFORMATION FOR AQUATIC AND RIPARIAN HABITATS, THE MONITORING PROGRAMS HELP SUPPORT ADAPTIVE MANAGEMENT AND STATE WATER QUALITY/WATERSHED HEALTH PROGRAMS.  THE LAKE PONTCHARTRAIN BASIN RESTORATION PROGRAM, STRIVES TO RESTORE ECOLOGICAL HEALTH BY DEVELOPING AND FUNDING RESTORATION PROJECTS WITHIN THE SIXTEEN PARISHES IN THE BASIN.  THE PROGRAM CONTINUES TO SUPPORT THE EFFORTS OF THE LAKE PONTCHARTRAIN BASIN FOUNDATION TO RESTORE AND PRESERVE THE WATER QUALITY, COAST, AND HABITATS OF THE ENTIRE LAKE PONTCHARTRAIN BASIN. THE LAKE PONTCHARTRAIN BASIN FOUNDATION (LPBF) CONDUCTS SAMPLING OF THE LAKE AND TRIBUTARY WATER QUALITY TO SUPPORT RELATED SCIENTIFIC AND PUBLIC EDUCATION PROJECTS.
Definition: EPA TARGETS EFFORTS TO PROTECT AND RESTORE VARIOUS COMMUNITIES AND ECOSYSTEMS IMPACTED BY ENVIRONMENTAL PROBLEMS.  UNDER THIS PROGRAM, THE AGENCY WORKS, DEVELOPS AND IMPLEMENTS COMMUNITY-BASED APPROACHES TO MITIGATE DIFFUSE SOURCES OF POLLUTION AND CUMULATIVE RISK FOR GEOGRAPHIC AREAS.  THE AGENCY ALSO FOSTERS COMMUNITY EFFORTS TO BUILD CONSENSUS AND MOBILIZE LOCAL RESOURCES TO TARGET HIGHEST RISKS.

Name: HOMELAND SECURITY:  COMMUNICATION AND INFORMATION
Title: 70 - HOMELAND SECURITY:  COMMUNICATION AND INFORMATION
Laws: NATIONAL OIL AND HAZARDOUS SUBSTANCES POLLUTION CONTINGENCY PLAN (NCP)COMPREHENSIVE ENVIRONMENTAL RESPONSE, COMPENSATION, AND LIABILITIES ACT (CERCLA); RCRA; SDWA; CWA; HOMELAND SECURITY ACT OF 2002; TSCA; BIO TERRORISM ACT OF 2003; POST-KATRINA EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT REFORM ACT;DEFENSE AGAINST WEAPONS OF MASS DESTRUCTION ACT (TITLE XIV OF PUBLIC LAW 104-201)
Narrative: OA: IN COORDINATION WITH THE EPA PROGRAM OFFICES, UPDATE AND MAINTAIN A HOMELAND SECURITY POLICY FOR PLANNING, PREPAREDNESS, RESPONSE AND RECOVERY FOR NATIONALLY SIGNIFICANT INCIDENTS.  EPA?S HOMELAND SECURITY EFFORTS WILL FOCUS ON MAINTAINING ITS PREPAREDNESS LEVEL, FILLING CRITICAL KNOWLEDGE AND TECHNOLOGY GAPS, AND WORKING WITH PARTNERS TO DEFINE COLLECTIVE CAPABILITIES AND LEVERAGE COMBINED RESOURCES TO CLOSE COMMON GAPS.  ENSURE THAT INTER-AGENCY INTELLIGENCE-RELATED PLANNING AND OPERATIONAL REQUIREMENTS ARE MET. THIS WILL BE ACHIEVED THROUGH COORDINATION WITH THE U.S. INTELLIGENCE COMMUNITY, INCLUDING THE OFFICE OF THE DIRECTOR FOR NATIONAL INTELLIGENCE, THE DEPARTMENT OF HOMELAND SECURITY, THE CENTRAL INTELLIGENCE AGENCY, THE NATIONAL SECURITY AGENCY, THE FEDERAL BUREAU OF INVESTIGATION, THE DEPARTMENT OF DEFENSE, AND THE WHITE HOUSE NATIONAL AND HOMELAND SECURITY COUNCILS;  AND TRACK EMERGING NATIONAL/HOMELAND SECURITY ISSUES IN ORDER TO ANTICIPATE AND AVOID CRISIS SITUATIONS AND TARGET THE AGENCY?S EFFORTS PROACTIVELY AGAINST THREATS TO THE UNITED STATES.FY 2012 RESOURCES WILL SUPPORT NATIONAL SECURITY EFFORTS THROUGH MONITORING ACROSS THE AGENCY?S IT INFRASTRUCTURE, TO DETECT, REMEDIATE AND ERADICATE MALICIOUS SOFTWARE OR ADVANCED PERSISTENT THREATS (APT) FROM EPA?S NETWORKS AND THROUGH IMPROVED DETECTION CAPABILITIES;   AND IMPROVE NATIONAL SECURITY EFFORTS, INCLUDING HEIGHTENED AWARENESS AND VIGILANCE ACROSS THE INFORMATION SECURITY COMMUNITY, BY INCREASING TRAINING AND AWARENESS OF THESE THREATS.
Definition: THIS PROGRAM ENSURES IMPLEMENTATION OF EPA?S HOMELAND SECURITY STRATEGY AND USE OF AN AGENCY-WIDE HOMELAND SECURITY COLLABORATIVE NETWORK. THIS PROGRAM ALSO DESIGNS, DEVELOPS, DEPLOYS AND MAINTAINS A SECURE AND STABLE INFRASTRUCTURE TO SUPPORT THE AGENCY?S CRITICAL COMMUNICATIONS AND DATA-TRANSFER DEMANDS IN THE EVENT OF A NATIONAL OR LOCAL DISASTER, AND SUPPORTS A DISPERSED WORKFORCE IN THE EVENT OF A LARGE-SCALE CATASTROPHIC INCIDENT, A CONTINUITY OF OPERATIONS (COOP) PLAN, OR PANDEMIC SITUATION.

Name: HOMELAND SECURITY:  CRITICAL INFRASTRUCTURE PROTECTION
Title: 71 - HOMELAND SECURITY:  CRITICAL INFRASTRUCTURE PROTECTION
Laws: PUBLIC HEALTH SECURITY AND BIOTERRORISM EMERGENCY AND RESPONSE ACT OF 2002SAFE DRINKING WATER ACTCLEAN WATER ACTOCEAN DUMPING ACTEMERGENCY PLANNING AND RIGHT TO KNOW ACT
Narrative: ASSIST DRINKING WATER AND WASTEWATER UTILITIES TO ASSESS THEIR VULNERABILITIES TO TERRORIST AND OTHER INTENTIONAL ACTS AND DEVELOP OR REVISE THEIR EMERGENCY RESPONSE PLANS ACCORDINGLY.SUPPORT SCIENTIFIC AND TECHNICAL ANALYSES ON METHODS AND TECHNOLOGIES THAT CAN DETECT AND TREAT AGENTS DELIBERATELY ADDED TO WATER AND WASTEWATER SYSTEMS AS WELL AS PROTECT THESE SYSTEMS FROM ATTACK.
Definition: THIS PROGRAM INVOLVES EPA ACTIVITIES THAT HELP PROTECT THE NATION?S CRITICAL PUBLIC INFRASTRUCTURE FROM TERRORIST THREATS.  EPA IS COMMITTED TO ASSESSING AND REDUCING VULNERABILITIES AND STRENGTHENING DETECTION AND RESPONSE CAPABILITIES FOR CRITICAL INFRASTRUCTURES.  EPA ASSISTS LOCAL DRINKING WATER AND WASTEWATER SYSTEMS IN ENSURING THAT THEY ARE SAFE FROM TERRORIST AND OTHER INTENTIONAL ACTS.  SCIENTIFIC EXPOSURE, HAZARD AND RISK DATA ON EXTREMELY HAZARDOUS CHEMICALS IS ALSO PROVIDED TO DIRECTLY SUPPORT CHEMICAL EMERGENCY PLANNING, RESPONSE, AND PREVENTION PROGRAMS.

Name: HOMELAND SECURITY:  PREPAREDNESS, RESPONSE, AND RECOVERY
Title: 72 - HOMELAND SECURITY:  PREPAREDNESS, RESPONSE, AND RECOVERY
Laws: CERCLA, SECTIONS 104, 105, 106 CLEAN WATER ACTOIL POLLUTION ACTVARIOUS PRESIDENTIAL DECISION DIRECTIVES
Narrative: DEVELOP AND MAINTAIN THE EPA?S HOMELAND SECURITY CAPABILITIES AND ASSETS IN BOTH HEADQUARTERS AND REGIONS AS FOLLOWS:DEVELOP AND IMPLEMENT EPA EMERGENCY PREPAREDNESS AND RESPONSE POLICIES TO MEET HOMELAND SECURITY REQUIREMENTS.IMPLEMENT THE EPA'S NATIONAL APPROACH TO RESPONSE (NAR) TO EFFECTIVELY MANAGE EMERGENCY RESPONSE ASSETS DURING LARGE-SCALE INCIDENTS. DEVELOP AND IMPLEMENT NATIONAL INCIDENT COORDINATION TEAM (NICT) AND REGIONAL INCIDENT COORDINATION TEAM (RICT) POLICIES AND ACTIVITIES TO ADDRESS MULTI-PROGRAM POLICY AND RESOURCE COORDINATION, INFORMATION SHARING, AND ISSUE RESOLUTION.ESTABLISH, OPERATE AND MAINTAIN THE EPA?S EMERGENCY OPERATIONS CENTER (EOC) AND REGIONAL EMERGENCY OPERATIONS CENTERS (REOC) TO ENSURE EFFECTIVE AND EFFICIENT RESPONSE OPERATIONS.SUSTAIN THE AGENCY'S RESPONDER BASE THROUGH SUPPORT OF THE RESPONSE SUPPORT CORPS (RSC).REPRESENT OR PARTICIPATE IN DEPARTMENT OF HOMELAND SECURITY AND OTHER FEDERAL AGENCY INTERAGENCY COMMITTEES, TASK FORCES AND WORKGROUPS.PROCURE, TEST, AND MAINTAIN DETECTION, MONITORING, RESPONSE AND PERSONAL PROTECTION EQUIPMENT AND GEAR TO ADDRESS CBR AGENTS, OR MULTIPLE LARGE-SCALE INCIDENTS.  INCLUDES THE SUPPORT OF THE AIRBORNE SPECTRAL PHOTOMETRIC ENVIRONMENTAL COLLECTION TECHNOLOGY (ASPECT) AIRCRAFT.PLAN, CONDUCT OR PARTICIPATE IN TRAINING AND EXERCISES RELATED TO CBR SUBSTANCES.  INCLUDES EPA, NATIONAL, REGIONAL, OR STATE AND LOCAL SPONSORED TRAINING AND EXERCISES.PROVIDE SUPPORT FOR THE INCIDENT MANAGEMENT ASSISTANCE TEAM (IMAT) WHICH ASSISTS EPA'S RESPONDERS DURING TERRORIST AND OTHER MAJOR INCIDENTS.ENHANCE ENVIRONMENTAL RESPONSE TEAM (ERT) (EDISON, CINCINNATI AND LAS VEGAS) CAPABILITIES AND ASSETS TO SUPPORT EPA?S RESPONSE TO CBR EVENTS. IMPLEMENT AN EMERGENCY INFORMATION MANAGEMENT PORTAL (EMP) WHICH CONSOLIDATES PREVENTION, PREPAREDNESS AND RESPONSE INFORMATION FOR EPA'S EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT COMMUNITY.DEVELOP AND MAINTAIN A LAB SUPPORT (CHEMICAL AND BIOLOGICAL) CAPABILITY AND CAPACITY TO SUPPORT ENVIRONMENTAL SAMPLING AND ANALYSIS ACTIVITIES DURING A RESPONSE.
Definition: DEVELOP AND MAINTAIN THE AGENCYWIDE CAPABILITY TO RESPOND TO CHEMICAL, BIOLOGICAL, AND RADIOLOGICAL (CBR) INCIDENTS.  ENSURE READINESS OF EPA PREPAREDNESS AND RESPONSE PERSONNEL THROUGH PLANNING, TRAINING, AND EXERCISES.  COORDINATE HOMELAND SECURITY ACTIVITIES WITH THE DEPARTMENT OF HOMELAND SECURITY AND OTHER FEDERAL AGENCIES TO ENSURE CONSISTENCY WITH THE NATIONAL RESPONSE FRAMEWORK.

Title: 73 - HOMELAND SECURITY:  PROTECTION OF EPA PERSONNEL AND INFRASTRUCTURE
Laws: PUBLIC HEALTH SECURITY AND BIOTERRORISM EMERGENCY AND RESPONSE ACT OF 2002
Narrative: ENSURE THE PHYSICAL SECURITY OF THE NATIONAL VEHICLES AND FUELS EMISSIONS LABORATORY.

THIS INVOLVES SAFEGUARDING EPA?S STAFF, ENSURING THE CONTINUITY OF EPA?S OPERATIONS, AND PROTECTING THE OPERATIONAL CAPABILITY OF EPA?S VITAL INFRASTRUCTURE ASSETS. Title: 76 - INDOOR AIR: RADON PROGRAM
Laws: RADON GAS AND INDOOR AIR QUALITY RESEARCH ACT OF TITLE IV OF THE SUPERFUND AMENDMENTS AND RE-AUTHORIZATION ACT OF 1986TOXIC SUBSTANCES CONTROL ACT (TSCA), SECTION 6, TITLES II, ANDTITLE III (15 U.S.C. 2605 AND 26412671)INDOOR RADON ABATEMENT ACT (IRAA), SECTION 306TOXIC SUBSTANCES CONTROL ACT (TSCA), SECTION 10CLEAN AIR ACT AMENDMENTSTITLE IV OF THE SUPERFUND AMENDMENTS & REAUTHORIZATION ACT OF 1986
Narrative: THIS PROGRAM/PROJECT CAN USE FUNDS TO OBTAIN SEE PROGRAM RESOURCES.EPM - HQPROVIDE ADVICE AND COUNSEL TO THE ADMINISTRATOR ON THE INDOOR ENVIRONMENTS RADON PROGRAM.PROVIDE EFFECTIVE POLICY, PROGRAM, AND MANAGEMENT GUIDANCE TO THE OFFICE OF AIR AND RADIATION PROGRAM, STAFF, AND REGIONAL OFFICES ON THE INDOOR ENVIRONMENTS RADON PROGRAM.PROVIDE ANALYSIS, PLANNING, BUDGETING, AND MANAGEMENT CAPABILITY TO ENSURE ANALYTIC SUPPORT TO REGIONAL, STATE, AND TRIBAL PROGRAMS FOR THE INDOOR ENVIRONMENTS RADON PROGRAM.PROMOTE ADOPTION OF LOCAL REAL ESTATE DISCLOSURE LAWS AND POLICIES AND WORK WITH THE REAL ESTATE COMMUNITY TO INCLUDE RADON TESTING AND DISCLOSURE IN RESIDENTIAL REAL ESTATE TRANSACTIONS.PROMOTE VOLUNTARY RADON-RESISTANT CONSTRUCTION AND NATIONAL, STATE AND LOCAL RADON-RESISTANT CODE ADOPTION TO AFFECT THE CONSTRUCTION OF NEW HOMES BUILT WITH RADON-RESISTANT FEATURES.PROVIDE OUTREACH ON RADON RISKS AND STEPS TO REDUCE EXPOSURE.IMPROVE SCIENTIFIC KNOWLEDGE AND TECHNOLOGIES TO SUPPORT AND DRIVE ACTION ON RADON.EPM - RTPROVIDE GRANTS OVERSIGHT FOR THE STATE INDOOR RADON GRANTS PROGRAM.
