COORDINATE THE NATIONAL RESPONSE TEAM (NRT) AND REGIONAL RESPONSE TEAM (RRT) ACTIVITIES AND WORKS WITH THE OTHER FEDERAL AGENCIES TO ENSURE EFFECTIVE, EFFICIENT AND CONSISTENT RESPONSE TO NATIONALLY SIGNIFICANT EVENTS.  ALSO ASSIST INDUSTRY IN PREVENTING RELEASES AND UNDERSTANDING THE CAUSE(S) OF ACCIDENTS WHEN THEY OCCUR.

Name: SUPERFUND:  FEDERAL FACILITIES
Title: C9 - SUPERFUND:  FEDERAL FACILITIES
Laws: COMPREHENSIVE ENVIRONMENTAL RESPONSE, COMPENSATION, AND LIABILITY ACT (CERCLA) OF 1980 (42 U.S. C. 9601 ET. SEQ.), AS AMENDED BY THE SUPERFUND AMENDMENTS AND REAUTHORIZATION ACT (SARA) OF 1986, SECTION 120RESOURCE CONSERVATION AND RECOVERY ACT (RCRA), AS AMENDED BY THE HAZARDOUS AND SOLID WASTE AMENDMENTS (HSWA) OF 1984, SECTION 7003NATIONAL ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION ACT (NEPA) OF 1970
Narrative: FACILITATE CLEANUP AND PROPERTY REUSE AT FEDERAL FACILITIES.PROVIDE OVERSIGHT OF REMOVAL, REMEDIAL, AND POST-REMEDIAL WORK CONDUCTED BY OTHER FEDERAL AGENCIES AT FEDERAL FACILITIES ; THIS MAY INCLUDE TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE, DOCUMENT REVIEW, AND STAKEHOLDER INVOLVEMENT ASSISTANCE TO OTHER FEDERAL AGENCIES. EXAMPLES OF THIS TYPE OF SUPPORT INCLUDE, BUT ARE NOT LIMITED TO:RESPONSE MANAGEMENT;COMMUNITY INVOLVEMENT;ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE AND TRIBAL SUPPORT;STATE AND LOCAL PROGRAM COORDINATION;REVIEW OF DECISION DOCUMENTS (I.E., RECORD OF DECISIONS, PROPOSED PLANS, FIVE-YEAR REVIEWS AND DELETION PACKAGES;CLEANUP CONTRACTS MANAGEMENT;TECHNOLOGY INNOVATION;SITE SAFETY AND EARLY ACTION WORK PLANS;COORDINATION WITH OTHER FEDERAL AGENCIES;MILITARY MUNITIONS TRAINING; ANDFACILITATING PROPERTY TRANSFER AND REUSE.PROVIDE ACCELERATED CLEANUP AND/OR PROPERTY TRANSFER ASSISTANCE TO OTHER FEDERAL AGENCIES AND PRIVATE PARTIES. EXAMPLES OF INTERAGENCY AGREEMENTS (IAS) THAT ARE CURRENTLY BEING MANAGED UNDER THE SUPERFUND FEDERAL FACILITIES PROGRAM:UNITED STATES COAST GUARD (USCG) IA -- TO ASSIST THE USCG WITH THE CLEANUP AND TRANSFER OF ITS SHORE FACILITIES, THE EPA REGIONS HAVE ENTERED INTO REGIONAL-SPECIFIC FUNDING AGREEMENTS WITH THE USCG TO SUPPORT ITS ACCELERATED TRANSFER EFFORT.UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF ENERGY (DOE) IA -- IN AN EFFORT TO EXPEDITE CLEANUP AND REUSE, DOE AND EPA HAS ENTERED INTO SEVERAL FUNDING AGREEMENTS FOR WORK BEING CONDUCTED AT SPECIFIC DOE SITES.
Definition: EPA?S SUPERFUND FEDERAL FACILITIES RESPONSE PROGRAM PROVIDES TECHNICAL AND REGULATORY OVERSIGHT AT FEDERAL FACILITIES, WHICH MAY INCLUDE FORMERLY USED DEFENSE SITES (FUDS), TO ENSURE PROTECTION OF HUMAN HEALTH AND THE ENVIRONMENT, EFFECTIVE PROGRAM IMPLEMENTATION, AND MEANINGFUL PUBLIC INVOLVEMENT, WHILE FACILITATING RESTORATION AND REUSE OF THE PROPERTIES.

Name: SUPERFUND:  REMEDIAL
Title: D2 - SUPERFUND:  REMEDIAL
Laws: COMPREHENSIVE ENVIRONMENTAL RESPONSE, COMPENSATION, AND LIABILITY ACT (CERCLA) OF 1980 (42 U.S. C. 9601 ET. SEQ.), AS AMENDED BY THE SUPERFUND AMENDMENTS AND REAUTHORIZATION ACT (SARA) OF 1986, SECTIONS 104, 105, 121
Narrative: EPA TRACKS THE STATE OF THE ART IN THE USE OF ANALYTICAL METHODS AND REMEDIATION TECHNOLOGIES, WORKING IN PARTNERSHIP WITH ACADEMIA, OTHER FEDERAL AGENCIES, AND INDUSTRY TO IDENTIFY AND DEPLOY PROMISING TECHNOLOGIES AND STRATEGIES.  THE PROGRAM DOCUMENTS SUCCESSFUL TECHNOLOGY APPLICATIONS, ENCOURAGES FIELD TRIALS OF EMERGING TECHNOLOGIES AND STRATEGIES, PROVIDES FIELD TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE FOR NEW APPROACHES, AND DELIVERS TRAINING TO EPA AND STATE PERSONNEL TO KEEP THEM ABREAST OF EMERGING INNOVATIONS.  THE PROGRAM ALSO PROVIDES INFRASTRUCTURE AND EQUIPMENT SUPPORT TO REGIONAL LABS TO MAINTAIN AND ENHANCE SUPERFUND?S ANALYTICAL CAPABILITIES.
Definition: PROVIDE SOUND SCIENCE AND CONTINUALLY INTEGRATE SMARTER TECHNICAL SOLUTIONS AND PROTECTION STRATEGIES THAT ENHANCE THE SUPERFUND REMEDIAL PROGRAM?S ABILITY TO PRESERVE LAND QUALITY AND REMEDIATE CONTAMINATED LAND FOR BENEFICIAL USE.

Name: SUPERFUND:  SUPPORT TO OTHER FEDERAL AGENCIES
Title: D3 - SUPERFUND:  SUPPORT TO OTHER FEDERAL AGENCIES
Laws: COMPREHENSIVE ENVIRONMENTAL RESPONSE, COMPENSATION, AND LIABILITY ACT (CERCLA) OF 1980 (42 U.S. C. 9601 ET. SEQ.), AS AMENDED BY THE SUPERFUND AMENDMENTS AND REAUTHORIZATION ACT (SARA) OF 1986, SECTIONS 104, 105, 120
Narrative: NATIONAL OCEANOGRAPHIC AND ATMOSPHERIC ADMINISTRATION (NOAA)  PROVIDES TECHNICAL SUPPORT DURING HAZARDOUS WASTE SITE INVESTIGATIONS, IDENTIFIES AND EVALUATES THE SEVERITY OF RISKS POSED TO NATURAL RESOURCES FROM HAZARDOUS WASTE SITES, AND EVALUATES STRATEGIES/METHODS OF MINIMIZING THOSE RISKS.   NOAA ASSISTS IN DEVELOPING AND CONDUCTING FIELD TESTING OF ADVANCED CHEMICAL SAMPLING AND ANALYTICAL EQUIPMENT USED FOR EFFICIENT RESPONSE OPERATIONS.  IN ADDITION, NOAA APPLIES NEW TECHNOLOGY AND INFORMATION TO IDENTIFY EFFECTIVE COUNTERMEASURES DURING RESPONSE OPERATIONS.U.S. COAST GUARD, DEPARTMENT OF HOMELAND SECURITY  UNDER CERCLA CONDUCTS AND MONITORS REMOVAL ACTIONS PERFORMED BY RESPONSIBLE PARTIES.  USCG MAINTAINS THE FIELD UNIT EMERGENCY RESPONSE CAPABILITY, PROVIDES ENFORCEMENT SUPPORT  AND MANAGES NATIONAL RESPONSE CENTER OPERATIONS.  THE USCG ALSO PROVIDES RESPONSE READINESS SUPPORT TO THE NATIONAL RESPONSE TEAM AND REGIONAL RESPONSE TEAMS.DEPARTMENT OF INTERIOR (DOI)  PROVIDES RESPONSE PREPAREDNESS AND MANAGEMENT ASSISTANCE TO THE NATIONAL RESPONSE TEAM/REGIONAL RESPONSE TEAMS (NRT/RRTS) AND PROVIDES TRUSTEE ASSISTANCE AND DAMAGE ASSESSMENT CAPABILITY (TA/DAC) WHICH BUILDS CAPACITY AMONG STATE AND FEDERAL TRUSTEE OFFICIALS FOR CONDUCTING NATURAL DAMAGE ASSESSMENTS RESULTING FROM HAZARDOUS SUBSTANCE RELEASES.OCCUPATIONAL SAFETY AND HEALTH ADMINISTRATION (OSHA), UNDER EXISTING SAFETY AND HEALTH STANDARDS, HAS THE PRIMARY RESPONSIBILITY FOR WORKER PROTECTION AT SUPERFUND SITES.  OSHA CARRIES OUT THIS RESPONSIBILITY BY INSPECTING SUPERFUND SITES FOR COMPLIANCE WITH OSHA STANDARDS AND PROVIDING EMPLOYERS, EMPLOYEES, AND OTHER ON-SITE PERSONNEL WITH THE MOST CURRENT TECHNICAL EXPERIENCE OR KNOWLEDGE IN THIS AREA.
Definition: RESOURCES SUPPORT OTHER FEDERAL AGENCIES THAT CONTRIBUTE TO THE SUPERFUND PROGRAM BY PROVIDING ESSENTIAL SERVICES IN AREAS WHERE EPA DOES NOT POSSESS THE REQUIRED SPECIALIZED EXPERTISE.

Name: SURFACE WATER PROTECTION
Title: D4 - SURFACE WATER PROTECTION
Laws: CLEAN WATER ACT SECTIONS:-     EFFLUENT GUIDELINES ? SECTIONS 301, 304, 306, 307, 501-    NPDES, PRETREATMENT, BIOSOLIDS - SECTIONS 104, 307, 402-    NONPOINT SOURCE - SECTIONS 104, 314, 319-    WATER QUALITY STANDARDS & CRITERIA - SECTIONS 104, 303, 304, 405-    ASSESSMENT AND MONITORING - SECTIONS 303(D), 305(B), 604(B)-EXECUTIVE ORDER 13626, ?GULF COAST ECOSYSTEM RESTORATION? AND OIL POLLUTION ACT OF 1990 , SECTION 1006
Narrative: MAJOR PROGRAM AREAS IN THIS PROGRAM INCLUDE TOTAL MAXIMUM DAILY LOADS (TMDL); WATER QUALITY MONITORING AND ASSESSMENT, NATIONAL WATER QUALITY DATA SYSTEMS, WATERSHED MANAGEMENT PLANNING, WATER QUALITY STANDARDS AND WATER QUALITY CRITERIA GUIDANCE AND STANDARDS, NATIONAL POLLUTANT DISCHARGE ELIMINATION SYSTEM (NPDES) PERMITTING, AND EFFLUENT GUIDELINES, AND OTHER TECHNOLOGY BASED POLLUTION CONTROLS AND NONPOINT SOURCE PROGRAM.  THE CLEAN WATER NEEDS SURVEY, MANAGEMENT AND OVERSIGHT OF THE CLEAN WATER STATE REVOLVING FUND AND SPECIAL NEEDS SUCH AS ALASKAN NATIVE VILLAGES, MANAGEMENT OF CONGRESSIONAL EARMARK PROJECTS, AND THE DEEPWATER HORIZON OIL SPILL NATURAL RESOURCE DAMAGE ASSESSMENT.MONITORING & ASSESSMENT WATERSHED PROTECTION, TMDLSTHROUGH THIS PROGRAM EPA MANAGES, CONDUCTS, AND SUPPORTS THE IDENTIFICATION AND TARGETING OF WATER BODIES FOR WATERSHED PROTECTION AND MANAGEMENT, THE DIAGNOSIS OF CAUSES OF WATER QUALITY PROBLEMS, AND THE DETERMINATION OF COST-EFFECTIVE LEVELS OF CONTROL REQUIRED TO MEET LOCAL WATER QUALITY OBJECTIVES. THE AGENCY PERIODICALLY PRODUCES A NATIONAL WATER QUALITY INVENTORY, UNDER SECTION 305(B) OF THE CLEAN WATER ACT, SUMMARIZING THE NATION?S WATER QUALITY BASED ON DATA SUBMITTED BY STATES. STATES ALSO PROVIDE INFORMATION TO EPA ON WATER BODIES THAT DO NOT MEET WATER QUALITY STANDARDS EVEN WHEN EFFLUENT LIMITATIONS PROVIDED FOR BY SECTION 301 OF CLEAN WATER ACT ARE MET. THIS LISTING OF WATERS, REQUIRED UNDER SECTION 303(D) OF THE CLEAN WATER ACT, IS A RANKING OF WATERS THAT TAKES INTO ACCOUNT THE SEVERITY OF THE POLLUTION AND THE USES TO BE MADE OF SUCH WATERS. FOR THESE WATERS, THE AGENCY DEVELOPS A TMDL CALCULATION THAT CONSIDERS POINT SOURCE POLLUTION, NONPOINT SOURCE POLLUTION, AND A FACTOR OF SAFETY SUCH THAT THE APPLICABLE WATER QUALITY STANDARD WILL BE ACHIEVED.THIS PROGRAM ALSO SUPPORTS PROGRAMS BY STATES, TRIBES AND OTHER STAKEHOLDERS IN DEVELOPING AND IMPLEMENTING WATERSHED-BASED PLANS TO PROTECT AQUATIC RESOURCES. WATERSHED ASSISTANCE GRANTS SUPPORT WATERSHED ORGANIZATION EFFORTS TO ENABLE LOCAL PARTNERSHIPS TO PROTECT AND RESTORE WATERSHEDS. ESTABLISH WATER QUALITY MONITORING AND ASSESSMENT PROGRAMS FOR STATES AND TRIBES, APPROPRIATE TO THEIR IDENTIFIED GOALS AND NEEDS, INCLUDING ADDRESSING THE ELEMENTS OUTLINED IN EPA?S MONITORING GUIDANCE.WORK WITH STAKEHOLDERS TO CONTINUE DEVELOPMENT OF DATA AND ENTRY INTO NATIONAL SYSTEMS, INCLUDING STORET.  (HQ ONLY)WORK WITH STATES TO IMPLEMENT TMDL PROGRAMS THAT MEET THE REQUIREMENTS OF SECTION 303 (D), INCLUDING TIMELY SUBMISSION OF APPROVABLE LISTS AND DEVELOPMENT OF TMDLS AT AN APPROPRIATE PACE AND WHERE PRACTICABLE ON A WATERSHED BASIS.ENCOURAGE AND ASSIST WATERSHED MANAGEMENT PROGRAMS FOR STATES, TRIBES AND OTHER STAKEHOLDERS, AND SUPPORT DEVELOPMENT AND IMPLEMENTATION OF PLANS TO RESTORE AND MAINTAIN THE HEALTH OF AQUATIC COMMUNITIES ON A PRIORITY BASIS.SERVE AS TRUSTEE FOR THE DEEPWATER HORIZON OIL SPILL PURSUANT TO EXECUTIVE ORDER 13626 REGARDING NATURAL RESOURCE DAMAGE ASSESSMENT AND RESTORATION IN CONNECTION WITH INJURY TO, DESTRUCTION OF, LOSS OF OR LOSS OF USE OF NATURAL RESOURCES, INCLUDING THEIR SUPPORTING ECOSYSTEMS, RESULTING FROM THE OIL SPILL.PREPARE BIENNIAL REPORTS TO CONGRESS FOR THE NATIONAL SEDIMENT QUALITY SURVEY.  (HQ ONLY)WATER QUALITY STANDARDSDEVELOP NATIONAL WATER QUALITY CRITERIA RECOMMENDATIONS AND GUIDANCE TO ADDRESS CHEMICAL POLLUTANTS, BIOLOGICAL CONDITIONS, PHYSICAL CHARACTERISTICS (E.G., SEDIMENTATION) BACTERIA AND NUTRIENTS TO PROTECT THE HIGHEST ATTAINABLE USES AND TO SUPPORT WATERSHED-BASED APPROACHES.  THIS INFORMATION ASSISTS STATES AND TRIBES TAILOR THEIR WATER QUALITY STANDARDS TO REFLECT UNIQUE LOCAL CONDITIONS.APPROVE/DISAPPROVE STATE/TRIBAL WATER QUALITY STANDARDS AND PROMPTLY PROPOSE AND PROMULGATE FEDERAL STANDARDS WHERE STATES AND TRIBES FAIL TO MEET CWA REQUIREMENTS.COORDINATE WITH OTHER FEDERAL AGENCIES, PARTICULARLY ON THE ENDANGERED SPECIES ACT AND NATIONAL HISTORIC PRESERVATION ACT, TO STREAMLINE CONSULTATION WHILE FURTHER INTEGRATING THE PROTECTION OF BIOLOGICAL RESOURCES INTO THE WATER PROGRAMS (WATER QUALITY STANDARDS, TMDLS AND NPDES PROGRAM).NONPOINT SOURCE CONTROLSUPPORT EFFORTS OF STATES, TRIBES, OTHER FEDERAL AGENCIES, AND LOCAL COMMUNITIES TO DEVELOP AND IMPLEMENT WATERSHED-BASED PLANS THAT SUCCESSFULLY ADDRESS ALL OF THESE FACTORS TO ENABLE IMPAIRED WATERS TO BE RESTORED THROUGH THE NATIONAL NONPOINT SOURCE PROGRAM (SECTION 319) WHILE ALSO CONTINUING TO PROTECT THOSE WATERS THAT ARE HEALTHY.ENHANCE ACCOUNTABILITY FOR RESULTS THROUGH THE USE OF EPA?S NONPOINT SOURCE PROGRAM GRANTS TRACKING SYSTEM, WHICH WILL CONTINUE TO TRACK ALL POLLUTANT LOAD REDUCTIONS ACHIEVED BY EACH PROJECT. FOCUSING ON THE DEVELOPMENT AND DISSEMINATION OF NEW TOOLS TO PROMOTE LOW IMPACT DEVELOPMENT (LID), THEREBY PREVENTING NEW NONPOINT SOURCES OF POLLUTION. IMPLEMENTING THE HEALTHY WATERSHEDS STRATEGY, IN COOPERATION WITH STATES, ACADEMIA, AND NON-GOVERNMENTAL ORGANIZATIONS, THAT FOCUSES ON PROTECTION OF THE WATERSHEDS OF HEALTHY WATERS (AS WELL AS HEALTHY COMPONENTS OF OTHER WATERSHEDS).  CONTINUING COORDINATION WITH THE U.S. DEPARTMENT OF AGRICULTURE TO ENSURE THAT FEDERAL RESOURCES, INCLUDING GRANTS UNDER SECTION 319 AND FARM BILL FUNDS, ARE MANAGED IN A COORDINATED WAY TO MAXIMIZE WATER QUALITY IMPROVEMENT IN IMPAIRED WATERS AND PROTECTION IN ALL OTHERS.POLLUTANT LOADING REDUCTIONS AND NATIONAL REGULATIONSREVIEW RISK ASSESSMENT METHODOLOGIES APPLIED TO CHEMICAL POLLUTANTS AND PATHOGENS IN BIOSOLIDS GENERATED BY WASTEWATER TREATMENT PLANTS, ASSESS THE NEED FOR NEW OR REVISED STANDARDS TO PROTECT PUBLIC HEALTH AND THE ENVIRONMENT. PROVIDE FOR THE SAFE BENEFICIAL USE AND DISPOSAL OF MUNICIPAL BIOSOLIDS.PROVIDE TECHNICAL, ECONOMIC, AND BENEFITS INFORMATION AND TOOLS WHICH ENABLE FEDERAL, STATE AND LOCAL ENTITIES TO REDUCE POLLUTANT LOADINGS POSING ENVIRONMENTAL AND HUMAN HEALTH RISK AND TAKE APPROPRIATE ACTIONS TO PROTECT THE HEALTH OF AQUATIC SYSTEMS FROM INTAKE PRACTICES OF POWER GENERATING AND OTHER INDUSTRIAL FACILITIES.IDENTIFY INDUSTRIAL CATEGORIES WHERE DISCHARGES TO WATER BODIES OR RELEASES TO POTWS POSE A SIGNIFICANT RISK TO WATER QUALITY AND DETERMINE WHETHER TO DEVELOP NEW NATIONAL POLLUTION CONTROL REGULATIONS, REVISE EXISTING REGULATIONS OR DEVELOP OTHER CONTROL TOOLS.ENSURE FACILITIES REQUIRED TO HAVE NPDES PERMITS ARE COVERED BY CURRENT, EFFECTIVE PERMIT THAT ENSURE WATER QUALITY PROTECTION AND ATTAINMENT OF STATUTORY TECHNOLOGICAL REQUIREMENTS.ENSURE POTWS REQUIRED TO OPERATE PRETREATMENT PROGRAMS ARE AUDITED AS REQUIRED TO ENSURE THEY CARRYOUT OBLIGATIONS TO PREVENT PASS THROUGH OF POLLUTANTS, PREVENT INTERFERENCE WITH THE TREATMENT WORKS OPERATION OR USE OR DISPOSAL OF BIOSOLIDS, AND IMPROVE OPPORTUNITIES TO RECYCLE AND RECLAIM WASTEWATER AND BIOSOLIDS.DEVELOP REGULATIONS AND POLICIES, WHICH WILL IMPROVE AND STRENGTHEN IMPLEMENTATION OF THE NPDES PROGRAM THROUGH STREAMLINING, INNOVATION, PROGRAM INTEGRATIONS, AND COORDINATED INFORMATION MANAGEMENT. (HQ ONLY)ENSURE THAT STATES/TRIBES HAVE THE CAPACITY TO AND ARE ADMINISTERING EFFECTIVE NPDES PROGRAMS, BY ASSESSING STATE PROGRAM HEALTH, IMPLEMENTING ELECTRONIC REPORTING, PROVIDING ASSISTANCE AND TRAINING IN VARIOUS PROGRAM AREAS.DEVELOP POLICY AND GUIDANCE THAT ADDRESSES UNREGULATED OR INADEQUATELY REGULATED POINT SOURCES IN THE AREA OF MINING, PESTICIDES, INVASIVE SPECIES, CRUISE SHIPS, AND OTHER SOURCES AS THEY ARE IDENTIFIED AND WORK WITH REGIONS, STATES, AND TRIBES TO IMPLEMENT.DEVELOP INFORMATION AND GUIDANCE ON INDIVIDUAL ON-SITE AND DECENTRALIZED WASTEWATER TREATMENT SYSTEMS TO HELP STATES ASSIST COMMUNITIES TO ABATE EXISTING POLLUTION AND PUBLIC HEALTH PROBLEMS AND TO EFFECTIVELY PLAN POLLUTION CONTROL FOR NEW DEVELOPMENT. (HQ ONLY)COLLECT CAPITAL NEEDS INFORMATION FOR TREATMENT OF WASTEWATER, STORM WATER AND NON POINT SOURCES OF POLLUTION REQUIRED TO MEET ECOLOGICAL AND HUMAN HEALTH STANDARDS, AND PERIODICALLY REPORT THAT INFORMATION TO THE CONGRESS.DEVELOP APPROACHES THAT IMPROVE CONTROL OF UNREGULATED OR INADEQUATELY REGULATED NONPOINT SOURCES BY SUPPORTING WATERSHED PLANNING. (HQ ONLY)DEVELOP REGULATIONS AND GUIDANCE THAT IMPROVE CONTROL OF UNREGULATED OR INADEQUATELY REGULATED CONCENTRATED ANIMAL FEEDING OPERATIONS AND WORK WITH REGIONS, STATES AND TRIBES TO IMPLEMENT.DEVELOP REGULATIONS AND/OR GUIDANCE TO IMPROVE CONTROL OF UNREGULATED OR INADEQUATELY REGULATED URBAN WET WEATHER POINT SOURCES (CSOS, STORM WATER, AND SSOS) AND WORK WITH REGIONS, STATES, AND TRIBES TO IMPLEMENT.CLEAN WATER STATE REVOLVING FUND, FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE, CONSTRUCTION GRANTSSAFEGUARD THE FISCAL INTEGRITY AND LONG-TERM VIABILITY OF ALL 51 SRF PROGRAMS BY ENSURING PROPER AUDIT COVERAGE, SOUND FUND MANAGEMENT AND HIGH PACE OF LOAN ISSUANCE, IN ORDER TO PROVIDE LONG-TERM ANNUAL ASSISTANCE.DIRECT SRF FUNDS TOWARD THE HIGHEST PRIORITY WATER QUALITY PROJECTS BY IMPLEMENTING INTEGRATED PRIORITY SYSTEMS AND PROMOTING USE OF THE SRF TO ADDRESS PRIORITY WATER QUALITY NEEDS INCLUDING NON POINT SOURCE AND ESTUARY PROJECTS.ADMINISTER PROGRAMS TO PROVIDE FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE TO STATES, TRIBES AND LOCALITIES TO IMPROVE WATER AND WASTEWATER INFRASTRUCTURE IN SUPPORT OF ENVIRONMENTAL AND HEALTH GOALS.CONTINUE TO MANAGE CONSTRUCTION GRANTS CLOSE OUT PROCESS.OA - NRDA FUNDSEPA IS ONE TRUSTEE (UNDER THE OIL POLLUTION ACT) EVALUATING THE NRDA CLAIM FOR THE BP DEEPWATER HORIZON OIL SPILL.  THE TRUSTEES HAVE A MANDATE TO RESTORE, REHABILITATE, REPLACE OR ACQUIRE THE EQUIVALENT OF THE NATURAL RESOURCES DAMAGED BY THE SPILL.  TO MEET THIS MANDATE, THE TRUSTEES SEEK TO RESTORE INJURED RESOURCES AND SERVICES TO BASELINE (THE CONDITION THEY WOULD HAVE BEEN IN HAD THE SPILL NOT OCCURRED) AND TO COMPENSATE THE PUBLIC FOR INTERIM LOSSES (THE LOSSES THAT OCCUR DURING THE TIME IT TAKES THE RESOURCES TO RECOVER TO BASELINE). ENJOYMENT OF ?HUMAN USE? ACTIVITIES SUCH AS BOATING, FISHING, HUNTING, SWIMMING, SUNBATHING AND WILDLIFE VIEWING MAY BE DIMINISHED, NOT ONLY BY THE PRESENCE OF OIL BUT ALSO BY THE THREAT OF OIL AND BY RESPONSE ACTIVITIES.  LOSS OF THESE ACTIVITIES IS A COMPENSABLE INJURY  WHEN THE PUBLIC?S ACCESS TO THE ENJOYMENT OF THEIR NATURAL RESOURCES IS DENIED OR RESTRICTED IN SOME WAY DUE TO AN OIL SPILL.THE TRUSTEES ARE EMPLOYING A VARIETY OF EFFORTS FOR ESTIMATING THE DIMINISHED HUMAN USE AND LOST VALUE FROM THE OIL SPILL. OA PERSONNEL WOULD MONITOR AND COMMENT ON THESE ASSESSMENT ACTIVITIES AND WILL REVIEW THESE ESTIMATES AS THEY ARE FINALIZED. THEY WOULD ALSO ASSIST WITH THE DEVELOPMENT OF BENEFIT COST RATIOS FOR EARLY RESTORATION PROJECTS AND WILL PROVIDE TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE ON SPECIFIC RESTORATION PROJECTS.
Definition: PROTECT AND RESTORE THE QUALITY OF SURFACE WATERS (STREAMS, RIVERS, LAKES). INCLUDE WATER QUALITY STANDARDS AND CRITERIA, EFFLUENT GUIDELINES, COOLING WATER INTAKE REGULATIONS, WATER QUALITY ASSESSMENT AND MONITORING, TOTAL MAXIMUM DAILY LOADS (TMDLS), NATIONAL POLLUTANT DISCHARGE ELIMINATION SYSTEM (NPDES), AND NONPOINT SOURCES.

Name: TOXIC SUBSTANCES:  CHEMICAL RISK MANAGEMENT
Title: D5 - TOXIC SUBSTANCES:  CHEMICAL RISK MANAGEMENT
Laws: POLLUTION PREVENTION ACT (PPA)TOXIC SUBSTANCES CONTROL ACT (TSCA)ASBESTOS SCHOOL HAZARD ABATEMENT ACT (ASHAA)ASBESTOS HAZARD EMERGENCY RESPONSE ACT (AHERA)ASBESTOS INFORMATION ACT
Narrative: NATIONAL PROGRAM CHEMICALS:  MOST CHEMICALS WERE INTRODUCED INTO COMMERCE BEFORE THE RISKS WERE KNOWN.  A NUMBER OF THESE CHEMICALS ARE BOTH PREVALENT AND HIGH-RISK.  THE AGENCY HAS ESTABLISHED A NATIONAL PROGRAM TO MANAGE REDUCTIONS IN USE, SAFE REMOVAL, DISPOSAL OR CONTAINMENT OF THESE CHEMICALS, AS APPROPRIATE.SIGNIFICANT RISKS ARE WELL ESTABLISHED FOR POLYCHLORINATED BIPHENYLS (PCBS), ASBESTOS, AND DIOXIN, FOR EXAMPLE, AND REDUCTIONS IN USE AND RELEASES HAVE BEEN IMPORTANT TO REDUCING EXPOSURE OF THE GENERAL POPULATION AND SENSITIVE SUBPOPULATIONS.  THE AGENCY WILL ALSO PURSUE OPPORTUNITIES FOR RISK REDUCTION FOR MERCURY, AND FOR CERTAIN INDUSTRIAL FIBERS THAT MAY POSE RISKS IN THE WORKPLACE.PERSISTENT BIOACCUMULATIVE TOXICS INITIATIVE (PBTI):  TO ADDRESS CONTINUING ISSUES ASSOCIATED WITH PBTS, EPA LAUNCHED A CROSS-OFFICE, CROSS-MEDIA PBT PROGRAM IN FY 1999.  THROUGH THIS EFFORT, THE AGENCY SEEKS TO PREVENT, MINIMIZE AND, WHEN POSSIBLE, ELIMINATE PBTS, WHICH ARE HARMFUL TO BOTH HUMAN HEALTH AND THE ENVIRONMENT.  THE INITIATIVE?S CROSS-MEDIA APPROACH IS DESIGNED TO STOP THE TRANSFER OF PBT POLLUTANTS ACROSS MEDIA USING ALL OF EPA?S TOOLS: REGULATORY, METHODS TO ENSURE COMPLIANCE, RESEARCH, VOLUNTARY ACTIONS, PREVENTION, AND INTERNATIONAL NEGOTIATIONS.
Definition: NATIONAL PROGRAMS TO MANAGE RISKS OF CHEMICALS INCLUDING POLYCHLORINATED BIPHENYLS (PCBS), ASBESTOS, MERCURY AND DIOXINS, THROUGH REDUCTIONS IN USE, SAFE REMOVAL, DISPOSAL OR CONTAINMENT.

Name: TOXIC SUBSTANCES:  CHEMICAL RISK REVIEW AND REDUCTION
Title: D6 - TOXIC SUBSTANCES:  CHEMICAL RISK REVIEW AND REDUCTION
Laws: POLLUTION PREVENTION ACT (PPA)TOXIC SUBSTANCES CONTROL ACT (TSCA)
Narrative: NEW CHEMICALS PROGRAM:  THE TOXIC SUBSTANCES CONTROL ACT (TSCA) REQUIRES EPA TO REVIEW A CHEMICAL OR MICROORGANISM BEFORE IT IS MANUFACTURED COMMERCIALLY (I.E., A NEW CHEMICAL) TO DETERMINE WHETHER IT CAN BE HANDLED AND USED SAFELY.  SINCE 1979, EPA HAS REVIEWED MORE THAN 39,000 PRE-MANUFACTURING NOTIFICATIONS (PMNS) AND TAKEN ACTIONS TO CONTROL RISKS FOR ABOUT 10 PERCENT OF THESE CHEMICALS AND MICROORGANISMS.  (HQ)THE AGENCY LAUNCHED IN 2003, SUSTAINABLE FUTURES, A PROGRAM WHICH OFFERS AN EXPEDITED PRE-MANUFACTURING NOTIFICATION PROCESS TO COMPANIES WHO TAKE TRAINING IN THE USE OF THE METHODS AND APPLY THE RESULTS TOWARD DEVELOPMENT OF SAFER CHEMICALS (HQ)THE NEW CHEMICALS PROGRAM ALSO EXAMINES NEW MICROORGANISMS DERIVED FROM BIOTECHNOLOGY TO ENSURE THAT POTENTIAL RISKS HAVE BEEN EVALUATED AND THAT ADEQUATE CONTROLS ARE IN PLACE BEFORE THEY ARE RELEASED INTO THE ENVIRONMENT.  (HQ)EXISTING CHEMICALS PROGRAM:  ONE OF EPA?S CRITICAL RESPONSIBILITIES UNDER TSCA IS TO IDENTIFY AND CONTROL ANY UNREASONABLE RISKS THAT MIGHT BE ASSOCIATED WITH THE THOUSANDS OF CHEMICALS WHICH ARE ALREADY IN COMMERCE.   EPA?S STRATEGY FOR ADDRESSING THE REMAINING CHEMICALS IN COMMERCE IS TO FOSTER THE PUBLIC AVAILABILITY OF RISK SCREENING INFORMATION TO ALLOW STATES, COMMUNITIES, INDUSTRY, AND THE PUBLIC TO ACT ON THEIR OWN AND IN CONCERT WITH EPA TO REDUCE POTENTIAL RISKS POSED BY THESE CHEMICALS.  (HQ AND RT)IN THE EXISTING CHEMICALS PROGRAM EPA WILL FOCUS ON THESE AREAS: OBTAINING NEEDED DATA - EPA WILL CONTINUE OBTAINING DATA NEEDED TO ASSESS THE SAFETY OF HPV CHEMICALS, INCLUDING CRITICAL CHEMICAL HAZARD AND FATE DATA DEFINED BY THE INTERNATIONALLY RECOGNIZED SCREENING INFORMATION DATA SET (SIDS) AND PRODUCTION AND USE DATA OBTAINED THROUGH TSCA INVENTORY UPDATE REPORTING (IUR).ASSESSING CHEMICALS FOR HAZARDS AND RISKS - EPA WILL CONTINUE USING OBTAINED AND AVAILABLE DATA TO ASSESS THE SAFETY OF EXISTING CHEMICALS, INCLUDING DEVELOPING SCREENING-LEVEL HAZARD CHARACTERIZATIONS FOR HPV CHEMICALS.  MANAGING CHEMICAL RISKS - EPA PLANS TO INITIATE OR CONTINUE RISK MANAGEMENT ACTIONS TO REDUCE HUMAN HEALTH AND ENVIRONMENTAL RISKS POSED BY A NUMBER OF PREVIOUSLY AND NEWLY IDENTIFIED PRIORITY EXISTING CHEMICALS USING TSCA REGULATORY AUTHORITIES AND OTHER STRATEGIES.
Definition: PROGRAMS TO IDENTIFY AND CONTROL ANY UNREASONABLE RISKS WITH EXISTING CHEMICALS AND TO ENSURE THAT NEW CHEMICALS CAN BE HANDLED AND USED SAFELY.

Name: TOXIC SUBSTANCES:  LEAD RISK REDUCTION PROGRAM
Title: D7 - TOXIC SUBSTANCES:  LEAD RISK REDUCTION PROGRAM
Laws: TOXIC SUBSTANCES CONTROL ACT (TSCA)RESIDENTIAL LEAD-BASED PAINT HAZARD REDUCTION ACT OF 1992    (TITLE IV OF TSCA)
Narrative: EPA IS PART OF THE FEDERAL EFFORT TO ADDRESS LEAD POISONING AND ELEVATED BLOOD LEVELS IN CHILDREN BY ASSISTING IN, AND IN SOME CASES GUIDING, FEDERAL ACTIVITIES AIMED AT REDUCING THE EXPOSURE OF CHILDREN IN HOMES WITH LEAD-BASED PAINT.  THE CONCENTRATION OF LEAD IN A CHILD'S BLOOD IS TYPICALLY USED AS AN INDEX OF LEAD EXPOSURE.  OVER TIME, INCREASED SCIENTIFIC EVIDENCE OF HARMFUL EFFECTS HAS LED TO CONCERN ABOUT BLOOD-LEAD LEVELS ONCE THOUGHT TO BE SAFE.EPA HAS FOCUSED ON REDUCING CHILDREN?S EXPOSURE TO LEAD IN PAINT AND DUST THROUGH A REGULATORY FRAMEWORK TO IMPROVE WORK PRACTICES ASSOCIATED WITH LEAD-BASED PAINT AND BY EDUCATING PARENTS AND THE MEDICAL COMMUNITY ABOUT THE EFFECTS OF LEAD POISONING AND STEPS THAT CAN BE TAKEN TO PREVENT IT.EPA WILL COLLABORATE WITH INDUSTRY ON A CAMPAIGN TO INCREASE LEAD-SAFE WORK PRACTICES IN HOME RENOVATION AND REMODELING AND TO IMPROVE HANDLING OF LEAD PAINT ON BUILDINGS AND STRUCTURES THROUGH MARKET-BASED INCENTIVES AND OTHER INNOVATIVE APPROACHES.CONTRACTORS PERFORMING RENOVATION, REPAIR AND PAINTING PROJECTS THAT DISTURB LEAD-BASED PAINT IN HOMES, CHILD CARE FACILITIES, AND SCHOOLS BUILT BEFORE 1978 MUST BE CERTIFIED AND MUST FOLLOW SPECIFIC WORK PRACTICES TO PREVENT LEAD CONTAMINATION.
Definition: PROGRAM TO REDUCE CHILDREN?S EXPOSURE TO LEAD FROM LEAD-BASED PAINT THROUGH REGULATIONS, CERTIFICATION AND TRAINING PROGRAMS AND PUBLIC OUTREACH EFFORTS.

Name: TRI / RIGHT TO KNOW
Title: D8 - TRI / RIGHT TO KNOW
Laws: SECTION 313 OF THE EMERGENCY PLANNING AND COMMUNITY RIGHT-TO-KNOW ACT OF 1986 (EPCRA)SECTION 6607 OF THE POLLUTION PREVENTION ACT OF 1990 (PPA)
Narrative: HQ ?EPMTHE TRI PROGRAM PROVIDES THE PUBLIC WITH INFORMATION ON THE RELEASES AND OTHER WASTE MANAGEMENT OF TOXIC CHEMICALS.  TWO LAWS, SECTION 313 OF THE EMERGENCY PLANNING AND COMMUNITY RIGHT-TO-KNOW ACT (EPCRA) AND SECTION 6607 OF THE POLLUTION PREVENTION ACT (PPA), MANDATE THAT EPA ANNUALLY COLLECT INFORMATION ON LISTED TOXIC CHEMICALS FROM CERTAIN INDUSTRIES AND MAKE THE INFORMATION AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC THROUGH VARIOUS MEANS, INCLUDING A PUBLICLY ACCESSIBLE NATIONAL DATABASE.  USING THIS INFORMATION, CITIZENS, BUSINESSES, COMMUNITY GROUPS, RESEARCHERS, AND GOVERNMENTS CAN WORK TOGETHER TO BETTER PROTECT THE ENVIRONMENT.  EPA WILL CONTINUE TO FACILITATE ELECTRONIC REPORTING BY FACILITIES BY PROVIDING ON-LINE TRI-MEWEB REPORTING SOFTWARE, WEB-BASED TRAINING MODULES AND A TELEPHONE HOTLINE.  EPA WILL ALSO CONTINUE ITS EFFORTS TO ENHANCE THE PROGRAM ? ADDING CHEMICALS AND/OR INDUSTRY SECTORS THROUGH RULEMAKINGS, AS APPROPRIATE ? IN ORDER TO PROVIDE COMMUNITIES WITH MORE COMPLETE INFORMATION ON TOXIC CHEMICAL RELEASES AND OTHER WASTE MANAGEMENT IN THEIR SURROUNDINGS.  THE ACCOMPLISHMENT OF THESE ACTIVITIES MAY BE SUPPORTED THROUGH GRANTS OR COOPERATIVE AGREEMENTS WITH NON-GOVERNMENTAL ORGANIZATIONS.  REGIONS - EPMTHE REGIONS ASSIST IN THE IMPLEMENTATION OF THE EPCRA AND PPA, ENGAGE IN INDUSTRY OUTREACH TO ASSURE THAT THE REGULATED COMMUNITY UNDERSTANDS HOW TO REPORT THEIR DATA FOR TRI, AND HELP IN ANALYZING THE REPORTED TRI DATA.
Definition: SUPPORTS PUBLIC ACCESS TO ENVIRONMENTAL DATA FROM CERTAIN INDUSTRIAL FACILITIES AND TOOL DEVELOPMENT FOR THE USE OF THAT DATA AS WELL AS SCIENTIFIC AND ECONOMIC SUPPORT FOR THE ANALYSIS AND USE OF TRI PROGRAM.  PROGRAMS ARE DESIGNED TO INFORM THE PUBLIC OF THEIR COMMUNITIES' ENVIRONMENTAL CONDITIONS.

Name: TRIBAL
Title: D9 - TRIBAL - CAPACITY BUILDING
Laws: ANNUAL APPROPRIATION ACTS; INDIAN ENVIRONMENTAL GENERAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAM (GAP) ACT OF 1992; PPA; FIFRA; CAA; TSCA; NEPA; CWA; SDWA; RCRA; CERCLA; NAFTA; MPRSA; INDOOR RADON ABATEMENT ACT; OPA; AND ADDITIONAL AUTHORITIES
Narrative: EPA HAS A TRUST RELATIONSHIP WITH TRIBAL GOVERNMENTS AND, UNDER FEDERAL ENVIRONMENTAL STATUTES, THE AGENCY IS RESPONSIBLE FOR ASSURING HUMAN HEALTH AND ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION IN INDIAN COUNTRY. AIEO PROVIDES LEADERSHIP IN THE ADVANCEMENT AND IMPLEMENTATION OF EPA?S INDIAN POLICY, COORDINATES THE AGENCY?S INDIAN PROGRAM, ASSISTS HEADQUARTERS PROGRAM OFFICES IN DEVELOPING NATIONAL TRIBAL ENVIRONMENTAL STRATEGIES, COLLABORATES WITH OTHER AGENCIES TO PROMOTE INTERAGENCY COORDINATION AND PROMOTES AND IMPROVES COMMUNICATIONS BETWEEN EPA AND TRIBES. EPA?S STRATEGY FOR THIS PROGRAM HAS TWO MAJOR COMPONENTS:  (1)WORK WITH FEDERALLY-RECOGNIZED TRIBES WHO WANT TO CREATE AN ENVIRONMENTAL PROGRAM THROUGH: DIRECT TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE; IMPLEMENTATION OF THE INDIAN GENERAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAM (GAP); DEVELOPMENT OF JOINT STRATEGIC PLANS; AND DEVELOPMENT OF MEASURES FOR TRACKING PROGRESS MADE TOWARD ACHIEVING ENVIRONMENTAL PROGRAM GOAL; (2) GATHER, TRACK, ANALYZE, AND PROVIDE THE INFORMATION AND DATA NECESSARY TO ACCESS, REVIEW, AND PRIORITIZE TRIBAL ENVIRONMENTAL CONDITIONS FOR JOINT PLANNING USES AND TO DETERMINE THE EFFECTIVENESS OF THE EPA AND TRIBAL PROGRAMS IN IMPROVING ENVIRONMENTAL CONDITIONS. IN HEADQUARTERS, AIEO OVERSEES THE INDIAN ENVIRONMENTAL GENERAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAM ACT OF 1992, WHICH PROVIDES TRIBAL GOVERNMENTS AND INTER-TRIBAL CONSORTIA WITH GENERAL ASSISTANCE GRANTS AND TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE FOR THE PURPOSE OF PLANNING, DEVELOPING, AND ESTABLISHING THE CAPABILITY TO IMPLEMENT PROGRAMS ADMINISTERED BY THE AGENCY.
Definition: UNDER FEDERAL ENVIRONMENTAL STATUTES, EPA HAS THE RESPONSIBILITY FOR PROTECTING HUMAN HEALTH AND THE ENVIRONMENT IN INDIAN COUNTRY WITH A SPECIAL EMPHASIS ON BUILDING TRIBAL CAPACITY TO ADMINISTER THEIR OWN ENVIRONMENTAL PROGRAMS. EPA HAS WORKED TO ESTABLISH THE INTERNAL INFRASTRUCTURE AND ORGANIZE ITS ACTIVITIES IN ORDER TO MEET THIS RESPONSIBILITY SUCH AS THE IMPLEMENTATION OF THE AGENCIES INDIAN POLICY.

Name: US MEXICO BORDER
Title: E1 - US MEXICO BORDER
Laws: IN CONJUNCTION WITH NEPA SECTION 102(2)(F): CAA 103(A), 42 U.S.C. 7403(A); CWA 104(A)(1) AND (2), 33 U.S.C. 1254(A)(1) AND (2); SDWA 1442(A)(1), 42 U.S.C. 300J-1(A)(1); SWDA 8001(A)(1), 42 U.S.C. 6981(A)(1);  FIFRA ?17(D) AND 20(A) , 7 U.S.C. ?136O(D) AND  136R(A); TSCA?10(A) OF THE TOXIC SUBSTANCES CONTROL ACT (TSCA), 15 U.S.C. ?2609(A) (IN CONSULTATION AND COOPERATION WITH THE DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH AND HUMAN SERVICES AND WITH OTHER APPROPRIATE DEPARTMENTS AND AGENCIES); MPRSA 203(A)(1), 33 U.S.C. 1443(A)(1), 42 U.S.C. 4332.
Narrative: OITA SERVES AS THE NATIONAL PROGRAM MANAGER FOR ALL U.S.-MEXICO BORDER ACTIVITIES. BUILDING ON THE SUCCESSES OF BORDER 2012, THE BORDER 2020 LAYS OUT A ROADMAP FOR CONTINUED ENVIRONMENTAL COOPERATION OVER THE NEXT 8 YEARS.  BORDER 2020 PROGRAM, LIKE ITS PREDECESSOR, EMPHASIZES LOCAL PRIORITY-SETTING, FOCUSES ON MEASURABLE ENVIRONMENTAL RESULTS, AND ENCOURAGES BROAD PUBLIC PARTICIPATION.  BORDER 2020 BUILDS ON THE 2012 PROGRAM WORK HIGHLIGHTING REGIONAL AREAS WHERE ENVIRONMENTAL IMPROVEMENTS ARE MOST NEEDED; ESTABLISHING THEMATIC GOALS SUPPORTING THE IMPLEMENTATION OF PROJECTS, CONSIDERING NEW FUNDAMENTAL STRATEGIES AND ENCOURAGING THE ACHIEVEMENTS OF MORE AMBITIOUS ENVIRONMENTAL AND PUBLIC HEALTH GOALS.  BORDER 2020 IDENTIFIES FIVE LONG-TERM STRATEGIC GOALS TO ADDRESS THE SERIOUS ENVIRONMENTAL AND ENVIRONMENTALLY-RELATED PUBLIC HEALTH CHALLENGES INCLUDING THE IMPACT OF TRANSBOUNDARY TRANSPORT OF POLLUTANTS IN THE BORDER REGION. THE FIVE GOALS ARE:  REDUCE AIR POLLUTION; IMPROVE ACCESS TO CLEAN AND SAFE WATER; PROMOTE MATERIALS AND WASTE MANAGEMENT AND CLEAN SITES; ENHANCE JOINT PREPAREDNESS FOR ENVIRONMENTAL RESPONSE; AND ENHANCE COMPLIANCE ASSURANCE AND ENVIRONMENTAL STEWARDSHIP.
Definition: ACTIVITIES CARRIED OUT UNDER THIS PROGRAM/PROJECT ADDRESSES THE ENVIRONMENT AND THE HEALTH OF OVER 14 MILLION PEOPLE ALONG THE 3,100 KILOMETER STRETCH OF THE U.S.-MEXICO BORDER.  THE PROGRAM IS IMPLEMENTED IN PARTNERSHIP WITH THE TEN (10) BORDER STATES, U.S. TRIBAL GOVERNMENT, AND THE GOVERNMENT OF MEXICO WITH ITS CORRESPONDING STATES AND TRIBES.  IT ADDRESSES THE AGENCY?S MAJOR THEMES OF CLEAN AIR, CLEAN WATER, AND CLEAN LAND.

Name: WETLANDS
Title: E2 - WETLANDS
Laws: CLEAN WATER ACT, SECTIONS 104 AND 4041990 GREAT LAKES CRITICAL PROGRAMS ACT; GREAT LAKES AND LAKE CHAMPLAIN ACT; WETLANDS PLANNING, RESTORATION AND RESTORATION ACT OF 2002; ESTUARIES AND CLEAN WATERS ACT OF 2000; NORTH AMERICAN WETLANDS CONSERVATION ACT; WETLANDS RESOURCES DEVELOPMENT ACT; 1909 THE BOUNDARY WATERS TREATY; GREAT LAKES WATER QUALITY AGREEMENT OF 1978; 1996 HABITAT AGENDA; 1997 CANADA-U.S. GREAT LAKES BI-NATIONAL TOXICS STRATEGY; U.S.-CANADA AGREEMENTS.
Narrative: MAJOR ACTIVITIES OF THE WETLANDS PROTECTION PROGRAM INCLUDE DEVELOPMENT AND DISSEMINATION OF GUIDANCE, INFORMATION AND SCIENTIFIC TOOLS TO IMPROVE MANAGEMENT AND PUBLIC UNDERSTANDING OF WETLAND PROGRAMS AND LEGAL REQUIREMENTS; REVIEW OF SECTION 404 PERMIT APPLICATIONS SUBMITTED TO THE U.S. ARMY CORPS OF ENGINEERS (CORPS) OR AUTHORIZED STATES; AND MANAGEMENT OF FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE TO SUPPORT DEVELOPMENT OF STATE AND TRIBAL WETLAND PROTECTION PROGRAMS UNDER THE CWA.
Definition: REGULATORY AND NON-REGULATORY EFFORTS TO PROTECT AND RESTORE WETLANDS AND RIPARIAN HABITAT.

Name: ADMINISTRATIVE LAW
Title: E3 - ADMINISTRATIVE LAW
Laws: APPROPRIATIONS ACT; CERCLA; FIFRA; CWA; CAA; TSCA; RCRA; SDWA; EPCRA; APA.
Narrative: ALJ WILL CONVENE FORMAL HEARINGS IN THE LOCATION OF THE ALLEGED VIOLATOR OR VIOLATION, AS REQUIRED BY STATUTE. THE ALJ ALSO WILL IMPLEMENT AN ELECTRONIC FILING SYSTEM TO ACHIEVE SIGNIFICANT REDUCTIONS IN: MAILING DELAYS FOR ALL PARTIES, MAILING COSTS FOR ALLEGED VIOLATORS, AND REQUESTS FOR PAPER DOCUMENTS FROM THE ALJ. THIS SYSTEM WILL MAKE CASE DOCKET MANAGEMENT MORE SECURE WHILE SIMULTANEOUSLY PROMOTING TRANSPARENT GOVERNMENT OPERATIONS BY POSTING PUBLIC DOCUMENTS TO THE WEB IN REAL-TIME. ALJ WILL OFFER PUBLIC TRAINING EVENTS ON ADMINISTRATIVE HEARING PROCEDURES FOR EPA EMPLOYEES AND THE REGULATED COMMUNITY, AS WELL AS WORK WITH EAB TO SUPPORT INTERNATIONAL JUDICIAL ENVIRONMENTAL TRAINING EFFORTS. IN FY 2013, THE BOARD WILL ALSO EVALUATE THE EAB?S APRIL 2011 STANDING ORDER GOVERNING PROCEDURES IN CLEAN AIR ACT NEW SOURCE REVIEW APPEALS FOR APPROPRIATE FOLLOW-UP ACTION. IN ADDITION, THE EAB WILL WORK TO STREAMLINE RESOLUTION OF APPEALS THROUGH ITS ALTERNATIVE DISPUTE RESOLUTION (ADR) PROGRAM. IN FY 2013, THE BOARD EXPECTS APPROXIMATELY FIVE ADR NEGOTIATION REQUESTS. THE BOARD ALSO WILL UPDATE ITS ELECTRONIC FILING SYSTEM IN ORDER TO MAKE THE SYSTEM MORE USER-FRIENDLY AND ALLOW USERS TO FILE PLEADINGS AND RETRIEVE ELECTRONIC FILINGS MORE QUICKLY. IN ADDITION, RESOURCES WILL BE PROVIDED TO MAINTAIN EPA?S HEARING ROOM AND TO SUPPORT JUDICIAL ENVIRONMENTAL TRAINING CONSISTENT WITH AGENCY PRIORITIES.
Definition: THIS PROGRAM SUPPORTS EPA?S ADMINISTRATIVE LAW JUDGES (ALJ) AND THE ENVIRONMENTAL APPEALS BOARD (EAB) OR THE BOARD. THE ALJ PRESIDE IN HEARINGS AND ISSUE INITIAL DECISIONS IN CASES INITIATED BY EPA'S ENFORCEMENT PROGRAM CONCERNING ENVIRONMENTAL VIOLATIONS. THE EAB ISSUES FINAL DECISIONS IN ENVIRONMENTAL ADJUDICATIONS (PRIMARILY ENFORCEMENT AND PERMIT-RELATED), THAT ARE ON APPEAL TO THE BOARD. THE EAB ALSO SERVES AS THE FINAL APPROVING BODY FOR PROPOSED SETTLEMENTS OF ENFORCEMENT ACTIONS INITIATED BY THE AGENCY. ALJ ISSUE ORDERS AND DECISIONS UNDER THE AUTHORITY OF THE ADMINISTRATIVE PROCEDURE ACT (APA) AND THE VARIOUS ENVIRONMENTAL STATUTES THAT ESTABLISH ADMINISTRATIVE ENFORCEMENT AUTHORITY. THE EAB ISSUES DECISIONS UNDER THE AUTHORITY DELEGATED BY THE ADMINISTRATOR. THE DECISIONS REFLECT FINDINGS OF FACT AND CONCLUSIONS OF LAW. BY ADJUDICATING DISPUTED MATTERS, THE ALJ AND EAB WILL FURTHER THE AGENCY?S MISSION TO PROTECT HUMAN HEALTH AND THE ENVIRONMENT. THE ALJ PRESIDES OVER HEARINGS AND ISSUE INITIAL DECISIONS IN CASES BROUGHT BY THE AGENCY?S ENFORCEMENT PROGRAM AGAINST THOSE ACCUSED OF VIOLATIONS UNDER VARIOUS ENVIRONMENTAL STATUTES. THE RIGHT OF AFFECTED PERSONS TO APPEAL THOSE DECISIONS IS CONFERRED BY VARIOUS STATUTES, REGULATIONS AND CONSTITUTIONAL DUE PROCESS RIGHTS. THE EAB ADJUDICATES ADMINISTRATIVE APPEALS IN A THOROUGH, FAIR AND TIMELY MANNER. IN APPROXIMATELY NINETY PERCENT OF CASES DECIDED BY THE BOARD, NO FURTHER APPEAL IS TAKEN TO FEDERAL COURT, PROVIDING A FINAL RESOLUTION TO THE DISPUTE.

Name: ALTERNATIVE DISPUTE RESOLUTION
Title: E4 - ALTERNATIVE DISPUTE RESOLUTION
Laws: ADMINISTRATIVE DISPUTE RESOLUTION ACT (ADRA) OF 1996, 5 UNITED STATES CODE (U.S.C.) SECTIONS 571, 572, AND 573; NEGOTIATED RULEMAKING ACT OF 1996, 5 U.S.C. SECTIONS 563, 565, 566, AND 568; EPA?S GENERAL AUTHORIZING STATUTES
Narrative: EPMOGC PROVIDES CONFLICT PREVENTION AND ALTERNATIVE DISPUTE RESOLUTION (ADR) SERVICES TO EPA HEADQUARTERS AND REGIONAL OFFICES AND EXTERNAL STAKEHOLDERS.  OGC ALSO ASSISTS IN DEVELOPING EFFECTIVE WAYS TO ANTICIPATE, PREVENT, AND RESOLVE DISPUTES, AND MAKE NEUTRAL THIRD PARTIES MORE READILY AVAILABLE FOR THOSE PURPOSES.SUPERFUNDWHEN THE RESPONSIBILITIES DESCRIBED ABOVE ARE PERFORMED FOR THE DIRECT AND IMMEDIATE BENEFIT OF THE SUPERFUND PROGRAM, SUPERFUND FUNDS WILL BE USED.
Definition: THIS PROGRAM/PROJECT PROVIDES ALTERNATIVE DISPUTE RESOLUTION SERVICES TO EPA HEADQUARTERS AND REGIONAL OFFICES AND EXTERNAL STAKEHOLDERS.

Name: CENTRAL PLANNING, BUDGETING, AND FINANCE
Title: E5 - CENTRAL PLANNING, BUDGETING, AND FINANCE
Laws: ANNUAL APPROPRIATIONS ACTCLINGER-COHEN ACT OF 1996COMPUTER SECURITY ACT OF 1987E-GOVERNMENT ACT OF 2002ELECTRIC FREEDOM ACT OF 1996FEDERAL GRANT AND COOPERATIVE AGREEMENT ACT OF 1977FEDERAL ACTIVITIES INVENTORY REFORM ACT OF 1998FEDERAL ACQUISITION REGULATIONS, CONTRACT LAW AND THE EPA'S ASSISTANCE REGULATIONS (40 CFR PARTS 30, 31, 35, 40, 45, 46, 47)FEDERAL MANAGERS FINANCIAL INTEGRITY ACT OF 1982FREEDOM OF INFORMATION ACT OF 1966GOVERNMENT MANAGEMENT REFORM ACT OF 1994IMPROPER PAYMENTS INFORMATION ACT OF 2002IMPROPER PAYMENTS ELIMINATION AND RECOVERY ACT OF 2010INSPECTOR GENERAL ACT OF 1978 AS AMENDEDPAPERWORK REDUCTION ACT OF 1995PRIVACY ACT OF 1974CHIEF FINANCIAL OFFICERS ACT OF 1990GOVERNMENT PERFORMANCE AND RESULTS ACT OF 1993PROMPT PAYMENT ACT OF 1982TITLE 5, U.S.C.NATIONAL DEFENCE AUTHORIZATION ACT
Narrative: NOTE:  MANAGEMENT AND SUPPORT ACTIVITIES ARE PROVIDED FOR MULTIPLE AGENCY APPROPRIATIONS.  FOR EXAMPLE, UNDER THE SUPERFUND APPROPRIATION, OCFO PROVIDES A FULL ARRAY OF FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT SUPPORT SERVICES NECESSARY TO PAY SUPERFUND BILLS AND RECOUP CLEANUP AND OVERSIGHT COSTS FOR THE TRUST FUND. WHERE IT IS NOT FEASIBLE TO USE A DIRECT CHARGING METHOD OF ACCOUNTING FOR THESE ACTIVITIES, ALLOCATION METHODOLOGIES ARE ESTABLISHED AND APPROVED TO ENSURE THAT COSTS ARE PROPERLY ATTRIBUTED TO THE CORRECT APPROPRIATIONS. EPA CONTINUES TO REDUCE THE AMOUNT OF IMPROPER PAYMENTS PURSUANT TO THE IMPROPER PAYMENT ACT OF 2002.FINANCIAL SERVICESPROVIDES A FULL RANGE OF NATIONAL, LOCAL AND SPECIALIZED ACCOUNTING, FINANCIAL AND CUSTOMER SERVICES THROUGH THE AGENCY'S THREE FINANCE CENTERS LOCATED IN CINCINNATI, LAS VEGAS, AND RESEARCH TRIANGLE PARK. THESE CENTERS HAVE NATIONWIDE RESPONSIBILITY FOR EPA IN THE FOLLOWING ACCOUNTING AND FINANCIAL AREAS:  CONTRACTS, PROGRAM AND INTER-AGENCY GRANTS;  AND FEDERAL AND BANKCARD PAYMENTS.PROVIDES NATIONWIDE ACCOUNTING SERVICES FOR EPA'S WORKING CAPITAL FUND AND MANAGING THE CENTRAL PAYMENT AND CERTIFICATION PROCESS WITH TREASURY. PROVIDES SERVICES FOR ITS LOCAL AND REGIONAL EPA COMMUNITY IN COMMERCIAL AND TRAVEL PAYMENTS, ALONG WITH TRAINING AND OTHER LOCAL FINANCE ACTIVITIES. PROVIDES A VARIETY OF SPECIALIZED FINANCIAL AND CUSTOMER SERVICE FUNCTIONS, INCLUDING:  CROSS-SERVICING OF PAYROLL, AND COST RECOVERY SERVICES.PROVIDES SUPPORT FOR THE BASE REALIGNMENT AND CLOSURE PROGRAM; AND MANAGEMENT OF THE POLLUTION ALLOWANCE AUCTION PROCEEDS AND THE ASBESTOS LOAN REPAYMENT PROGRAM.FINANCIAL MANAGEMENTDEVELOPS AND ISSUES AGENCY-WIDE RESOURCES MANAGEMENT POLICIES AND PROCEDURES INCLUDING THE ESTABLISHMENT OF STANDARD ACCOUNTING PRACTICES. DEVELOPS AND PREPARES THE AGENCY'S ANNUAL FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AND AUDIT RESULTS, AND MANAGES AND REPORTS ON THE CHIEF FINANCIAL OFFICER (CFO)/SENIOR RESOURCE OFFICIAL PERFORMANCE MEASURES. IMPLEMENTS COST ACCOUNTING REQUIREMENTS STEMMING FROM THE FEDERAL ACCOUNTING STANDARDS ADVISORY BOARD, MANAGERIAL COST ACCOUNTING STANDARD, AND STATUTORY MANDATES FOR COST ACCOUNTING SUCH AS THE CFO ACT, GOVERNMENT PERFORMANCE AND RESULTS ACT (GPRA), AND FEDERAL MANAGER'S FINANCIAL INTEGRITY ACT. PROVIDES FINANCIAL REPORTING INVOLVING DETAILED COST ACCOUNTING DATA FOR PROGRAM OFFICE NEEDS AND SPECIALIZED FINANCIAL REPORTING CAPABILITIES. ANNUAL PLANNING AND BUDGETMANAGES THE AGENCY'S ANNUAL BUDGET PROCESS, INCLUDING DEVELOPMENT AND COORDINATION OF THE BUDGET PROCESS AND RESOURCE ALLOCATION PLANS.IMPLEMENTS THE ANNUAL OPERATING PLANS AND MAINTAINS BUDGET DATA AND INFORMATION SYSTEMS. MONITORS EXPENDITURES OF AGENCY FUNDS AND ENSURES THAT THE AGENCY COMPLIES WITH THE ANTI-DEFICIENCY ACT AND OTHER EXECUTIVE BRANCH AND CONGRESSIONALLY IMPOSED FIDUCIARY CONTROLS. ACTS AS THE PRIMARY AGENCY LIAISON WITH THE CONGRESS ON ALL MATTERS PERTAINING TO THE AGENCY'S BUDGET AND APPROPRIATIONS; PREPARES AND IMPLEMENTS SYSTEMS, POLICIES, GUIDANCE, PROCEDURES, AND REPORTS FOR AGENCY-WIDE FUNDS CONTROL; AND ENSURING AGENCY COMPLIANCE WITH ALL ADMINISTRATIVE, LEGAL, AND CONGRESSIONALLY IMPOSED FUNDS CONTROL REQUIREMENTS. DEVELOPS AND MAINTAINS BUDGET EXECUTION POLICY AND GUIDANCE AFFECTING THE ISSUANCE OR CONTROL OF FUNDS INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO: START-OF-YEAR AND END-OF-YEAR GUIDANCE, ADVICE OF ALLOWANCE LETTER, TRAVEL, ADMINISTRATIVE CEILING POLICIES, AND VARIOUS POLICIES AND PROCEDURES ON REPROGRAMMINGS, CARRYOVER, OVERRUNS, RE-CERTIFICATIONS, AND STATE GRANT LIMITATIONS. PREPARES AGENCY RESPONSES TO THE GOVERNMENT ACCOUNTABILITY OFFICE (GAO) RECOMMENDATIONS AND GAO DRAFT DOCUMENTS REQUIRED BY LAW.  COMMUNICATES WITH AND INVOLVES AGENCY SENIOR MANAGERS IN SIGNIFICANT GAO REVIEWS ON MEETING NATIONAL ENVIRONMENTAL GOALS AND PROGRAM EFFECTIVENESS AND EFFICIENCY ISSUES. STRATEGIC PLANNINGOVERSEES THE STRATEGIC PLANNING PROCESS, INCLUDING DESIGN AND IMPLEMENTATION OF THE STRATEGIC PLAN AND ITS SUBMISSION TO OMB AND THE CONGRESS.  WORKS WITH EPA OFFICES, REGIONS AND EXTERNAL STAKEHOLDERS TO ESTABLISH THE AGENCY'S STRATEGIC DECISIONS, LONG-TERM GOALS, AND GENERAL STRATEGIES FOR ATTAINING THE GOALS. DESIGNS THE PROCESS AND GUIDANCE FOR FORMULATING STRATEGIC GOALS, OBJECTIVES,  AND STRATEGIC MEASURES, AND COORDINATES THEIR DEVELOPMENT WITH EPA OFFICES.  FACILITATES PROGRAM AND REGIONAL PLANNING AND PERFORMANCE MEASUREMENT ACTIVITIES ON AN ONGOING BASIS, LENDING ASSISTANCE AS "DESK OFFICERS" FOR EACH OF THE AGENCY'S STRATEGIC GOALS/NPMS. DEVELOPS AND MANAGES OUTREACH AND STAKEHOLDER INVOLVEMENT EFFORTS IN SUPPORT OF GPRA COMPLIANCE.  DEVELOPS AND PROVIDES TRAINING MATERIALS AS NEEDED FOR FURTHERING THE UNDERSTANDING AND EFFECTIVE IMPLEMENTATION OF THE GOVERNMENT PERFORMANCE AND RESULTS MODERNIZATION ACT PROCESSES. ANALYSIS  DEVELOPS METHODS TO ESTIMATE AND COMPARE THE RELATIVE IMPACTS OF HUMAN HEALTH, ECOLOGICAL, AND QUALITY OF LIFE RISKS ADDRESSED BY AGENCY ACTIVITIES. THIS PROVIDES AGENCY MANAGEMENT WITH IMPROVED COMPARISONS OF THE RELATIVE BENEFITS OF THE INDIVIDUAL PROGRAMS AND ALLOWS RESOURCES TO BE FOCUSED ON HIGH PRIORITY AREAS. ESTIMATES AND COMPARES THE COSTS AND BENEFITS OF DIFFERENT STRATEGIES THAT MAY BE USED TO ACHIEVE THE AGENCY'S GOALS. DEVELOPS ECONOMIC ANALYSES THAT PROVIDE AGENCY DECISION MAKERS WITH AN ADDITIONAL TOOL FOR SETTING PRIORITIES BY IDENTIFYING PROGRAMS THAT PROVIDE THE MOST ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION IN THE MOST COST-EFFECTIVE MEANS. WORKS TO DEVELOP PERFORMANCE INFORMATION OF KNOWN QUALITY THAT CAPTURES ACTUAL ENVIRONMENTAL OUTCOMES AND GAUGES PROGRESS IN ACHIEVING PROGRAMMATIC AND ENVIRONMENTAL GOALS. THESE PERFORMANCE MEASURES INCLUDE MEASURES OF DISCHARGES OR EMISSIONS, AMBIENT CONDITIONS, BODY BURDENS, AND DIRECT MEASURES OF ECOLOGICAL, HUMAN HEALTH, AND QUALITY OF LIFE IMPACTS. THE RESULTS OF THIS ONGOING EFFORT IMPROVES THE EPA?S ABILITY TO MEASURE AND EVALUATE PROGRESS IN ACHIEVING AGENCY COMMITMENTS IN TERMS OF ENVIRONMENTAL OUTCOMES. DEVELOPS APPROACHES, ANALYZES PROGRAM PERFORMANCE INFORMATION, AND ASSESSES IMPACTS FROM PROGRAM ACTIVITIES ON ACHIEVING ANNUAL AND LONGER-TERM HEALTH AND ENVIRONMENTAL OUTCOMES TO INFORM AGENCY STRATEGIES AND RESOURCE DECISIONS.SOLICITS INFORMATION AND ANALYSES OF POSSIBLE FUTURE ECOLOGICAL, HUMAN HEALTH, AND QUALITY OF LIFE ISSUES. THE STAFF FACILITATES AND COORDINATES THE WORK OF OTHERS, BOTH INSIDE AND OUTSIDE OF EPA, SO THAT THE AGENCY WILL BE ABLE TO ADDRESS EMERGING ISSUES AND MITIGATE FUTURE IMPACTS TO HUMAN HEALTH AND THE ENVIRONMENT. ACCOUNTABILITY   DEVELOPS, IMPLEMENTS AND MANAGES THE AGENCY'S ACCOUNTABILITY PROCESS, INCLUDING A SUPPORTING CENTRALIZED DATABASE FOR REPORTING PERFORMANCE DATA AND CHARACTERIZING RESULTS FOR DEVELOPMENT OF EPA'S ANNUAL PERFORMANCE REPORT TO OMB AND THE CONGRESS, WHICH IS INCLUDED IN THE AGENCY?S CONGRESSIONAL BUDGET JUSTIFICATION.  RESULTS OF THIS EFFORT OVER TIME WILL SUPPORT MANAGING FOR RESULTS BY AGENCY SENIOR OFFICIALS AND LINE MANAGERS, AND CONTRIBUTE TO THE ASSESSMENT OF ANNUAL PROGRESS TOWARDS THE ACHIEVEMENT OF LONGER-TERM AGENCY GOALS. MANAGES THE AGENCY'S AUDIT MANAGEMENT PROGRAM TO PROMOTE TIMELY RESPONSE TO INSPECTOR GENERAL AUDIT FINDINGS AND CORRECTION OF IDENTIFIED WEAKNESSES.  ROUTINELY ADVISES EPA SENIOR MANAGERS OF SENSITIVE OR SIGNIFICANT AUDIT ISSUES AND DEVELOPS THE ADMINISTRATOR'S SEMI-ANNUAL REPORTS ON AUDIT MANAGEMENT TO THE CONGRESS. IMPLEMENTS THE AGENCY'S MANAGEMENT INTEGRITY PROGRAM TO ENSURE SOUND MANAGEMENT PRACTICES AND EFFECTIVE INTERNAL CONTROLS TO PROTECT EPA PROGRAMS FROM WASTE, FRAUD, ABUSE AND MISMANAGEMENT.  FACILITATES TOP MANAGEMENT REVIEW OF EXISTING OR NEW SYSTEMIC WEAKNESSES FOR DEVELOPMENT OF THE ADMINISTRATOR'S ANNUAL INTEGRITY ACT REPORT TO THE PRESIDENT AND THE CONGRESS.PROGRAM MANAGEMENT  PROVIDES LONG-TERM AND STRATEGIC DEVELOPMENT, POLICY DEVELOPMENT, BUDGET FORMULATION AND EXECUTION, HUMAN RESOURCE COORDINATION, INFORMATION RESOURCES MANAGEMENT, RESOURCE MONITORING, AND ADMINISTRATIVE MANAGEMENT OVERSIGHT. COORDINATES AND CONSOLIDATES THE OFFICE OF THE CHIEF FINANCIAL OFFICER'S (OCFO'S) INTERNAL CONTROL REPORTING, ENSURES COMPLIANCE WITH FREEDOM OF INFORMATION ACT AND AGENCY AUDIT RECOMMENDATIONS, AND OVERSEES INFORMATION MANAGEMENT NEEDS. PROVIDES RESOURCE MANAGEMENT GUIDANCE TO AGENCY MANAGEMENT, THE ADMINISTRATION, AND CONGRESS. DEVELOPS AND MONITORS IMPLEMENTATION OF AGENCY POLICY AS IT RELATES TO THE WORKING CAPITAL FUND. SYSTEMS PLANNING AND INTEGRATION  PROVIDES A STRATEGIC PORTFOLIO MANAGEMENT APPROACH TO PLANNING, BUDGETING, DEVELOPING, AND IMPLEMENTING CRITICAL OCFO FINANCIAL SYSTEMS AND SERVES AS THE CFO LEAD ON MANY CRITICAL AGENCY-WIDE E-GOVERNMENT AND INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY INITIATIVES.COORDINATES THE AGENCY'S STRATEGIC PLANNING OF FINANCIAL SYSTEMS MODERNIZATION AND REPLACEMENT.  REPLACES EXISTING FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT SYSTEMS, AND DEVELOPS THE FINANCIAL SYSTEM FRAMEWORK AND ARCHITECTURE TO PROMOTE SYSTEMS INTEGRATION.MANAGES, MAINTAINS, AND ENHANCES EPA'S EXISTING COMPASS AND OTHER RELATED FINANCIAL AND ADMINISTRATIVE SYSTEMS.  DEVELOPS AND MAINTAINS FINANCIAL AND INFORMATION SYSTEMS TO SUPPORT AND ENHANCE OCFO SERVICE DELIVERY. ENVIRONMENTAL FINANCEPROVIDES DIRECTION AND LEADERSHIP IN FORMULATING CREATIVE APPROACHES TO FUNDING ENVIRONMENTAL PROGRAMS AND ACQUIRING THE TOOLS AND RESOURCES NEEDED FOR MEETING THE NATION?S ENVIRONMENTAL NEEDS. PROVIDES ADVICE AND RECOMMENDATIONS ON SUSTAINABLE WAYS TO PAY FOR THE GROWING COSTS OF ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION AND INCREASING INVESTMENT IN ENVIRONMENTAL INFRASTRUCTURE THROUGH THE LEVERAGING OF PUBLIC AND PRIVATE RESOURCES. PROVIDES STATE, TRIBAL, AND LOCAL OFFICIALS, AND SMALL BUSINESSES WITH ADVISORY SERVICES, EDUCATION, PUBLICATIONS, TRAINING, AND TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE AND ANALYSES ON FINANCING ISSUES AND ALTERNATIVES. IDENTIFIES WAYS TO AVOID AND LOWER COSTS, IMPROVE EFFICIENCIES, PROMOTE PARTNERSHIPS, AND INCREASE INVESTMENT IN ENVIRONMENTAL PROGRAMS, ACTIVITIES, FACILITIES, AND SYSTEMS.  PROVIDES A CLEARINGHOUSE FOR ENVIRONMENTAL FINANCE INFORMATION -- INCLUDING ON-LINE DATABASE, FINANCIAL TOOL GUIDES, TECHNOLOGY ALTERNATIVES, AND NETWORKING LINKS. DEEP WATER HORIZON OIL SPILLPROVIDE FINANCIAL SUPPORT FOR THE AGENCY IN ITS ROLE AS TRUSTEE FOR THE DEEPWATER HORIZON  OIL SPILL PURSUANT TO EXECUTIVE ORDER 13626 REGARDING NATURAL RESOURCE DAMAGE ASSESSMENT AND RESTORATION IN CONNECTION WITH INJURY TO, DESTRUCTION OF, LOSS OF OR LOSS OF USE OF NATURAL RESOURCES, INCLUDING THEIR SUPPORTING ECOSYSTEMS, RESULTING FROM THE OIL SPILL.E-ENTERPRISECONTINUE TO IMPLEMENT THE E-ENTERPRISE BUSINESS STRATEGY, A TRANSFORMATIVE 21ST CENTURY STRATEGY ? JOINTLY GOVERNED BY STATES AND THE EPA - FOR MODERNIZING GOVERNMENT AGENCIES? DELIVERY OF ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION. UNDER THIS STRATEGY, THE AGENCY WILL STREAMLINE ITS BUSINESS PROCESSES AND SYSTEMS TO REDUCE THE REPORTING BURDEN ON STATES AND REGULATED FACILITIES, AND IMPROVE THE EFFECTIVENESS AND EFFICIENCY OF REGULATORY PROGRAMS FOR THE EPA, STATES AND TRIBES. IT/DM ACTIVITIES WILL CONTINUE TO FACILITATE SHARED SERVICES AND ELECTRONIC TRANSACTIONS WITH THE REGULATED COMMUNITY AND EXTERNAL PARTNERS WHO ROUTINELY CONDUCT ENVIRONMENTAL BUSINESS WITH THE EPA. TO SUPPORT THE E-ENTERPRISE STRATEGY, IT APPLICATIONS AND INFRASTRUCTURE WILL BE ENHANCED TO ENABLE GREATER PUBLIC ACCESSIBILITY AND ELECTRONIC EXCHANGE OF INFORMATION BETWEEN THE EPA, STATES AND TRIBES. FOUNDATIONAL SHARED INFRASTRUCTURE AND SERVICES WILL CONTINUE TO BE USED TO ENABLE E-ENTERPRISE PRINCIPLES.  E-ENTERPRISE PROVIDES A STRUCTURE AND STRATEGY FOR MODERNIZING THE EPA?S PUBLICLY FACING SYSTEMS.
Definition: PLANNING AND RESOURCE MANAGEMENT -  MANAGE INTEGRATED PLANNING, BUDGETING, FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT AND ACCOUNTABILITY PROCESSES AND SYSTEMS TO ENSURE EFFECTIVE STEWARDSHIP OF RESOURCES WHICH MEETS STATUTORY REQUIREMENTS OF THE GOVERNMENT PERFORMANCE AND RESULTS ACT, CHIEF FINANCIAL OFFICER AND RELATED LEGISLATION.MANAGEMENT SERVICES AND STEWARDSHIP - RESPONSIBLE FOR PROVIDING A FULL RANGE OF NATIONAL, LOCAL AND SPECIALIZED ACCOUNTING, FINANCIAL AND CUSTOMER SERVICES THROUGH EPA?S  THREE FINANCE CENTERS.

Name: CIVIL RIGHTS / TITLE VI COMPLIANCE
Title: E7 - CIVIL RIGHTS / TITLE VI COMPLIANCE
Laws: TITLE VI OF THE CIVIL RIGHTS ACT OF 1964, AS AMENDED (42 U.S.C. ?2000D TO 2000D-7); 40 C.F.R. PART 7; SECTION 13 OF THE FEDERAL WATER POLLUTION CONTROL ACT AMENDMENTS OF 1972; TITLE VII OF THE CIVIL RIGHTS ACT OF 1964, AS AMENDED (42 U.S.C. ?2000E ET SEQ.); EQUAL PAY ACT OF 1963 (29 U.S.C. ?206(D)); SECTION 50L OF THE REHABILITATION ACT OF 1973; AMERICANS WITH DISABILITIES ACT OF 1990 (42 U.S.C. ?12101); ADA AMENDMENTS ACT OF 2008, OLDER WORKERS BENEFIT PROTECTION ACT (OWBPA) AS AMENDED; AGE DISCRIMINATION IN EMPLOYMENT ACT (ADEA) OF 1967, AS AMENDED (29 U.S.C. ? 621-634); TITLE IX OF THE EDUCATION AMENDMENTS OF 1972; AGE DISCRIMINATION ACT OF 1975; REHABILITATION ACT OF 1974, AS AMENDED; EEOC MANAGEMENT DIRECTIVE 715; EXECUTIVE ORDERS 13163, 13164, 13078, 13087, 13171, 11478, 13125, 13096, 13230, 13270 JULY 3, 2002 (TRIBAL COLLEGES), 13339 MAY 13, 2004 (ASIAN AMERICAN PARTICIPATION IN FEDERAL PROGRAMS).
Narrative: IN FY 2013, THE PRIORITIES OF EPA?S CIVIL RIGHTS PROGRAM WILL INCLUDE: TITLE VI ? PARTNER WITH THE OFFICE OF GENERAL COUNSEL (OGC) AND THE OFFICE OF GRANTS AND DEBARMENT TO IDENTIFY EPA FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE RECIPIENTS THAT HAVE FREQUENT OCCURRENCES OF TITLE VI COMPLAINTS. THIS WILL HELP THE OFFICE OF CIVIL RIGHTS (OCR) ENSURE THE EFFECTIVE UTILIZATION OF COMPLIANCE REVIEW RESOURCES, AID EPA IN ENSURING THE RECIPIENTS? COMPLIANCE WITH FEDERAL CIVIL RIGHTS LAWS AND REGULATIONS, AND PROVIDE THE PUBLIC GREATER ASSURANCE OF RECIPIENTS? EQUITABLE IMPLEMENTATION OF ENVIRONMENTAL POLICIES. ? INCREASE THE NUMBER OF COMPLIANCE REVIEWS CONDUCTED OF EPA FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE RECIPIENTS. THIS SHOULD INCREASE RECIPIENTS? COMPLIANCE WITH FEDERAL CIVIL RIGHTS LAWS, STATUTES, AND REGULATIONS, THEREBY PROVIDING THE PUBLIC WITH AN EQUITABLE ADMINISTRATION OF ENVIRONMENTAL SERVICES; ? OCR WILL COLLABORATE WITH THE NATIONAL PROGRAM AND REGIONAL OFFICES TO DEVELOP AND IMPLEMENT EFFECTIVE PROCESSES TO (1) REDUCE THE TITLE VI COMPLAINT BACKLOG AND (2) PROVIDE THE PUBLIC WITH THE TIMELY PROCESSING OF NEW TITLE VI COMPLAINTS; ? INCREASE OUTREACH REGARDING THE USE OF ALTERNATIVE DISPUTE RESOLUTION (ADR) TO MEMBERS OF THE PUBLIC (COMPLAINANTS) AND RECIPIENTS TO CREATE A BETTER UNDERSTANDING OF MUTUAL CONCERNS AND CONSTRAINTS, AS WELL AS THE BENEFIT TO PARTIES WHEN RESOLVING AN OUTSTANDING COMPLAINT; ? PROVIDE TRAINING ON THE MEDIATION PROCESS TO COLLATERAL DUTY MEDIATORS TO ENSURE CONSISTENT APPROACHES NATIONALLY TOWARDS THE INFORMAL RESOLUTION OF TITLE VI COMPLAINTS, AND TO ENSURE THAT THE PUBLIC AND RECIPIENTS ARE PROVIDED WITH A FAIR PROCESS. TITLE VII ? DEVELOP AN EEO COUNSELOR TRAINING CURRICULUM TO INCLUDE CERTIFICATION TRAINING, TECHNICAL GUIDANCE, AND PROVIDE ONGOING MENTORING AND COACHING TO ENSURE THAT EMPLOYEES AND APPLICANTS FOR EMPLOYMENT RECEIVE HIGH QUALITY COUNSELING SERVICES; ? PROVIDE LEGAL REVIEW AND ANALYSIS OF COMPLEX CASES, AND ASSIST WITH THE COMPLETION OF FINAL AGENCY DECISIONS. THESE FUNCTIONS WILL PROVIDE OCR WITH THE NECESSARY EXPERTISE TO AID IN THE MORE TIMELY ADJUDICATION OF TITLE VII COMPLAINTS; ? ENGAGE PARTIES EARLY IN THE TITLE VII PROCESS ON THE BENEFITS OF ADR. THIS WILL CREATE A BETTER UNDERSTANDING OF CONCERNS AND CONSTRAINTS BY BOTH PARTIES; ? CREATE A GROUP OF COLLATERAL DUTY MEDIATORS TO ASSIST WITH THE INFORMAL RESOLUTION OF TITLE VII COMPLAINTS. PROVIDE TRAINING ON THE MEDIATION PROCESS TO ENSURE THE USE OF CONSISTENT APPROACHES. ? DEVELOP EEO TRAINING PROGRAMS TO INFORM EPA LAS VEGAS LABORATORY EMPLOYEES OF THEIR RIGHTS AND RESPONSIBILITIES REGARDING THE EEO LAWS, STATUTES AND REGULATIONS; ? PERIODICALLY, MONITOR THE IMPLEMENTATION OF EPA?S POLICY ON HARASSMENT/DISCRIMINATION IN THE WORKPLACE BASED ON SEXUAL ORIENTATION, STATUS AS A PARENT, MARITAL STATUS AND POLITICAL AFFILIATION BY EXAMINING THE NUMBER AND BASES OF THESE COMPLAINTS FILED IN THE AGENCY; ? UPDATE THE ON-LINE MANDATORY TRAINING FOR THE NO FEAR ACT TO PRESENT THE INFORMATION IN A MORE USER FRIENDLY FORMAT. REASONABLE ACCOMMODATIONS ? DEVELOP AND IMPLEMENT A NEW POLICY TO IMPROVE THE DELIVERY OF SERVICES TO EPA EMPLOYEES AND APPLICANTS FOR EMPLOYMENT WITH DISABILITIES. OCR WILL USE AN ITERATIVE BARGAINING PROCESS INVOLVING THE OFFICE OF ADMINISTRATION AND RESOURCES MANAGEMENT (OARM), OGC, LABOR EMPLOYEE RELATIONS AND THE AGENCY?S BARGAINING UNIONS;407 ? OFFER TRAINING TO MANAGERS, TRACK ACCOMMODATION REQUESTS AND DECISIONS, AND MONITOR THE AGENCY?S COMPLIANCE WITH THE STATUTES, EEOC REGULATIONS AND THE AGENCY?S POLICY AND PROCEDURES RELATED TO THE REASONABLE ACCOMMODATION OF QUALIFIED APPLICANTS AND EMPLOYEES WITH DISABILITIES. AFFIRMATIVE EMPLOYMENT AND DIVERSITY ? IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE ADMINISTRATOR?S DIRECTIVE, EXPAND EFFORTS TO ENGAGE HEADQUARTERS AND THE REGIONS IN DEVELOPING A MODEL CIVIL RIGHTS PROGRAM AT EPA. OCR WILL USE THE EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITY COMMISSION?S (EEOC) MANAGEMENT DIRECTIVE (MD)-715 REPORT AS A GUIDE FOR EPA?S AFFIRMATIVE EMPLOYMENT EFFORTS. OCR WILL ESTABLISH A METRIC TO DETERMINE PROGRESS IN ACHIEVING ?MODEL EEO STATUS? AND ON A QUARTERLY BASIS, MONITOR THE REGIONS? AND HEADQUARTERS PROGRAM OFFICES? MD-715 ACTION PLANS WHICH DESCRIBE THEIR EFFORTS TO ACCOMPLISH PLANNED ACTIVITIES.
Definition: EPA?S CIVIL RIGHTS PROGRAM INCLUDES THE FOLLOWING ACTIVITIES: ? INTAKE AND PROCESSING OF TITLE VI COMPLAINTS OF DISCRIMINATION FROM THE PUBLIC ABOUT EPA?S FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE RECIPIENTS, COMPLAINT ADJUDICATION, AND CIVIL RIGHTS COMPLIANCE REVIEWS; ? INTAKE AND PROCESSING OF TITLE VII COMPLAINTS OF DISCRIMINATION FROM AGENCY EMPLOYEES AND APPLICANTS FOR EMPLOYMENT, AND COMPLAINT ADJUDICATION; ? IMPLEMENTATION OF PROCESSES AND PROGRAMS IN SUPPORT OF REASONABLE ACCOMMODATION; AND ? AFFIRMATIVE EMPLOYMENT AND DIVERSITY PROGRAM PLANNING AND IMPLEMENTATION. PROGRAM FUNCTIONS ALSO INCLUDE ACCOUNTABILITY FOR IMPLEMENTATION, PROGRAM EVALUATION AND COMPLIANCE MONITORING OF THE CIVIL RIGHTS ACT OF 1964 (TITLES VI, VII, IX), STATUTORY REQUIREMENTS AND EXECUTIVE ORDERS COVERING CIVIL RIGHTS, AFFIRMATIVE EMPLOYMENT, PERSONS WITH DISABILITIES, ALTERNATIVE DISPUTE RESOLUTION, AND REASONABLE ACCOMMODATIONS.

Name: CONGRESSIONAL, INTERGOVERNMENTAL, EXTERNAL RELATIONS
Title: E8 - CONGRESSIONAL, INTERGOVERNMENTAL, EXTERNAL RELATIONS
Laws: AS PROVIDED IN APPROPRIATIONS ACT FUNDING; FACA; EAIA; NAFTA IMPLEMENTATION ACT; RLBPHRA; NAAED; LPA-US/MX-BR; CERCLA
Narrative: THE IMMEDIATE OFFICE OF THE ADMINISTRATOR (IO) PROVIDES MANAGEMENT, LEADERSHIP AND DIRECTION FOR EPA'S PROGRAMS AND ACTIVITIES AND DEVELOPS THE GUIDANCE NECESSARY TO ENSURE THE ACHIEVEMENT OF THE AGENCY?S STRATEGIC GOALS AND PRIORITIES. TO ENSURE THAT REGIONAL VIEWS AND PRIORITIES ARE CONSIDERED IN THE FORMULATION OF ITS POLICIES AND DURING MAJOR PHASES OF DECISION MAKING, EACH REGIONAL OFFICE OF THE REGIONAL ADMINISTRATOR WORKS CLOSELY WITH THE IO AND THE OFFICE OF REGIONAL OPERATIONS TO ADDRESS NATIONAL, REGIONAL AND LOCAL ENVIRONMENTAL CONCERNS. THESE THREE UNITS WORK IN UNISON WITH GOVERNMENT POLICY MAKERS, STATES, TRIBES, AND THE PUBLIC BY COMMUNICATING AGENCY PROPOSALS, ACTIONS, POLICIES, RESEARCH, AND DATA THROUGH MASS MEDIA, PRINT PUBLICATIONS, AND THE WEB. THE OFFICE OF CONGRESSIONAL AND INTERGOVERNMENTAL RELATIONS (OCIR) ALONG WITH THEIR REGIONAL COUNTERPARTS LEAD EPA?S INTERACTIONS WITH CONGRESS, GOVERNORS, AND OTHER STATE AND LOCAL OFFICIALS. IN FY 2013, OCIR WILL PREPARE EPA?S OFFICIALS FOR HEARINGS AND MEETINGS WITH MEMBERS OF CONGRESS, OVERSEE RESPONSES TO WRITTEN INQUIRIES AND OVERSIGHT REQUESTS FROM MEMBERS OF CONGRESS, AND COORDINATE AND PROVIDE TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE AND BRIEFINGS TO MEMBERS OF CONGRESS AND STAFF ON LEGISLATIVE AREAS OF INTEREST. OCIR WILL WORK WITH PROGRAM OFFICES TO PREPARE NOMINEES FOR CONFIRMATION HEARINGS. IN ADDITION, OCIR WILL COORDINATE WITH THE WHITE HOUSE?S OFFICE OF LEGISLATIVE AND INTERGOVERNMENTAL AFFAIRS AND THE COUNCIL FOR ENVIRONMENTAL QUALITY ON ISSUES RELATED TO ACHIEVING THE GOALS AND PRIORITIES OF THE AGENCY. OCIR?S INTERGOVERNMENTAL OFFICE WILL SERVE AS THE AGENCY?S LIAISON TO STATE AND LOCAL GOVERNMENT OFFICIALS AND WILL MANAGE THE ADMINISTRATOR?S LOCAL GOVERNMENT ADVISORY COMMITTEE AND THE SMALL COMMUNITY ADVISORY SUBCOMMITTEE. THESE ACTIVITIES WILL HELP TO ENSURE THAT EPA?S POLICIES AND REGULATIONS CONSIDER SPECIFIC IMPACTS ON STATE AND LOCAL GOVERNMENTS. THE OFFICE WILL ALSO MONITOR REGULATIONS TO ENSURE THAT PROPER CONSULTATION WITH STATE AND LOCAL GOVERNMENTS TAKES PLACE IN ACCORDANCE WITH FEDERALISM GUIDELINES. THE OFFICE WILL CONTINUE TO WORK CLOSELY WITH PROGRAM OFFICES TO MORE FULLY INTEGRATE THE NATIONAL ENVIRONMENTAL PERFORMANCE PARTNERSHIPS SYSTEM (NEPPS) FRAMEWORK AND PRINCIPLES INTO THE AGENCY'S CORE BUSINESS PRACTICES. NEPPS IS A PERFORMANCE-BASED SYSTEM OF ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION DESIGNED TO IMPROVE THE EFFICIENCY AND EFFECTIVENESS OF STATE-EPA PARTNERSHIPS. BY FOCUSING EPA AND STATE RESOURCES ON THE MOST PRESSING ENVIRONMENTAL PROBLEMS AND TAKING ADVANTAGE OF THE UNIQUE CAPACITIES OF EACH PARTNER, PERFORMANCE PARTNERSHIPS MAY HELP ACHIEVE THE GREATEST ENVIRONMENTAL AND HUMAN HEALTH PROTECTION. OCIR?S EFFORTS WILL SUPPORT EPA?S STRATEGIC PLAN AND THE ADMINISTRATOR?S PRIORITY FOR BUILDING ON STATE PARTNERSHIPS. THE OFFICE OF FEDERAL ADVISORY COMMITTEE MANAGEMENT AND OUTREACH (OFACMO) CREATES UNIFORM POLICY AND GUIDANCE AND HAS OVERSIGHT RESPONSIBILITY FOR THE AGENCY?S FACA COMMITTEE MANAGEMENT PROCESS. IT SURVEYS COMMITTEE MEMBERS AND STAKEHOLDERS, IDENTIFIES AND SHARES BEST PRACTICES, AND PROVIDES TRAINING TO AGENCY DESIGNATED FEDERAL OFFICERS (DFOS), COMMITTEE CHAIRPERSONS, AND COMMITTEE MEMBERS. THIS WORK WILL ENSURE THAT EPA?S 50 FEDERAL ADVISORY COMMITTEES (FACS) AND SUB-COMMITTEES ARE IN COMPLIANCE WITH FACA REQUIREMENTS AND ADMINISTRATIVE GUIDELINES PROVIDED BY THE GENERAL SERVICES ADMINISTRATION?S COMMITTEE MANAGEMENT SECRETARIAT. IN FY 2013, OFACMO WILL CONDUCT COMPREHENSIVE ?OVERSIGHT/ASSIST? VISITS TO ENSURE THAT EPA?S FEDERAL ADVISORY COMMITTEES COMPLY WITH NOTICE, OPEN MEETING, PUBLIC DOCUMENT, AND RECORD KEEPING REQUIREMENTS. THESE VISITS WILL HELP REDUCE PRACTICES THAT EXPOSE THE COMMITTEES TO LEGAL CHALLENGES AND VULNERABILITY. IN ADDITION, THIS OFFICE IS RESPONSIBLE FOR MANAGING FIVE FACS: THE GOOD NEIGHBOR ENVIRONMENTAL BOARD, THE NATIONAL ADVISORY COMMITTEE, THE GOVERNMENTAL ADVISORY COMMITTEE, THE NATIONAL ADVISORY COUNCIL FOR ENVIRONMENTAL POLICY AND TECHNOLOGY, AND THE FARM, RANCH AND RURAL COMMUNITIES COMMITTEE. IN FY 2013, OFACMO WILL ALSO IMPLEMENT A STRATEGIC OUTREACH INITIATIVE TO ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE AND SCIENCE-BASED GROUPS, SCHOOLS AND ORGANIZATIONS TO INCREASE THE NUMBER OF UNDERREPRESENTED AND UNDERSERVED COMMUNITIES ON EPA?S FEDERAL ADVISORY COMMITTEES. AN ENHANCED POOL WILL ALLOW DFOS AND PROGRAM OFFICES TO HAVE PARTICIPATION ON EXISTING COMMITTEES FROM INDIVIDUALS, COMMUNITIES AND GROUPS THAT HAVE TRADITIONALLY BEEN UNDERSERVED AND/OR UNDERUTILIZED ON EPA?S COMMITTEES. SUCH AN APPROACH WILL ALLOW THE AGENCY TO HAVE BALANCED, DIVERSE POINTS OF VIEWS, A KEY COMPONENT OF THE FACA PROCESS. OFACMO WILL CREATE AND MAINTAIN A POOL OF DIVERSE CANDIDATES IN A CENTRAL ?DIVERSITY? DATABASE THAT WILL BE A KEY RESOURCE FOR THE AGENCY?S ADVISORY COMMITTEES. FURTHER, THE PROGRAM WILL VISIT REGIONAL OFFICES TO BRIEF MANAGERS AND STAFF ON THE BENEFITS THAT ADVISORY COMMITTEES BRING TO THEIR PROGRAMS. TO STRENGTHEN ITS PUBLIC PARTICIPATION FUNCTION, OFACMO WILL IMPLEMENT A PLAN TO EXPAND THE CONVERSATION ON ENVIRONMENTALISM. THIS WILL INCLUDE INTEGRATING NEW TECHNOLOGIES, INCLUDING VIDEOCONFERENCING, WEBCASTING, AND OTHER FORMS OF SOCIAL MEDIA, WITH OTHER COMMUNICATION AND OUTREACH EFFORTS. BY USING THESE TOOLS, OFACMO CAN ENSURE LINKS BETWEEN EPA?S FEDERAL ADVISORY COMMITTEES. MOREOVER, IT WILL ALLOW THE OFFICE TO HOLD PUBLIC MEETINGS, ATTEND CONFERENCES, AND FORM PARTNERSHIPS WITH MINORITY ACADEMIC INSTITUTIONS, THE NATIONAL SCIENCE FOUNDATION, AND OTHER SCIENCE/POLICY BASED ORGANIZATIONS. THE OFFICE OF EXTERNAL AFFAIRS AND ENVIRONMENTAL EDUCATION (OEAEE) LEADS EPA IN PROVIDING A CONSISTENT, TRANSPARENT FLOW OF INFORMATION FROM THE AGENCY?S HEADQUARTERS AND REGIONAL OFFICES TO THE PUBLIC, THE MEDIA, FEDERAL, STATE AND LOCAL GOVERNMENT ENTITIES AND STAKEHOLDERS. IN FY 2013, EPA?S HEADQUARTERS AND REGIONAL OFFICES OF EXTERNAL AFFAIRS WILL INCREASE THEIR RESPONSIVENESS TO THE MEDIA BY STREAMLINING INTERNAL PROCESSES TO MEET REPORTERS? DEADLINES. THE OFFICE WILL RESEARCH AND EVALUATE NEW MULTIMEDIA AND WEB APPLICATION TOOLS TO DETERMINE IF THEY ARE MORE EFFECTIVE THAN CURRENT TOOLS IN PROVIDING STAKEHOLDERS, INCLUDING CITIZENS AND LOCAL, STATE, AND TRIBAL GOVERNMENTS, TRANSPARENT, ACCURATE AND COMPREHENSIVE INFORMATION ON EPA?S ACTIVITIES AND POLICIES. IN FY 2013, RESOURCES IN OEAEE WILL SUPPORT MANDATORY IT/TELECOMMUNICATIONS COSTS. ADDITIONALLY, THE OFFICES WILL STRENGTHEN THEIR CUSTOMER SERVICE BY REACHING OUT TO STAKEHOLDERS, INCLUDING FAITH-BASED, NEIGHBORHOOD, MULTILINGUAL, EDUCATIONAL, AND HEALTH GROUPS, SO THAT CITIZENS HAVE A BETTER UNDERSTANDING OF THE ACTIONS THAT EPA IS TAKING TO PROTECT PEOPLE?S HEALTH AND THE ENVIRONMENT. EXTERNAL AFFAIRS WILL USE TRADITIONAL AND SOCIAL MEDIA, THE WEBSITE, AND OTHER INNOVATIVE CHANNELS SUCH AS WEBINARS, VIRTUAL TOWN HALLS, PUBLIC SERVICE ANNOUNCEMENTS, PHOTO PROJECTS, AND VIDEOS TO REACH STUDENTS, COMMUNITIES, AND MULTILINGUAL POPULATIONS TO INFORM THEM OF HEALTH AND ENVIRONMENTAL ISSUES AND RECEIVE FEEDBACK. FINALLY, THE AGENCY WILL RETOOL THE OVERALL EDUCATION EFFORT BY INTEGRATING ENVIRONMENTAL OUTREACH AND TRAINING ACTIVITIES WITHIN CORE EPA ENVIRONMENTAL PROGRAMS, THUS BETTER SERVING THE PUBLIC. OEAEE WILL LEAD AND COORDINATE THE AGENCY?S REVAMPED AND STREAMLINED ENVIRONMENTAL OUTREACH AND LITERACY EFFORT. AS THE CENTRAL ADMINISTRATIVE MANAGEMENT COMPONENT OF THE OFFICE OF THE ADMINISTRATOR (AO), THE OFFICE OF EXECUTIVE SERVICES (OES) PROVIDES ADVICE, TOOLS, AND ASSISTANCE FOR THE ORGANIZATION?S PROGRAMMATIC OPERATIONS INCLUDING HUMAN RESOURCES MANAGEMENT, BUDGET AND FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT, INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY MANAGEMENT AND SECURITY. IN FY 2013, EXECUTIVE SERVICES WILL MANAGE THE UTILIZATION OF AO?S RESOURCES, TRACK AND DEVELOP PROJECTIONS ASSOCIATED WITH PAYROLL UTILIZATION TO ENSURE SOUND FISCAL MANAGEMENT, AND ACHIEVE COST SAVINGS WHEREVER POSSIBLE. OES ALSO WILL ASSIST OTHER ORGANIZATIONS BY CREATING COST-EFFECTIVE INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY SOLUTIONS (I.E., DATABASE TOOLS AND SYSTEMS), ASSESS RESOURCE NEEDS, PREPARE ORGANIZATIONAL MATERIALS, OVERSEE THE OFFICE?S IT AND TELECOMMUNICATIONS RESOURCES, AND PROCESS AND REPORT ON PERSONNEL ACTIONS. THE OFFICE OF THE EXECUTIVE SECRETARIAT (OEX) SERVES AS THE CORRESPONDENCE, RECORDS MANAGEMENT AND FREEDOM OF INFORMATION ACT (FOIA) HUB OF THE OFFICE OF THE ADMINISTRATOR. OEX MANAGES EXECUTIVE CORRESPONDENCE, OVERSEES THE FOIA PROCESS, MAINTAINS THE ADMINISTRATOR?S AND DEPUTY ADMINISTRATOR?S RECORDS, ENSURES THAT OA MEETS ITS RECORDS MANAGEMENT RESPONSIBILITIES AND MANAGES THE CORRESPONDENCE MANAGEMENT SYSTEM, A MAJOR AGENCY INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY APPLICATION. IN FY 2013, RESOURCES IN OEX WILL SUPPORT MANDATORY IT/TELECOMMUNICATIONS COSTS. IN FY 2013, OEX WILL ASSIST STAFF, NATIONAL-PROGRAM OFFICES AND REGIONAL OFFICES IN IMPLEMENTING PAPERLESS TECHNOLOGIES FOR CORRESPONDENCE, RECORDS MANAGEMENT AND FOIA PROCESSING, ENSURING GREATER EFFICIENCY, IMPROVED ACCOUNTABILITY AND REDUCED COST, SUCH AS PHYSICAL RECORDS STORAGE AT THE FEDERAL RECORDS CENTER. OEX ANTICIPATES RESULTS CONSISTENT WITH THOSE DETAILED IN ITS FY 2011 FEDERAL MANAGERS? FINANCIAL INTEGRITY ACT LETTER, INCLUDING SIGNIFICANT REDUCTIONS IN INITIAL CORRESPONDENCE PROCESSING TIMES, LOWER COSTS TO REQUESTERS ACCESSING RECORDS THROUGH THE FOIA AND LOWER STORAGE COSTS FOR PAPER RECORDS STORED OFFSITE.
Definition: THE CONGRESSIONAL, INTERGOVERNMENTAL AND EXTERNAL RELATIONS PROGRAM PROVIDES VITAL EXECUTIVE AND LOGISTICAL SUPPORT FOR EPA?S ADMINISTRATOR. IN ADDITION TO THE ADMINISTRATOR?S IMMEDIATE OFFICE (IO), RESOURCES IN THIS PROGRAM SUPPORT FIVE ADDITIONAL HEADQUARTERS OFFICES THAT CONTRIBUTE TO THE AGENCY?S ABILITY TO MEET ITS COMMITMENTS AND TO PROTECT HUMAN HEALTH AND THE ENVIRONMENT, INCLUDING THE OFFICE OF CONGRESSIONAL AND INTERGOVERNMENTAL RELATIONS (OCIR), A PORTION OF THE OFFICE OF EXTERNAL AFFAIRS AND ENVIRONMENTAL EDUCATION (OEAEE), THE OFFICE OF FEDERAL ADVISORY COMMITTEE MANAGEMENT AND OUTREACH (OFACMO), THE OFFICE OF EXECUTIVE SERVICES (OES), AND THE OFFICE OF THE EXECUTIVE SECRETARIAT (OEX). FUNDING IN THIS PROGRAM ALSO SUPPORTS VITAL NEEDS IN EPA?S 10 REGIONAL ADMINISTRATOR?S OFFICES ACROSS THE COUNTRY. THE ACTIVITIES CONDUCTED BY THESE HEADQUARTERS AND REGIONAL OFFICES ARE THE CRITICAL LINK TO THE AGENCY?S ENGAGEMENT WITH OUTSIDE ENTITIES INCLUDING CONGRESS, STATE AND LOCAL GOVERNMENTS, NONGOVERNMENTAL ORGANIZATIONS, NATIONAL AND COMMUNITY ASSOCIATIONS, AND THE PUBLIC. WITHIN THIS PROGRAM, FUNCTIONS INCLUDE BUT ARE NOT LIMITED TO: RESPONDING TO CONGRESSIONAL REQUESTS FOR INFORMATION; PROVIDING WRITTEN AND ORAL TESTIMONY BRIEFINGS, AND BRIEFING MATERIALS; AS WELL AS OUTREACH AND COORDINATION TO STATE AND LOCAL GOVERNMENTS; MAINTAINING PUBLIC RELATIONS AND COMMUNICATION WITH THE PRESS; AND THE MANAGEMENT OF EPA?S FEDERAL ADVISORY COMMITTEE ACT (FACA) PROCESS. THIS PROGRAM ALSO INCLUDES FUNCTIONS THAT SUPPORT THE ADMINISTRATIVE MANAGEMENT SERVICES INVOLVING CORRESPONDENCE CONTROL AND RECORDS MANAGEMENT SYSTEMS; HUMAN RESOURCE MANAGEMENT, BUDGET FORMULATION AND EXECUTION AND INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY SERVICES.

Name: ENVIRONMENTAL EDUCATION
Title: E9 - ENVIRONMENTAL EDUCATION
Laws: NATIONAL ENVIRONMENTAL EDUCATION ACT (PL 101-619); SECTION 103 OF THE CLEAN AIR ACT; SECTION 104 OF THE CLEAN WATER ACT; SECTION 8001 OF THE SOLID WASTE DISPOSAL ACT; SECTION 1442 OF THE SAFE DRINKING WATER ACT; SECTION 10 OF THE TOXIC SUBSTANCES CONTROL ACT; SECTION 20 OF THE FEDERAL INSECTICIDE, FUNGICIDE, AND RODENTICIDE ACT.
Narrative: NO NEW ACTIVITIES OR FUNDING IS PLANNED FOR THIS PROGRAM. THE AGENCY HAS PROPOSED TO ELIMINATE ITS ENVIRONMENTAL EDUCATION PROGRAM IN ORDER TO FOCUS OUR LIMITED RESOURCES ON FURTHER INTEGRATING ENVIRONMENTAL EDUCATION ACTIVITIES INTO EXISTING ENVIRONMENTAL PROGRAMS UNDER A STREAMLINED APPROACH. IN FY 2012, EPA ESTABLISHED THE INTRA-AGENCY ENVIRONMENTAL EDUCATION WORKGROUP TO INCORPORATE ENVIRONMENTAL LITERACY AND STEWARDSHIP ACTIVITIES ACROSS ALL EPA PROGRAMS. BY ALIGNING ENVIRONMENTAL EDUCATION AND OUTREACH ACTIVITIES WITH THE APPROPRIATE NATIONAL PROGRAMS, EPA IS IMPROVING THE ACCOUNTABILITY AND OUTCOMES OF THESE ACTIVITIES. EPA WILL WORK TO BETTER LEVERAGE ITS RESOURCES FOR ENVIRONMENTAL OUTREACH ACTIVITIES, THUS BETTER SERVING THE PUBLIC WHILE PROMOTING ENVIRONMENTAL LITERACY. THE AGENCY ALSO WILL ENHANCE EFFORTS TO DEVELOP ADDITIONAL PUBLIC-PRIVATE PARTNERSHIP TO HELP SUPPORT ENVIRONMENTAL EDUCATION STAKEHOLDERS.
Definition: THIS PROGRAM HAS ENSURED THAT ENVIRONMENTAL EDUCATION, USING A SCIENCE-BASED APPROACH AND EFFECTIVE EDUCATION PRACTICES, HAS BEEN USED AS A TOOL TO PROMOTE THE PROTECTION OF HUMAN HEALTH AND THE ENVIRONMENT, AND HAS ENCOURAGED STUDENT ACADEMIC ACHIEVEMENT. ENVIRONMENTAL EDUCATION HAS TAUGHT THE PUBLIC ABOUT CHOICES AND ENVIRONMENTAL STEWARDSHIP TO PRODUCE THE NEXT GENERATION OF ENVIRONMENTALLY LITERATE CITIZENS AND STEWARDS, AND HAS GENERATED SUPPORT FOR ENVIRONMENTAL POLICY. THE NATIONAL ENVIRONMENTAL EDUCATION ACT HAS PROVIDED A FOUNDATION FOR THE ACTIVITIES THAT THE AGENCY HAS CONDUCTED UNDER THIS PROGRAM PROJECT.

Name: EXCHANGE NETWORK
Title: F1 - EXCHANGE NETWORK
Laws: FEDERAL ADVISORY COMMITTEE ACT (FACA), 42 U.S.C. 553 ET SEQ. AND GOVERNMENT INFORMATION SECURITY ACT (GISRA), 40 U.S.C. 1401 ET SEQ. ? SECTIONS 3531, 3532, 3533, 3534, 3535 AND 3536 AND COMPREHENSIVE ENVIRONMENTAL RESPONSE, COMPENSATION AND LIABILITY ACT (CERCLA), 42 U.S.C. 9606 ET SEQ. ? SECTIONS 101-128, 301-312 AND 401-405 AND CLEAN AIR ACT (CAA) AMENDMENTS, 42 U.S.C. 7401 ET SEQ. ? SECTIONS 102, 103, 104 AND 108 AND CLEAN WATER ACT (CWA), 33 U.S.C. 1314 ET SEQ. ? SECTIONS 101, 102, 103, 104, 105, 107, AND 109 AND TOXIC SUBSTANCES CONTROL ACT (TSCA), 15 U.S.C. 2611 ET SEQ. ?
